I bet it belongs over here somewhere. Title: How to Cure Braces Pain Introduction: Orthodontic treatment with braces can cause discomfort or pain at times. This is completely normal as your teeth are being moved into their correct positions gradually. However, there are several ways you can alleviate this pain without having to visit your orthodontist every time. Here's our comprehensive guide on how to cure braces pain. Step 1: Understand the Source of Discomfort The primary source of braces-related pain comes from the pressure applied by the brackets and wires against your teeth and gums. As they slowly move your teeth into position, minor inflammation occurs causing temporary soreness or irritation. Recognizing this fact will help you better manage the associated discomfort. Key Tip: If severe toothache persists along with fever, swelling, or pus around the affected area, seek immediate dental care as these could be signs of infection requiring professional attention. Step 2: Adjust Your Diet Avoid hard, crunchy, sticky foods that require excessive chewing which may exacerbate the pain. Opt instead for softer options like yogurt, soup, mashed potatoes, scrambled eggs, cooked cereals, etc., especially after an adjustment appointment. Guideline: Maintain good oral hygiene even when eating soft food items; sugary substances might get trapped in between braces and lead to decay. Step 3: Rinse With Salt Water Rinsing your mouth thoroughly with warm salt water (a teaspoon of salt dissolved in half a cup of warm water) multiple times a day reduces inflammation and eases discomfort caused by new braces or adjustments. Explanation: The sodium chloride present in saltwater has anti-inflammatory properties helping reduce swelling while creating a more alkaline environment discouraging bacterial growth. Step 4: Over-the-Counter Medication Non-prescription medicines such as ibuprofen (Advil, Motrin), acetaminophen (Tylenol), or naproxen (Aleve) can provide significant relief if taken according to package directions. Precaution: Always consult your healthcare provider before starting any new medication regimen, particularly if you have pre-existing health conditions or allergies. Step 5: Use Orthodontic Relief Wax Applying small amounts of orthodontic wax over sharp edges or protruding parts of your braces provides instant comfort by forming a barrier between the appliance and delicate tissues inside your mouth. Instructions: Roll a pea-sized amount of wax between fingers till smooth, dry bracket/wire briefly using alcohol pad, then place the wax firmly covering the problematic area. Replace daily or whenever necessary. Step 6: Cold Compress Application Applying an ice pack wrapped in cloth externally near the jaw where it hurts intermittently (20 minutes on followed by 20 minutes off) numbs nerve endings reducing perceived pain. Science Behind It: Cold temperatures constrict blood vessels reducing blood flow and thus decreasing sensation of pain. Conclusion: While experiencing some level of discomfort during orthodontic treatment is inevitable, armed with these strategies, managing brace-related pain becomes much easier. Remember, patience and consistency yield great results! I don't mind if you get me. You don't see a picture of a pig either. Most aircraft use engine power. Examples are aeroplanes, helicopter, and airships. Gliders and balloons use no power. A few aircraft use muscle power. " My little baby is gone. Title: How to Change Your Name on Zoom Video Communication Introduction: Zoom is a popular video communication platform that allows users to host or join virtual meetings with ease. One customizable feature within Zoom is the ability to change your name during a meeting or before entering one. This can be helpful when you want to maintain privacy, distinguish yourself from others who share your same name, or simply express your individuality. Here's a comprehensive guide on changing your name on Zoom Video. Part 1: Changing Your Name Before Joining a Meeting Step 1: Open the Zoom Application Start by opening the Zoom application on your device (Windows, Mac, Linux, iOS, or Android). If you do not have the app installed yet, download it first from the official website and install it accordingly. Key Tip: Make sure your internet connection is stable to ensure seamless navigation through the process. Step 2: Click on 'Join a Meeting' On the main screen, click on the option 'Join a Meeting.' You will find this button located at the top-right corner of the window if you are using Windows, macOS, or Linux; otherwise, look for it at the bottom part of the interface for mobile devices. Step 3: Enter the Meeting ID You will now see an input box asking for the Meeting ID or Personal Link Name. Type in the required details provided by the meeting organizer and then press 'Join'. Step 4: Edit Display Name Before joining the actual meeting, there will be another prompt where you can configure audio and video settings as well as rename yourself under the 'Display Name' field. Fill in the new name you wish to appear as during the meeting. Guideline: Avoid using offensive language or impersonating someone else while renaming yourself. It may violate Zoom's terms of service and cause disruptions within the meeting environment. Step 5: Confirm Changes and Join Meeting After filling in the preferred display name, confirm the changes by clicking on 'Join' again. Now, you should successfully enter the meeting with your chosen moniker visible to all participants. Part 2: Renaming Yourself During an Active Meeting Step 6: Locate Participants List During an ongoing Zoom session, locate the participants list either at the right sidebar (desktop) or bottom part (mobile) of the screen. Step 7: Find Your Current Name Within the participant roster, search for your current displayed name. Once found, hover over it until more options become available. Step 8: Select Rename Option Click on the 'Rename' option that appears next to your name after hovering. A pop-up dialog box requesting a new display name will show up. Step 9: Input New Desired Name In the text entry space provided, type in the new name you would like to adopt for the rest of the meeting. Remember to adhere to community standards and avoid any misleading or inappropriate names. Step 10: Confirm Name Change To finalize the name modification, hit the 'OK' button. All attendees present in the meeting room will now notice your updated identifier above your video feed. Conclusion: Changing your name on Zoom Video Communication provides flexibility and personalization tailored to user preferences. By following these ten steps, you can effectively modify your display name both prior to participating in a call or even midway through an active session. Always keep in mind appropriate naming practices and respectful behavior towards other members of the virtual gathering. Take the pop out of your mouth when you talk. Master Math with a Rounding Race This is a simple, fast game that you and your child can make together to help him master rounding with decimals. Find an opponent for your child, or use the clock. Either way, be prepared for a loud, exciting and competitive game! What You Need: - 6-7 sheets of paper or 3x5 note cards - 2 black markers What You Do: - Cut the paper into approximately 50 cards, or use 25 3x5 note cards, cut in half. - On each card, write dollar amounts in black marker. Use amounts from a penny, all the way up to $999.99. For example: - Briefly review the process of rounding to the nearest dollar. Show your child how $38.14 rounds to $38.00. Here's how: the "ones" place comes just before the decimal, circle it (the 8) and look at the number to its right - 1 - which is less than 5. Therefore, the 8 stays the same and the 1 gets knocked down to 0. You have successfully rounded to $38.00. Have your child practice rounding with a few cards. - Now, it's time to play! Have your fourth-grader challenge a sibling or friend. Both players sit next to each other facing you. Lay down a dollar card in front of the players. The first player to correctly round the number to the nearest dollar wins the card. Set a timer and play for 3 minutes. The player with the most cards at the end of 3 minutes wins the round. The winner of the game is the player with the best 3 out of 5 rounds. - If no one is available to "race" your child, challenge her to play against the clock. Set the timer for 3 minutes. Show her dollar cards one at a time and have her call out the rounded amount. See how many cards she can get in 3 minutes. - Challenge her to beat her record and reduce her time to 2 minutes, then 1 minute. It's always more fun if you supply "prizes" for the winner (and a consolation prize for the runner-up). But whatever way you crack it, this game will give kids another reward-the ability to approach rounding without fear. Such at least had been the intimated attitude of Mrs. Stringham, the elder of the companions, who had her own view of the impatiences of the younger, to which, however, she offered an opposition but of the most circuitous. She moved, the admirable Mrs. Stringham, in a fine cloud of observation and suspicion; she was in the position, as she believed, of knowing much more about Milly Theale than Milly herself knew, and yet of having to darken her knowledge as well as make it active. The woman in the world least formed by nature, as she was quite aware, for duplicities and labyrinths, she found herself dedicated to personal subtlety by a new set of circumstances, above all by a new personal relation; had now in fact to recognise that an education in the occult--she could scarce say what to call it--had begun for her the day she left New York with Mildred. She had come on from Boston for that purpose; had seen little of the girl--or rather had seen her but briefly, for Mrs. Stringham, when she saw anything at all, saw much, saw everything--before accepting her proposal; and had accordingly placed herself, by her act, in a boat that she more and more estimated as, humanly speaking, of the biggest, though likewise, no doubt, in many ways, by reason of its size, of the safest. In Boston, the winter before, the young lady in whom we are interested had, on the spot, deeply, yet almost tacitly, appealed to her, dropped into her mind the shy conceit of some assistance, some devotion to render. I fear that unmarried ladies of thirty-five will declare that there can be no probability of so absurd a project being carried through; that young women on their knees before their lovers are sure to get kissed, and that they would not put themselves in such a position did they not expect it; that Eleanor is going to Bold only because circumstances prevent Bold from coming to her; that she is certainly a little fool, or a little schemer, but that in all probability she is thinking a good deal more about herself than her father. Dear ladies, you are right as to your appreciation of the circumstances, but very wrong as to Miss Harding's character. Miss Harding was much younger than you are, and could not, therefore, know, as you may do, to what dangers such an encounter might expose her. She may get kissed; I think it very probable that she will; but I give my solemn word and positive assurance, that the remotest idea of such a catastrophe never occurred to her as she made the great resolve now alluded to. Operations with the Golden Beads - As for addition - To understand the concept of multiplication as equal addition - To learn the vocabulary: multiplication, multiplicand, multiplier, and product - (Note: Because multiplication is the addition of equal quantities, it may be introduced at any time after children have learned addition. Since these problems do not include exchanging, the product of any heirarchy must not be greater than 9, and the largest product possible is 9999.) - 5 years onwards - A group exercise for a few children. The material is arranged as for addition. One child is responsible for the golden beads material. One child is in charge of the large number cards. One child oversees the small number cards. - The teacher thinks of a problem, e.g. 2322 x 3. The teacher makes 2322, three times in small number cards and puts them on each of three trays. She gives a tray to each of three children. the children to read the number on their tray in turn. When they have done so, she stresses the fact that they each have the same numeral. "You each have the same numeral. You each have 2322. Will you all go to the bank and collect that amount in - The children collect 2322 each and come back to the teacher. - The teacher takes each tray in turn. She takes the quantities off each tray, saying, "You have brought 2322." She arranges the quantities on the mat one below the other, and takes the small number cards and places them under each other at the top of the mat. The teacher says, indicating the golden beads, "We have 2322 - We will add them together and see how much we have altogether. She adds the hierarchies. She asks a child to count the result beginning with the units. After each hierarchy has been counted, the corresponding amount in large number cards is placed beside it. - The teacher superimposes the number cards and places them under the small ones at the top of the table. She recaps, "We had 2322 three times. We added them together and got 6966." Other problems can be worked in this way. - (Note: In multiplication small equal quantities are added together to make a larger quantity. Small number cards are used for the quantities and large number cards for the product to help give this impression.) And I put power in my bike. You want to try it on your arm? 'Fused quartz' is glass made of silica in non-crystalline form. It does not contain other ingredients that are added to other glass to lower the melting point. Fused silica has high working and melting temperatures. For some purposes, fused quartz is better than other types of glass due to its purity. The glass is used in a number of high-tech products. However, it does have a hard, pointy beak. Beaks come in all shapes and sizes. The shape of a bird's beak is a clue to what it eats and how it gathers its food. Since scientists theorize that birds do not have a good sense of smell or taste, they feel that birds generally find food by its color and shape. - identify beaks belonging to birds of prey and non-birds of prey - determine the kinds of food a bird with a certain type of beak might eat Take the time to make sure your students understand: - the importance of the shapes of beaks - how beaks are used based on their shape - the differences between beaks belonging to birds of prey and non-birds Allow students time to work through the Curvy Beaks activity sheet(s). Emergent Literacy Project: Ehhh? I can't Materials: Primary paper and pencil, sentence strip with "Everybody saw Eddie and the Eskimo enter the elevator on the elephant", Wet Legs (Innovative Kids), worksheet with pictures of a cat, elephant, bed, house, desk, dress, mouse, and eggs, red crayons. 1) Tell students: Have you ever heard someone say /e/ when they cannot hear something? A person might say/e/, I can't hear you! We can hear the /e/ sound in many words. Let's try the word red. Re-e-e- See there is the /e/ sound. 3) Now, say this tongue twister after I say it, " Everybody saw Eddie and the Eskimo enter the elevator on the elephant." Now lets all say it together. Now I will take off the /e/ of each word. /E/verybody saw /E/ddie and the /E/skimo enter the /e/lavator on the /e/lephant. Now let's all take off the /e/ and say it again. 4) Now let's write the letter /e/. Start with your pencil in the center of the space below the fence, go toward the door, up to touch the fence, around and up. Now I will come around and look at your e. If I say your e looks good, please write a whole row of e's. 5) Now I say some words. Hear are the words(say words) pet, sad, wet, run, some, leg, apple, dead. Tell me which words you hear the /e/ sound in. Hold up your hand by your hear if you hear the /e/ sound. Do not do anything if you don't heart the /e/ sound 6) Read "Wet Legs" and talk about the story. Read story again and ask the students to raise their hands when they hear the /e/ sound. Give students adequate time to hear the words. Then write the /e/ words on the board that the children tell you they hear. 7) For assessment, give the students a picture page and have them circle each picture with the /e/ sound with a red crayon. The planet Uranus has a system made of 13 rings which is far fewer than the rings of Saturn but more than those around Jupiter and Neptune. The rings of Uranus were discovered in 1977. More than 200 years ago, William Herschel also reported observing rings, but modern astronomers do not believe that he saw them, because they are very dark and faint. Two additional rings were discovered in 1986 in images taken by Voyager 2, and two outer rings were found in 2003-2005 by the Hubble Space Telescope. Title: How to Buy a Gift for a Kindergarten Graduate Introduction: Graduating kindergarten is a significant milestone in a child's life. It marks their transition from early childhood education to primary school, where they will learn more complex subjects and gain new skills. As a parent, relative, or friend, you may want to celebrate this achievement by buying a gift that supports their growth and development. This article outlines steps and considerations when purchasing a gift for a kindergarten graduate. Step 1: Determine Your Budget Before looking at potential gifts, decide on your budget. A clear financial plan ensures you don't overspend while still finding something meaningful and memorable. Key tip: While setting a budget, remember that thoughtful and educational gifts do not have to be expensive. Step 2: Consider Their Interests Consider what activities or topics the child enjoys most. Are they passionate about art, science, sports, or animals? Knowing their interests can help guide you toward a gift tailored specifically to them. Guideline: If unsure, ask someone close to the child (parent, teacher) for suggestions. Step 3: Encourage Learning Choose a gift that promotes learning and skill-building appropriate for their age group. At kindergarten level, these could include puzzles, building blocks, board games, storybooks, or simple craft kits. By doing so, you support their continued intellectual development during summer break and prepare them for first grade. Step 4: Opt for Quality Products Select high-quality items built to last. Though children often outgrow toys quickly, durable products provide longer usage time and better value. Moreover, quality materials reduce safety risks associated with cheaply made toys prone to breaking apart easily. Step 5: Personalize When Possible Personalized gifts add sentimental value and make the recipient feel special. Examples include custom-made books featuring the child as the main character, name-engraved jewelry, monogrammed backpacks, etc. Remember, personalization doesn't necessarily mean higher cost; sometimes, it just requires extra effort or creativity. Step 6: Think Beyond Traditional Toys Gifts needn't always be traditional toys. Musical instruments, gardening tools, binoculars, or even cooking utensils can foster curiosity and teach real-world skills. Always ensure any non-toy item chosen is safe and suitable for young children. Step 7: Avoid Potentially Harmful Gifts Steer clear of gifts containing small parts or toxic substances unsuitable for kids under three years old due to choking hazards. Also, avoid electronic devices meant for older kids, which might hinder social interaction and physical activity levels. Step 8: Shop Responsibly When shopping online, read reviews carefully before making a purchase. Look for reputable sellers who prioritize customer satisfaction and product safety. For local purchases, visit trusted stores known for carrying reliable brands and following strict quality control measures. Step 9: Present the Gift Appropriately Wrap the present nicely using eco-friendly wrapping paper if possible. Adding a handwritten note expressing congratulations and well wishes adds a personal touch. During presentation, emphasize the importance of their accomplishment and encourage future academic successes. Conclusion: Buying a gift for a kindergarten graduate involves careful planning, understanding of the child's preferences, and consideration of both fun and educational aspects. Following these steps ensures selecting an age-appropriate, engaging, and valuable present that contributes positively towards the child's ongoing development. Once there was a naughty little girl. She was very naughty and always misbehaving. One day she do something very naughty. She took her daddy s tie and put it in the dirt. It began to rot away in the soil. In a criminal trial, the prosecution lawyer will often bring in the victim of the crime to say what happened, and anyone else who can prove the accused person did the crime. The defense lawyer will nearly always call up the accused person to explain why they are not guilty, and bring in other witnesses who can prove the accused person is not guilty. Each time a lawyer brings in a witness, the other lawyer will "cross examine" the witness - they will ask them questions to make sure they are telling the truth. The Cold War was the tense relationship between the United States (and its allies), and the Soviet Union (the USSR and its allies) between the end of World War II and the fall of the Soviet Union. It is called the "Cold" War because the US and the USSR never actually fought each other directly. Instead, they opposed each other in conflicts known as proxy wars, where each country chose a side to support. Yet Tarzan did not hesitate more than had Teeka, but leaped upon Histah with all the speed and impetuosity that he would have shown had he been springing upon Bara, the deer, to make a kill for food. Thus beset the snake writhed and twisted horribly; but not for an instant did it loose its hold upon any of its intended victims, for it had included the ape-man in its cold embrace the minute that he had fallen upon it. [pointing to a picture in the book] Making sharp, clean lines on metal requires a sharp, clean point. A scriber is made from hardened and tempered tool steel. To mark a line with a scriber, draw it towards you, and keep it angled in the direction it's going to travel so it doesn't dig into the surface being marked. Dividers are used to mark up circles and arcs. They are held at an exact angle by an adjusting nut. Because of the sharp points on their legs, they also give an accurate way to transfer measurements from say, a steel rule, across to the work. Pin point the lines on the rule and make sure the circle has exactly the right radius. One way to make sure this leg of the dividers stays put on the surface is to make a small dent with the prick punch, before starting to draw. On some hard or shiny surfaces, scribed marks can be hard to see, so marking-dye helps. A thin coat is applied to the area being marked. It dries very quickly. Then the path cut by the point of the scriber is easy to see. Engineer's blue is similar to marking dye. It comes in tubes and it's a bit like blue butter. Sometimes in checking a fit, or testing for flatness, it's difficult to see the area being worked on. By smearing a small amount of engineer's blue on a surface plate it can be used to indicate if the surface to be tested is flat.The blue marks on the housing indicate the high areas which must be removed to obtain a flat surface. A flat surface would be blue over the whole surface. But you have the same as this one. And uh she mostly nurses, but she gets a bottle at nighttime and then um. The TV is on in the background] Title: How to Delete Your Zillow Account: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Zillow is an online real estate marketplace that provides information about homes for sale, rentals, and mortgage rates. If you no longer wish to use their services or want to protect your personal data, deleting your account might be the best option. This tutorial will guide you through the process of permanently closing your Zillow account with clear instructions and helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Log into Your Zillow Account To begin, visit <https://www.zillow.com/> and click 'Sign In' at the top right corner of the page. Enter your email address and password associated with your Zillow account. It's essential to log in first so that you can access your account settings and delete it. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Make sure you have access to the email address linked to your Zillow account since you may need to verify your identity before proceeding. - Double-check if any devices are currently logged into your Zillow account; sign out from all other sessions to ensure maximum security. Step 2: Access Your Account Settings Once logged in, hover over your profile picture (or default avatar) located at the upper right-hand side of the screen. From the dropdown menu, select 'Account Profile.' You will now see various options related to managing your Zillow account. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Familiarize yourself with different sections within your account settings as they contain valuable features like Privacy Controls, Notifications Preferences, etc. Step 3: Navigate to the 'Close My Account' Option Scroll down until you find the section labeled 'Need Help?' Underneath this heading, there should be a link saying 'Close My Account.' Click on this link to initiate the account deletion process. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Be aware that after confirming the closure request, you won't be able to recover your account or its contents. Therefore, make sure you've saved any necessary information before continuing. Step 4: Verify Your Identity You will be prompted to enter your password again to verify your identity. Type in your current password and hit 'Continue.' By doing so, Zillow ensures that only authorized users can close accounts. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Use strong and unique passwords across platforms to maintain good cybersecurity habits. Consider using a reputable password manager tool to help generate secure passwords and store them safely. Step 5: Provide Feedback (Optional) Before finalizing the deletion, Zillow gives you the opportunity to share feedback regarding why you're leaving. Although optional, providing honest input could potentially contribute to improving their service offerings. After completing this step or deciding to skip it, click 'Continue.' Key Tips & Guidelines: - Constructively expressing your concerns might lead to positive changes in Zillow's user experience. However, avoid sharing sensitive personal details when submitting feedback. Step 6: Confirm Account Deletion Finally, review the confirmation message displayed by Zillow, which summarizes what will happen once your account has been deleted. To complete the process, click on the button stating 'Yes, Close My Account.' Congratulations! You have successfully closed your Zillow account. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Keep in mind that it takes some time for Zillow to fully remove your account due to system processing delays. During this period, you may still receive automated emails or app notifications. Ignore these and wait patiently while your account gets wiped off their servers completely. Title: Dump a Boyfriend Without Hurting His Feelings - A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Breakups are never easy, but sometimes they become necessary for personal growth or due to compatibility issues. This guide aims at helping you end a relationship with your boyfriend gently and respectfully while minimizing emotional harm. By following these steps, you'll be able to communicate your feelings effectively without causing unnecessary distress. Step 1: Reflect On Your Feelings Before taking any action, introspect deeply about your emotions and thoughts. Are you sure that ending the relationship is what you want? Have all attempts at resolving conflicts failed? If yes, then proceed to the next step. Key Tip: Be certain about your decision before initiating the breakup conversation as changing your mind later might confuse him further and cause more pain. Step 2: Choose The Right Time And Place Plan when and where you would like to have this discussion. It should ideally occur in a private setting where both of you feel comfortable expressing yourselves freely. Avoid public places or times when he could potentially be stressed or upset already (e.g., just before an important work meeting). Guideline: Make sure there are no distractions so you can focus solely on the conversation. Turn off electronic devices and ensure you have enough time set aside to discuss everything fully. Step 3: Prepare What You Want To Say Think carefully about why you wish to end things. Consider his perspective too; how will he perceive your reasons? Writing down your points can help structure your thoughts coherently. Remember to stay focused on your feelings rather than blaming or criticizing him. Key Tip: Use "I" statements instead of "you" statements to avoid sounding accusatory. For example, say "I feel unheard in our conversations," instead of "You never listen to me." Step 4: Initiate The Conversation Gently Start by mentioning something positive about your time together to soften the blow. Then calmly explain that you need to talk about something serious. Maintain eye contact and speak softly to show empathy. Guideline: Beginning on a positive note sets a compassionate tone for the difficult conversation ahead. Step 5: Clearly State Your Intention Express that you believe it's best for both of you to part ways amicably. Explain your reasons honestly, using the prepared script from Step 3. However, do so kindly and tactfully, avoiding harsh language or criticism. Key Tip: Avoid getting defensive if he protests or gets emotional. Listen attentively and validate his feelings by acknowledging them. Step 6: Offer Support During The Transition Period Show understanding towards his feelings of hurt and disappointment. Offer support during the transition phase by suggesting counseling, giving space, or referring to mutual friends who can provide comfort. Guideline: Showing kindness and consideration even after breaking up shows maturity and respect, which can ease the healing process. Step 7: Establish Boundaries Post-Breakup Decide upon boundaries regarding communication and interaction post-breakup. Whether you prefer minimal contact or maintaining friendship, make sure to convey this clearly yet sensitively. Key Tip: Stick to the agreed terms to prevent confusion and additional heartache. Conclusion: Ending a relationship gracefully requires patience, empathy, and clear communication. While it may not entirely eliminate the pain associated with breakups, employing these strategies can significantly reduce the risk of hurting your boyfriend's feelings. Aluminium is one of the primary factors that reduce the growth of plants on acidic soils. In acid soils the concentration of toxic Al cations increases and disturbs the growth and function of the root. It is generally harmless to plant growth in pH-neutral soils. Wheat has developed a tolerance to aluminium. It releases organic compounds that bind to harmful aluminium cations. Sorghum is thought to have the same method of tolerating aluminium. In species with annual rings, these rings can be counted to find the age of the tree. This way, wood taken from trees in the past can be dated, because the patterns of ring thickness are very distinctive. This is dendrochronology. For example: keys, blocks, scissors, combs, jar lids (at least 5 items per Take all your materials outside to a flat space that receives direct sunlight and where you will be able to leave your experiment set up for a few hours. Give a piece of construction paper to each child. Let each child chose five objects, and have them arrange them on the paper with the flat side down. Leave these in the direct sun for several hours, until the sun has bleached the exposed areas of the paper. Then let the children take their objects off the paper, noting the patterns produced by the sun. your own Sand Garden Make your own Sand Garden. Many fruit seeds and vegetable cuttings will sprout easily indoors. whole ear of corn, potato "eyes" (any kind of potato), carrot tops, peas, beans, seeds from various fruit - peach, plum, Place fruit seeds or vegie cuttings in a shallow pan lined with paper towels and enough water to just cover the seed or vegetable cuttings. Place pan in a warm, bright area and watch and record the changes over the next few days/weeks. In egg cartons with planting soil - grass seed, mustard seed, watercress or herbs. Try using a celery stalk with leaves or white carnations!! A wonderful visual experience of color changes. Make red, yellow and blue ice cubes using food coloring and water. Place one red and yellow; one red and blue; and one yellow and blue, and one of each color in separate ziploc bags. Place them in the science area and as the colored ice cubes melt they will create new colors. The kids will be busy going back and forth to check on what is happening! One night amongst the rest, she being growen almost hopelesse, of ever seeing him againe, having a long while wept and greevously lamented; her senses and faculties utterly spent and tired, that she could not utter any more complaints, she fell into a trance or sleepe; and dreamed, that the ghost of _Lorenzo_ appeared unto her, in torne and unbefitting garments, his lookes pale, meager, and staring: and (as she thought) thus spake to her. Creating a machine snare roll in Propellerhead's Reason 4 is an excellent way to add energy and excitement to your music productions. A snare roll is a rapid succession of snare drum hits that creates a powerful rhythmic pattern. By using Reason 4's devices and sequencing tools, you can create professional-sounding snare rolls with ease. This step-by-step guide will walk you through the process of creating a machine snare roll in Reason 4. **Step 1: Create a new song** 1. Launch Reason 4. 2. Click 'File' > 'New Song'. Choose a suitable tempo (e.g., 128 BPM) and signature (e.g., 4/4). Name your song appropriately and click 'Save'. **Step 2: Add Dr. REX Loop Player** The Dr. REX Loop Player is a versatile device capable of playing back sampled loops, including percussive sounds like snares. We'll use this device as our primary sound source for the snare roll. 1. Go to the 'Create' menu or click the '+' button at the bottom left corner of the rack area. 2. Select 'Dr. REX Loop Player', then click anywhere in the rack area to place it. ![Adding Devices](https://wikihowcom.cbsistatic.com/thumbnail/ Mary Wollstonecraft (27 April 1759 - 10 September 1797) was a British writer. She was born in Spitalfields, a daughter of a rich farmer who inherited his fortune. Her father was known because he was sometimes violent towards her, her four siblings, and their mother when his farms failed. Mary Wollstonecraft was the second oldest child in her family. She was the oldest female child. She left home at the age of nineteen to work and become independent. Hello young scholars! Today, let's talk about a fun concept in probability theory called "stochastic processes." You may already know about probabilities and random events, like rolling a dice or flipping a coin. Now imagine these events happening over time - this creates something exciting called a stochastic process! Let's think of it as a story where our main characters are *random variables* who change their behavior based on different moments in time. We could call them Roller (our friend when dealing with dice rolls) and Coiny (your pal for head or tail outcomes). At each point in time, both Roller and Coiny have unique characteristics represented by numbers, which means they're actually just special types of number collections known as random variables! In our example, since we'll focus on situations changing step-by-step, we need to keep things nice and organized. So, we'll make sure to line up all those moments in order like steps on stairs; hence the term *discrete-time*. Imagine climbing up a staircase while playing with your friends Roller and Coiny, observing them carefully at every single step! Now here comes the magical part: even though we observe our pals throughout various stages, we'll say that a Discrete-Time Markov Chain exists if two cool conditions hold true: 1. Whatever happens at the current step depends only on that very moment, not influenced by previous happenings. It's almost like having a short memory! Our friend Roller doesn't remember past die rolls, so no matter what was rolled before, each new roll has equal chances of showing up. 2. The chance of moving into another character's state at the next step must remain constant, making future predictions more manageable. Let's assume Coiny always lands heads-up half of the times, regardless of his earlier landings. So there you go, little scientists! When working with systems composed of elements evolving randomly through distinct phases, understanding Discrete-Time Markov Chains helps predict their behaviors mathematically. And don't forget-you encountered this advanced notion way back in grade school! I developed a mat to help my students finally understand place value. I created a city block called Place Value Town. Reading Road, at the top of the mat, is a one way street from left to right. The street at the bottom of the mat is Placement Parkway, a one way street from right to left. Two cross streets are Comma Cut on the left of the block and Decimal Drive on the right of the block. There are three houses on this block called H, T and O for hundreds, tens and ones. There is also one house on the other side of Comma Cut called TH for thousands. This visual helps my students see that a number is read only one way but the numbers are placed in reverse order. This established the place. The value referred to the number of people who live in the house. For example, 105 means that one person lives in house H, five people live in house O but, just because no one lives in house T doesn't mean the house isn't there. The house on the next block, TH, is there so they can begin to understand higher numbers as well. I gloss over the decimal point by talking about the decimal point in money. This satisfies them as well as prepares them for future math. 'Look 'ere,' began the cabman, in a pained voice. 'Oh, dry up,' said Farnie. 'Want a lift, Gethryn?' Title: How to Correct Poorly Dyed Blonde Hair Introduction: Dyeing your hair blonde can be an exciting change; however, it often comes with its own set of challenges. When done incorrectly, you may end up with brassy tones, uneven color, or damage. This guide will help you correct poorly dyed blonde hair by following these steps carefully. Please remember that if your hair is severely damaged or broken, consider seeking professional assistance from a hairstylist. **Step 1: Assess the Damage** Examine your hair closely to determine the extent of the damage caused by the previous dye job. Look out for signs such as split ends, excessive dryness, frizziness, and overall porosity (how quickly your hair absorbs moisture). If your hair feels straw-like and snaps off easily when stretched, it's likely overprocessed and might need more intensive treatment before proceeding. Key Tip: Be patient with yourself throughout this process. Restoring your hair back to health takes time. **Step 2: Deep Condition Your Hair** To begin repairing the damage, deep condition your hair once a week using a high-quality moisturizing mask. Leave it on for at least 30 minutes to allow maximum absorption. Deep conditioning helps restore hydration, elasticity, and manageability to your hair. Guideline: Avoid applying heat during this period. Heat further damages already weakened strands. **Step 3: Trim Split Ends** Trimming your hair regularly is crucial to maintaining healthy lengths. Regular trims remove damaged splits before they travel up the shaft causing breakage. A trim every six weeks should suffice but consult with your stylist if unsure. Key Tip: Ask your stylist for a dusting - a technique where only the absolute minimum length is removed. **Step 4: Choose the Right Color Correction Product** Choose a color correction product based on your specific issues. For brassiness, opt for purple shampoo or toner. For orange undertones, choose blue shampoo instead. Always perform a strand test first to check compatibility and results. Guideline: Don't forget to read reviews and understand ingredients before purchasing any product. **Step 5: Apply Color Correction Treatment** Follow the manufacturer's instructions explicitly when applying the chosen product. Generally, you want to evenly distribute the product throughout damp hair, focusing particularly on problem areas. Allow it to sit for the recommended duration before rinsing thoroughly. Key Tip: Never leave color correction treatments on longer than instructed as they could potentially cause more harm than good. **Step 6: Protect Your Hair** Protect your newly corrected blonde hair by incorporating protective measures into your routine. Use sulfate-free shampoos and conditioners, limit heat styling tools, sleep on silk pillowcases, and avoid chlorinated water. Regular maintenance like monthly touch-ups and quarterly trims are also important. Guideline: Consider getting a professional glossing service at a salon which adds shine and seals the cuticle layer, preventing future discoloration. Conclusion: Remember, correcting poorly dyed blonde hair requires patience and consistency. By following our comprehensive guide, you'll gradually see improvements leading to vibrant, lustrous locks. However, if your hair remains extremely damaged despite efforts, seek expert advice from a certified hairdresser. They'll probably throw her out of the store. Grammar Index : 2 A and B are talking to each other. We use reciprocal pronoun when each of two or more subjects is acting in the same way towards the other. For example : A is talking to B. B is talking to A. So we say : The action is reciprocated. John talks to Mary and Mary talks to John. I give you a present and you give me a present. The dog bites the cat and the cat bites the dog. There are only two reciprocal pronouns and they are both two words. When we use these reciprocal pronouns : There must be two or more people, things or groups involved (so we cannot use reciprocal pronouns with I, you [singular], he/she/it). They must be doing the same thing. The children can help each other in doing the home work. Victor and Laurie were sitting next to each other. They gave all evidence against each other. The two of them sat facing one another. From Reciprocal Pronoun to HOME PAGE What about our guests you didn't offer our guests any. Sample Life Poetry Worksheet Reading Comprehension Worksheets Return to Poetry Theme Unit Build a printable worksheet with the complete story By Brenda B. Covert 1 Today we're going to learn to write poetry that celebrates your life. You are alive today; that makes you a survivor! During times of struggle, whether it be personal problems or perhaps a national attack, you can remember that you are a survivor. You are worth celebrating. 2 I call this poetry, "Life Poetry." It is also called "catalog or list poetry." It's easy to write. There is no rhyme or rhythm involved unless you want to use them! The only rule for "Life Poetry" is that you are honest. 3 To begin, get a clean sheet of paper. You are going to be making short lists under six headings. After you have completed the lists, I'll explain how to turn them into a life poem. 4 Think of the room in your home where you spend most of your time. List four to six nouns that relate to that room, without naming the room! You can include verbs, adjectives, and adverbs. For example, if I were to make a list for my kitchen, I might write this: sink cluttered with dishes, the aroma of peach butter, corn flakes scattered everywhere, fluffy cat looking for a treat, child proudly making a sandwich. Those things are not always there, but they represent that room of my house! Notice that I didn't write complete sentences in my list. Make your first list. Be creative! 5 The focus of the next heading is a little larger. While the first list was about a room, this heading covers your home and yard or play area, the places where you primarily live. Think not only about objects in these places, but also activities and emotions that are common there. Are there any sounds or smells that are familiar to your home and play area? Choose around six thoughts for your list. 6 The third list is about your community and state. By community I mean the country, small town, or big city where you live, local places you have visited, and things like that. Are there any attractions where you live? For instance, if you live in Cincinnati, Ohio, you could include King's Island in your list. This list should include things with which you are familiar; you shouldn't have to research your community. Then move on to your state, which may require research. Without naming your state, list clues that a person could use to guess your home state. What makes your state different from other states? What makes it special? 7 The fourth list is about our country. Without writing the words "America" or "USA," write down some of our most famous landmarks, such as the Statue of Liberty and the Liberty Bell, and your feelings about our country and its people. Think about how we help each other during times of crisis. Remember the courage of our police officers, our firefighters, our military, and even our ordinary citizens who risk their lives to protect us and keep us safe! 8 Now we'll switch gears and focus on your heritage. Think about your ancestors as well as your living relatives. Did they come from other lands? What types of work have they done? Don't list them all, but choose the ones that stand out in your mind. How do you feel about your heritage? This list will show others the path that leads to you. Title: How to Draw Horses' Hair and Create a Detailed Horse Drawing Introduction: Drawing horses can be a challenging yet rewarding experience. One of the most intricate parts of drawing a horse is depicting its hair - from the flowing mane to the tail and even the texture of the coat. This tutorial will guide you through the process of drawing horses' hair and creating a detailed horse drawing, step by step. Step 1: Understanding the Anatomy of a Horse Before starting your drawing, familiarize yourself with the basic anatomy of a horse. Study pictures or live models if possible. Pay particular attention to the shape and form of the head, neck, body, legs, and the way they all connect. Knowledge of the underlying structure will help you create a more realistic and accurate representation when adding the details such as the hair. Step 2: Sketching the Basic Structure Using light strokes, sketch out the overall shape of the horse. Begin with simple geometric shapes like circles, ovals, and rectangles to represent different parts of the horse's body. Refine these forms into a rough outline of the horse's body, ensuring that proportions are correct. Erase any unnecessary lines leaving only the guideline for the main features. Step 3: Adding Musculature and Shading Horses have well-defined muscles which give them their powerful appearance. To show this, add some shadows where muscle groups meet. Observe real horses or photographs to understand where these areas typically occur. Remember, less is often more; subtle indications go a long way towards implying muscularity without overpowering the rest of the image. Step 4: Drawing the Mane The mane runs down the crest of the neck and is one of the most expressive elements of a horse's physique. Start by deciding on the direction of the wind blowing through the mane. Then, draw small clumps of hairs following this flow using short, quick lines close together but varying slightly in length and angle. Overlap these clusters to build up volume and density. Don't forget to leave gaps between strands to suggest individual hairs. Step 5: Depicting Texture within the Coat A horse's coat isn't smooth; instead, it has a slight sheen due to tiny overlapping scales called cuticles. You can replicate this effect by drawing faint parallel lines spaced unevenly apart, going in the same general direction. Vary the pressure applied to create darker and lighter tones simulating the play of light across the fur. Step 6: Creating Realistic Tails Like the mane, start by determining the direction of the wind or movement. Divide the tail into sections (bunches) and draw individual hairs within each section. Make sure to taper the width of the tail at both ends naturally. Also, consider how much of the underside of the tail would be visible based on its position relative to the viewer. Step 7: Finalizing Details and Touch-ups Once you've added the main elements, review your artwork carefully. Check for consistency in shading, line weight, and accuracy of detail placement. Correct any mistakes and enhance areas needing emphasis. If necessary, take photos of real horses under various lighting conditions to study the effects of light and shadow on their coats. Conclusion: Drawing horses requires patience and practice, especially when dealing with complex aspects like the hair. By breaking down the process into manageable steps and paying close attention to reference materials, anyone can learn to produce convincing equine illustrations. Happy drawing! Title: Easily Wash a Greasy Popcorn Bowl Introduction: Grease from popcorn can be stubborn and difficult to remove from bowls. However, with the right techniques and cleaning products, you can effectively clean your greasy popcorn bowl without much hassle. This tutorial will provide you with a step-by-step guide on how to wash a greasy popcorn bowl using common household items. Materials Needed: * Dish soap (preferably one that cuts through grease) * Hot water * Non-abrasive sponge or dishcloth * Paper towels * Baking soda (optional) Step 1: Remove Excess Popcorn Kernels and Oil Before washing the bowl, make sure to scrape off any leftover popcorn kernels stuck at the bottom of the bowl. Use a spatula or fork to do this carefully. Discard these remnants into a trash bin. If there is oil residue left over, wipe it down gently with paper towels until most of the surface grease has been absorbed. Key Tip: Removing excess food particles and oil first makes the actual washing process easier as it reduces the likelihood of food bits drying onto the sides of the bowl during cleanup. Step 2: Fill The Sink With Hot Water Fill up your sink about halfway with hot water. Make sure the temperature isn't too scalding but just enough to help break down the remaining grease stains. Add several drops of dishwashing liquid into the water and stir until suds form. Guideline: Hotter water dissolves oils more efficiently than cold water, making it ideal for breaking down tough grease stains often found in popcorn bowls. Step 3: Soak The Popcorn Bowl Place the greasy popcorn bowl into the soapy water. Allow it to sit submerged for around 15 minutes. For extremely dirty bowls, consider adding two tablespoons of baking soda to the mixture before soaking. The alkaline nature of baking soda enhances the effectiveness of the detergent against stubborn grease. Did You Know? Soaking allows time for the dirt and grime to loosen up, which decreases scrubbing effort later on. Step 4: Scrub The Inside Of The Bowl After soaking, take out the bowl and start scrubbing its interior using a non-abrasive sponge or cloth. Apply gentle pressure while moving in circular motions focusing on areas where there are visible stains. Be careful not to scratch any delicate surfaces like painted logos or decorative elements. Step 5: Rinse Thoroughly Rinely the soap thoroughly under running warm water. Check if all traces of dirt and grease have disappeared. Repeat steps four and five if necessary. Step 6: Dry The Bowl Once cleaned, place the bowl face down on a dry towel to air dry completely. Alternatively, you could also manually dry it using another fresh towel, ensuring no moisture remains inside crevices. Leaving damp spots might lead to bacterial growth or unpleasant odors over time. Conclusion: By following these simple yet effective steps, washing a greasy popcorn bowl becomes an easy task. Regular maintenance and quick cleanups prevent buildup of hardened grease, keeping your dishes sparkling clean and hygienic. Happy Cleaning! Zero is a number which means an amount of null size; that is, if the number of brothers is zero, that means the same thing as having no brothers, and if something has a weight of zero, it has no weight. If the difference between the numbers of pieces in two piles is zero, it means the two piles have an equal number of pieces. Before counting starts, the result can be assumed to be zero; that is the number of items counted before one counts the first item, and counting the first item brings the result to one. And if there are no items to be counted, zero remains the final result. We have to get some toys out for you to play with momma. I see, and they just put a decal on it to... British Columbia at the middle of the 19th century, had 3 options: Remain a colony of Britain, annex itself to the United States, or join the Dominion of Canada. The option of remaining a colony was undesirable, for Britain had long adopted a "little Briton" policy, under which the key to economic efficiency, and reduction of bloat, was to cut off connections to its various colonies. The option of joining with Canada was also viewed as impractical, and unlikely to occur; the closest Canadian neighbor lay more than a thousand kilometers away, to the east. For most British Colombians, annexation to the United States seemed most logical. Ultimately, however, British Columbia did confederate. The advocacy of the Confederation League, resulted in British Colombian's demanding that BC join Canada. On July 20, 1871, British Columbia joined Canada. In return, BC was absolved of its debt, which became Canada's responsibility, and Canada promised to build a railway to BC within 10 years. Title: How to Become Familiar with the Baywatch TV Series Introduction: Baywatch is an iconic American actionized drama series that follows the personal and professional lives of lifeguards who patrol the beaches of Los Angeles County, California. The show ran from 1989 to 2001, making it one of the longest-running television shows of all time. This tutorial will provide you with comprehensive steps to become familiar with this popular series. Step 1: Understand the Context Before diving into the world of Baywatch, understand its context. It was created by Michael Berk, Douglas Schwartz, and Gregory J. Bonann. The setting is primarily at Santa Monica Beach and later moves to Malibu's Zuma Beach in LA County. Knowing these details sets the stage for your understanding. Key Tip: Remember, while some elements might seem dated or unrealistic today, they reflect the era when the show was produced. Step 2: Learn About the Characters Get acquainted with the main characters, including Mitch Buchannon (David Hasselhoff), C.J. Parker (Pamela Anderson), Stephanie Holden (Alexandra Paul), Matt Brody (David Charvet), Summer Quinn (Nicole Eggert), Eddie Kramer (Billy Warlock) among others. Each character brings unique traits contributing significantly to plot development. Guideline: Pay attention to their roles within the team as well as their individual storylines. Step 3: Recognize Recurring Subplots Recognizing recurring subplots can enhance viewer engagement. For instance, there are ongoing themes around romance, rivalry, rescue missions, personal growth, tragedy, and triumph. These add depth to the narrative and make watching more enjoyable. Key Tip: Don't miss out on subtle nuances; every episode has something new to offer! Step 4: Watch the Show Systematically To fully grasp the progression of events, watch the episodes systematically. Start from season one and move chronologically forward. Skipping ahead may cause confusion since character arcs and relationships evolve over time. Guideline: Allocate dedicated time slots for viewing to maintain consistency. Step 5: Appreciate Behind-the-Scenes Details Knowing behind-the-scenes information enriches the viewing experience. For example, did you know that many real-life lifeguards appeared on the show? Or that despite initial skepticism, Baywatch became a global phenomenon? Such trivia adds another layer of interest. Step 6: Engage With Other Fans Join online communities, discussion boards, or social media groups centered around Baywatch fandom. Here, you can share insights, theories, favorite moments, quotes, etc., fostering a sense of community and deepening your appreciation for the series. Key Tips: Always respect fellow fans' opinions and avoid sharing spoilers if someone isn't caught up yet. Step 7: Reflect on What You've Learned After becoming familiar with Baywatch, take time to reflect on what you've learned - about television production, beach safety, human nature, friendship, leadership, and so forth. Consider how these lessons might apply to your own life. Conclusion: Familiarizing yourself with the Baywatch TV series involves more than just passive consumption. By following these steps, you actively engage with the content, thereby enhancing your overall enjoyment and comprehension. Whether relishing nostalgia or discovering it for the first time, prepare for a memorable journey through sunny Californian shores! Reading Lesson Design Rationale: In order for children to learn how to read and spell words, they need to develop a sturdy sense of phonemic awareness, as well as an understanding that spellings map out the phonemes in spoken words. Vowel sounds are the most essential phonemes children can learn because they can be found in every spoken and written word. Without the understanding of vowel sounds, written words cannot be accurately decoded. Since long vowels can be created with more than one letter, learning short vowels is a good place to begin. Beginning readers must know that words are made up of sounds. They must also learn the correspondences between written letters and their phonemes. This lesson will focus on the vowel correspondence a = /a/. Students will learn how to distinguish the /a/ sound in spoken words, practice spelling the /a/ sound by using Elkonin boxes, and identifying and decoding the /a/ sound in written text. 1. Introduce the lesson by explaining to the students that we use letters to write down words, and that these letters represent different sounds. In order to become excellent readers, we must learn how to match the letters to their sounds. Today, we are going to learn that the letter a makes the /a/ sound. As you begin understanding the sound the a makes, you will be able to read and spell all kinds of words. 2. Write the letter a on the board. Explain to the students that this a makes the /a/ sound. Ask students: "Have any of you ever seen a baby cry? *wait for student's responses* If you have, then you know that it is very important to be really quiet while the baby is sleeping, because if it wakes up, you'll hear a very loud and noisy, Aaaaaa! Can everyone make a crying baby sound for me. Great job! This sound is very similar to the sound made by the letter a, /a/!" 3. Now I have a fun tongue twister to share. Present the tongue twister that is written on the large poster. Read the tongue twister to the class, "Adam and Amy ate apples and accidentally sat on ants." Now let's read it together, "Adam and Amy ate apples and accidentally sat on ants." This time whenever you hear a word with the /a/ sound, I want you to cry like a baby, "Aaadam and Aaamy aaate aaapples aaand aaaccidentally saaat on aaants." Great job, class! Can anyone tell me a word they heard with the /a/ sound in it? *wait for student's responses* Let's practice spotting the /a/ sound in some spoken words. Ask the students the following questions and call on them to answer. Do you hear /a/ in sat or sit? Cat or dog? Rat or mouse? Tap or tip? 4. Ask the students to take out their primary paper and pencil. "Most of you already know how to write the letter a. Today, we are going to practice writing the letter a again, to refresh our memories. Everyone put on their thinking caps!" On the board, model writing an a, then go through the steps with the students: "For lowercase a, you start under the fence then you go around and touch the sidewalk, around and straight down. Can anyone tell me what sound this makes? On the count of three, lets all say it together! Aaaaaa! Very good, my classroom sounds like crying babies! Please write ten a's on your paper to represent the /a/ sound." The teacher should walk around while the students are writing and view everyone's a's. 5. After writing their ten a's, each child will receive an Elkonin letterbox as well as the appropriate letter tiles. Say: "Now we are going to practice using our knowledge of the /a/ sound to spell words. What if I wanted to spell the word "bat". First I am going to unfold three of my boxes because bat has three sounds, /b/ /a/ /t/. I am going to start with the first sound I hear in bat. B-B-B-at. I hear the /b/ sound. I will place the letter "b" in the first letterbox. The next sound I hear is the baby crying /a/ sound. I am going to place the letter "a" in the second letterbox, because it is the second sound I hear. Now I have ba-. To finish I need to find the last sound in the word bat. Ba-t-t-t. I hear the /t/ sound, which means I will place the letter "t" in the last letterbox. Each of you have your own letterboxes and I want you to try and spell some words using this same procedure that I just modeled for you. We will first open our letterboxes to only three boxes, meaning that each word we spell contains three sounds." I will now ask the children to spell pat, fan, dad, jam, and rat. Once they finish spelling each word, we will go around the different groups and I will ask every child to explain to the other members of their group how they spelled the particular word. We will continue on in this manner spelling 4 phoneme words such as: crab, flag, back, clap, and sand. Last, I will write each word one at a time on the board (pat, fan, dad, jam, rat, crab, flag, back, clap, sand) and call on students individually to read them out loud to the class. 6. Now we will work on reading words with the /a/ sound in the decodable text, Pat's 7. For assessment, I will give each student a picture page with the correct number of letterboxes to spell the word underneath the picture. As a class, we will name the first picture and then I will have them spell the word in the letterboxes below the picture. Remind the children that each box has only one sound. We will do this with each picture. I will have the picture words written on note cards. I will have the students, one by one, come read the cards I ask them to read. This will allow me to hear them saying the /a/ sound and it will also let me know that they have a better understanding of the /a/ sound while they read. I will also let the class scream out, "Aaaaa, one more time as loud as they can!" Murray, B.A., and Lesniak, T. (1999) "The Letterbox Lesson: A hands-on approach for teaching decoding." "" is also sometimes called "DS9". It is set in the late 24th century, at the end of "The Next Generation"'s time line and the start of Voyager's. It is not like "TOS" and "TNG" because it is set on a space station and is not about exploring. This means it has more soap opera elements (lots of stories about the characters). Most of the stories are about the Cardassian race and the war with the Dominion. Imagine you have a flat piece of paper with different shapes drawn on it. You want to move these shapes around on the paper in a special way - first, you want to make sure some of them don't change their size or position (this is like projecting them onto a plane). Then, you want to slide others horizontally or vertically so that they stay in the same place but get stretched out a little bit (this is called shearing). Now, let's think about how we can represent these movements using numbers. We will write down three special rules (or "matrices") that tell us exactly how each point on our paper moves during the projection and the shearing steps. First, let's create a rule for the projection step. To do this, imagine there's a light source directly above your paper. Some points on the paper receive more light than others depending on how close they are to the light. Those closer to the light will have brighter values while those further away will have darker ones. This creates a kind of shadow that helps us see which parts of the picture should remain unchanged when projected onto a new plane. Next, let's make up another set of rules for shearing the image. Shearing means moving things sideways without changing their heights. So, if you have a rectangle, shearing would stretch it into a parallelogram shape without making any part taller or shorter. In other words, shearing keeps the distances between horizontal lines constant while distorting vertical distances differently. Finally, combining both sets of rules gives us one big rule (a single 3x3 matrix) describing the entire transformation - first projection then shearing! By multiplying this final matrix with coordinates of every point in the original picture, we obtain its transformed location in the resulting image. That's why sometimes people call these transformations linear mappings since multiplication is essentially repeated addition - something very familiar even to young students. He went outside to catch the bus. I'm taking Keith Devlin's course "Introduction to Mathematical Thinking" on Coursera for the next seven weeks. We've started with some thoughts and readings on set theory and logic. And the first big thing in set theory is the way the stuff is written. We invented shorthand to help us write stuff. You may be old enough to remember Gregg shorthand. They discontinued the class the same year I intended to take it in high school. But I remember seeing it and really wanting to learn it. The idea behind shorthand, both Gregg and any other, is that you have agreed upon symbols allow you to write things quickly, or more concisely. Logic and set theory both use shorthand to do this. And in fact, you're not a stranger to it either! We live in an age of texting shorthand. FYI and CC have been around for a long time. But LOL is relatively new. So is IMHO. And every now and then I come across a brand-new one that completely throws me. Logic and set theory shorthand works the same way. Consider the statement: For every number that's a positive number, we know that that number is bigger than the number -13. That's a math mouthful! So instead we have invented some shorthand to make that easier. We use variables like pronouns. The first shorthand we use is variables. If the number were a guy, we would say, If some number is positive, he's bigger than the number -13. Unfortunately numbers aren't people. So instead of using he and she, we use x and y. So we say, For every positive number x, we know that x is bigger than the number -13. Then we get freaky! Because math and set theory have been around a really long time, we have symbols that are easy to write with pencil and paper. But not so easy to write with typing! Let's start with one of my fav's: . That upside down A stands for "for all" or "for every." If we were to invent that notation now, we would probably use FA or something easy to text. But we didn't, so we're stuck with it. Here's how we would use it in our example: x such that x is a positive number, then x is bigger than -13. You can also use it like this: x such that x is a dish, you will wash x before you go to bed. How about some more... means "there exists" and can be used like this: a dish in the sink, so you're not going to bed yet. means "therefore" or "thus" or "because of that, this will happen..." Here's a way to use it: a dish in the sink, you're not going to bed yet. means "and" - to be used like this: a dish a glass in the sink, you're not going to bed and you just might lose your phone privileges tomorrow. Math isn't a new language - it's a shorthand of normal language. And it isn't really hard. Give this sentence a shot. I'll be you can figure out what the symbols mean! a dish a glass that isn't washed you're getting into serious trouble! Title: How to Change the Background Color in Adobe Photoshop Introduction: Adobe Photoshop is an incredibly powerful tool that can help you modify and create stunning visuals. One common task when editing photos or graphics is changing the background color. This tutorial will guide you through the process using various tools and techniques available within Photoshop. By following these steps, you'll be able to change any background color with ease. Step 1: Open Your Image in Photoshop Start by launching Adobe Photoshop and opening your image file via File > Open or simply dragging and dropping it into the workspace. Make sure the layer containing the object whose background you want to change is unlocked (indicated by no lock icon next to its name). If locked, double click on the layer thumbnail and hit 'Enter' without making changes. Key Tip: Working on a duplicate layer ensures safety of original data. To do this, right-click the layer > Duplicate Layer. Name the new layer appropriately before clicking OK. Step 2: Select the Object Using Quick Selection Tool Select the Quick Selection Tool from the Tools panel (or press Shift + W until it appears), which allows selecting specific areas based on their contrast against surrounding pixels. Click and drag over the area you wish to isolate. The selection should adjust as you move along the edges of the object. Use the [ or ] keys to decrease or increase brush size if needed. Guideline: Be patient while refining the edge; accuracy here determines how seamless your final result will look. Step 3: Refine Edge for Better Selections Once you have roughly selected the object, go to Select > Refine Edge. A dialog box opens up offering several options like View Mode, Radius, Smooth etc. Play around with these settings till you get a clean outline. Check Smart Radius under Edge Detection to automatically adapt radius according to varying border widths. Don't forget to preview results using different view modes such as On Layers or Black & White. Step 4: Create New Layer Fill Go back to the Layers palette and add a Solid Color fill layer by pressing Ctrl+Shift+N or choosing Layer > New Fill Layer > Solid Color. Set the mode dropdown menu to Multiply so only the underlying colors show through the transparent parts of our isolated object. Choose a color using the Color Picker window. Step 5: Mask Unwanted Areas Now we need to hide the colored background outside our subject. With the new solid color layer active, select Add Layer Mask at bottom of layers panel or choose Layer > Layer Mask > Reveal All. Then Alt+Click between two layers to edit mask content directly. Brush over unwanted areas with black paintbrush (B) set to 100% opacity. Switch to white if you accidentally cover wanted portions. Conclusion: Changing the background color in Adobe Photoshop requires careful selections, fine-tuning, and clever utilization of masks and blend modes. Practice makes perfect - experiment with diverse pictures, objects, and hues to master this skill! Do you remember this that you played with last time? A matrix, that has many rows, but only one column, is called a column vector. Title: How to Fold a Square of Paper into Thirds Introduction: Folding a square piece of paper into thirds is a simple yet useful skill that can be applied in various situations such as origami, crafting, or even packing items securely. This tutorial will provide you with clear, concise, and easy-to-follow steps along with helpful tips and guidelines. By following this guide, you'll learn how to fold a square of paper into equal thirds accurately and efficiently without any visual aid. Materials Needed: 1. A square piece of paper (any size) 2. Flat surface 3. Ruler (optional but recommended) Step 1: Prepare Your Workspace and Materials Choose a clean, flat workspace free from distractions where you can lay out your materials. Place the square piece of paper on the table so its one side faces you horizontally. If using a ruler, place it near the edge of the paper for quick reference during the process. Step 2: Identify the Centerline Locate the midpoint of both horizontal edges by folding the top corner down towards the bottom corner and creasing firmly at the halfway point. Unfold the paper after marking the crease line visually or lightly pressing the folded edge against the ruler if available. Repeat this action for the other side to ensure symmetry. These two lines represent the centerlines of your square paper. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Press firmly when creating folds to make crisp, accurate creases. - Be patient; precise folding takes practice. - Use the ruler only as needed to maintain straightness while avoiding overreliance. Step 3: Align Edges Along the Centerline Bring together the left and right corners of the paper by aligning their respective edges along the vertical centerline created earlier. The goal here is to have an even overlap between both sides. You may need to adjust the alignment slightly until satisfied. Make sure the outermost edges are aligned properly before proceeding to the next step. Step 4: Create the First Equal Third Once confident about the alignment, press down gently on the overlapping sections and flatten them neatly onto the lower part of the sheet. Next, take note of the remaining uncreased area above the folded section - this represents one of three equal parts vertically. It should occupy approximately one-third of the total height of the original unfolded paper. Step 5: Complete the Second Equal Third Perform another gentle fold similar to Step 3, bringing the newly exposed upper edge toward the middle crease. Again, ensure proper alignment with the existing fold line. Afterward, carefully smooth out the new layer so it matches the previous fold made in Step 4, completing the division of the paper into thirds vertically. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Maintain consistency in fold placement throughout the entire process. - Double-check all measurements visually or through tactile feedback before finalizing the folds. Conclusion: Congratulations! With patience and precision, you have successfully folded a square piece of paper into thirds. Utilize these skills in future projects involving papercraft, origami, or packaging needs. Remember, accuracy and repetition lead to mastery, making every attempt valuable practice towards perfecting your technique. Happy crafting! Title: How to Cut Lemongrass Lemongrass (Cymbopogon citratus) is an essential ingredient in many Asian dishes due to its unique citrus flavor and aroma. Fresh lemongrass can be intimidating to cut and prepare because of its tough outer layers and fibrous texture. However, with patience and practice, you can master cutting lemongrass like a pro. This step-by-step guide will provide clear instructions, helpful tips, and crucial guidelines to help you confidently cut lemongrass for your culinary creations. **Things You'll Need:** * Fresh lemongrass stalks * Sharp knife * Cutting board * Measuring spoons or cups (optional) ## **Step 1: Selecting Fresh Lemongrass Stalks** When purchasing fresh lemongrass from a grocery store or farmer's market, look for firm, pale yellow stems that are heavy for their size. Avoid those that have brown spots or show signs of wilting. The bottom part should feel solid, while the top may be slightly softer. If possible, buy organic lemongrass as conventional options might contain pesticide residues. ![Selecting Fresh Lemongrass](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/4/47/Prepare-and-Use-Fresh-Lemongrass-Step-1-Version-3.jpg/aid206859-v4- [GRF and EXP still talking about scary stuff] I don't know, maybe a rolling pin. Were there birds walking near the water? Lesson: Understanding Sets and Solving Simple Equations Hi there! Today we're going to learn about two important math concepts - "Sets" and solving basic algebraic equations. These topics are typically covered in early middle school or even elementary school. Let's dive in! **Part 1: Introduction to Sets** Imagine you have a bag full of different colored marbles: red, blue, green, yellow, etc. In mathematics, this collection of unique items (or marbles in our example) is called a Set. Each marble represents an element of the set. We often write sets inside curly brackets like so: {red, blue, green, yellow}. Now let's look at some examples from the snippet: * The set {1, 2, 3, ...} contains all natural numbers starting from one. It doesn't have an end since it goes on forever. * The set {1} simply contains the number one. This is known as a Singleton Set because it has just one element. * The empty set {} has no elements. Think of it like an empty box. Question: Which of these sets is the subset of all the given sets? Remember, a Subset is a set whose elements are all part of another set. **Part 2: Power Sets & Basic Operations** The Power Set of a set consists of all possible subsets of the original set, including the empty set and the original set itself. So if we were to find the power set of {1, 2, 3}, it would include {}, {1}, {2}, {3}, {1, 2}, {1, 3}, {2, 3}, and {1, 2, 3}. That makes eight total subsets! --- Next up, let's talk about solving quadratic equations. They may seem scary at first but once you understand them, they're quite straightforward. Look at question 3a in the snippet which asks us to solve `x^2 + 2 = 0`. To solve this equation, we need to isolate `x` by moving everything else to the other side of the equals sign. To do this, follow these steps: 1. Subtract 2 from both sides: `x^2 = -2` 2. Since there's nothing under the square root on the left side yet, take the square root of both sides while remembering that a negative times a negative gives a positive result: `x = (-2)` However, note that this will give complex solutions because we cannot take the square root of a negative real number without leaving the realm of real numbers. Middle schoolers generally don't deal with complex numbers, hence why this problem type isn't introduced until later stages. Remember, understanding mathematical concepts takes practice. Keep practicing problems similar to those found in the snippet and soon enough, you'll become more comfortable working through them!
