We had the video, and then we would cook it and break it. Induction works like dominos - you first knock down the first domino and each one knocks down the next one along and so on. In maths, you prove that it's true for the number 1 then you prove that IF it's true for a number, then it's true for the next number along. Then you say: "Ah, but it's true for 1, so it must be true for 2, which means it must work for 3, and 4, and 5, etc". "every natural number can be expressed as the sum of distinct powers of 2" [By the way, if this WASN'T true, then binary wouldn't really work :-) ] Now, let's prove it for n = 1. 1 can be written as 2^0. So that's that one done. Let's look at a number X, which will ASSUME (we have no proof or anyt hing, we're just going to say so) that it CAN be written as a sum of powers of 2. where any of these little x_n s can be either 0 or 1. 0 indicates that there is none of this power of two in the sum. 1 indicates there is. You need distinct powers, so none of these x_n s can be more than 1. So to use your example, The next number after X must be X + 1. X + 1 can be written as X + 2^0 so you just make sure that x_0 is turned into a 1. If 2^0 is "taken" (i.e. x_0 is = 1) then that means that you have to gather the existing 2^0 and the new one together and make a 2^1 which you can add (so you would add a 2^1 and take away their 2^0 because you "used it up"). If you can't do that (if x_0 and x_1 are both = 1) then you add the 2^0 of yours to the 2^0 and the 2^1 already there to make a 2^2 ("using up the existing 2^0 and 2^1) and add that. The point is: Those x_0, x_1, x_2, etc in that equation that I wrote that go all the way up to x_n: You just go along them until you find the first one that's a zero and turn it into a 1 and turn all the ones before it into a zero. In that way, you'll have added 1 to the number and written X+1 as a sum of distinct powers of 2. Phew. If you recognise binary, that will sound familiar. Now we know that IF X can be written as a sum of powers of 2 then so can X + 1. We know it's true for 1, because proved it in step 1. So then we know that it must be true for 1 + 1 = 2. But if it's true for 2 then it must be true for 2 + 1 = 3 But if it's true for 2 then it must be true for 3 + 1 = 4 But if it's true for 2 then it must be true for 4 + 1 = 5 Congratulations, you've just proved it for all natural numbers. [CHI is sitting next to her mother] Title: How to Get the Weekly Free Music on iTunes Introduction: iTunes offers users free music every week as part of their "Single of the Week" promotion. This feature allows you to discover new artists and enjoy fresh tunes without spending any money. By following this simple guide, you can learn how to access and download these weekly free songs from iTunes. Step 1: Open iTunes on your computer Launch iTunes by clicking its icon on your desktop or searching for it in your applications folder (Mac) or Start menu (Windows). Make sure that you have the latest version installed to ensure proper functionality and compatibility with current promotions. Key tip: If you don't already have iTunes installed, download it directly from Apple's official website at <https://www.apple.com/itunes/>. Step 2: Navigate to the iTunes Store In the top bar of the iTunes window, click on 'iTunes Store' to enter the digital marketplace. Here, you will find various categories like Music, Movies, TV Shows, and more. The Single of the Week is typically located under the Music category. Guideline: Always check if you are connected to Wi-Fi before browsing through the store, as streaming previews may consume data on some internet plans. Step 3: Access the Free On iTunes section Once inside the iTunes Store, look for the "Free On iTunes" section, which should be prominently displayed near the top of the page. Click on it to reveal all available content currently offered for free. Key Tip: Alternatively, you can search for 'Single Of The Week' or simply type 'free song' into the iTunes Store Search Bar to quickly locate the free track. Step 4: Browse through the selection The Free On iTunes section contains different types of media such as apps, videos, and songs. To filter out only the music tracks, select the 'Music' tab within the banner. Now, scroll down until you see the Single of the Week. Step 5: Preview the Song Before downloading, make sure you actually want the song by previewing it first. Simply click the play button next to the title to listen to a short snippet. Key Tip: Don't forget to rate the song after listening! Your feedback supports emerging artists and contributes to better recommendations tailored specifically for you. Step 6: Download the Track After deciding that you would like to keep the song, click the price tag next to the title - since it is free, there won't be an actual charge listed here. Instead, the word 'GET' will appear; click it once more to confirm the download. Guideline: Keep an eye on your hard drive space while downloading multiple files simultaneously to avoid filling up your storage too rapidly. Step 7: Locate Downloaded Songs To view your newly acquired free music, go back to your Library, then choose the 'Songs' tab. All recently added songs will display alphabetically, making them easy to locate. You can also sort them based on other criteria such as artist name, album, genre, etc., using the dropdown menu above the list. Conclusion: By following these steps, you now know how to get the weekly free music on iTunes and expand your musical library without breaking the bank. Remember to regularly visit the iTunes Store every Thursday to take advantage of new releases and uncover hidden gems among upcoming musicians. No you can't say Mrs Bear it's bath time anyway you'd height find a rocket first. This lesson will focus on common Japanese particles as well as vocabulary. Particles are very important to the Japanese language. They appear before many verbs and often after the subject of the sentence. The particles covered in this lesson are: is used to talk about where an event or action takes place. It is like the English work "at." For example, if you read at the library, you would say: (I read at the library.) Many sentences often use more than one particle. If you wanted to modify the above sentence to say, "I read books at the library," you would actually use the particle as well. I read books at the library. The place where the event takes place usually appears first in the sentence, but Japanese does not have really strict rules about sentence placement. and are both used to mean "to." If you want to say you are going somewhere or returning somewhere, you need to use or . Both particles signify movement. Examples: I will return home. (You can also use e instead of ni here). I am going to Japan. (You can also use ni instead of e here). can also be used to say "at x time" and "on x day." Examples: . I have a class at nine. I return home Monday. In the above sentences, cannot be replaced by . The particle is used to indicate the direct object of a sentence. Many verbs use this particle. Examples: (I drink water.) (I am watching a movie.) (I am listening to music.) (I eat meat.) The particles for verbs stay the same regardless of what form the verb is in. Below is a vocabulary list to help you form new sentences in Japanese. Green tea *Note: For time words such as "tomorrow," and "today," you do not need to use a particle with the sentence ( ). Study the sentences below to become familiar with the new vocabulary. I don't eat breakfast. I will eat dinner at a restaurant. What will you do over the weekend? I will watch a movie. I will not go to school on Tuesday. I will read a magazine at home. I drink green tea. What time will you be returning? What time will you go to bed? I will go to sleep at 10. I will watch television at home. When will you go? I will come/arrive tomorrow. Title: How to Hide a Small Object or Note Introduction: Hiding small objects or notes can be useful for various reasons such as keeping valuables safe, playing pranks, or simply organizing your space. This tutorial will provide you with detailed steps and helpful tips on how to effectively hide small items out of sight. Remember that the goal is to keep the object hidden from casual observation while still being able to retrieve it when needed. Step 1: Choose an Appropriate Hiding Place Selecting the right spot is crucial for successfully hiding a small object or note. Consider these factors when choosing a location: - Accessibility: The hiding place should be easy for you to access but difficult for others to stumble upon accidentally. - Visibility: Opt for locations where the item blends in with its surroundings, reducing the chances of it being noticed. - Security: Avoid areas that are frequently disturbed or cleaned, ensuring your hidden object remains undisturbed. Key Tip: Select multiple hiding spots if possible, so you have backup options if someone discovers one of them. Step 2: Camouflage the Item To make your small object less noticeable, try camouflaging it by matching its color or pattern with the surrounding environment. For example, if you're hiding a pen, conceal it among other writing instruments or inside a book about pens. Guideline: When using this method, ensure that the chosen container or area has some connection to the hidden item, making it less suspicious if discovered. Step 3: Disguise the Item Another effective way to hide a small object or note is by disguising it as something else. Here are some ideas: - Turn a coin into a button by gluing it onto clothing. - Hollow out a book and create a secret compartment for your item. - Use a fake rock with a hollow center to store small valuables in your garden. Key Tip: Make sure the disguise is convincing and doesn't attract unnecessary attention. Step 4: Create a Diversion Sometimes, the best way to hide something is by drawing attention away from the actual hiding place. You can do this by placing decoy items nearby or creating a messy appearance around the area. However, be cautious not to overdo it, as too much clutter might prompt someone to clean up and potentially discover your hidden item. Step 5: Test the Effectiveness of Your Hiding Spot Before fully committing to a hiding place, test its effectiveness by attempting to locate the object yourself. If you can find it easily, consider adjusting the position or trying another spot altogether. Step 6: Memorize the Location Once you've found a suitable hiding place, commit its location to memory. It may help to associate the hiding spot with a landmark or specific feature in the room. This will enable you to quickly and discreetly retrieve the item when necessary. Key Guideline: Refrain from sharing the hiding place with anyone unless absolutely necessary, as doing so increases the risk of exposure. Conclusion: By following these steps and utilizing the provided tips, you'll be well on your way to mastering the art of hiding small objects or notes effectively. Just remember to always choose appropriate hiding places, camouflage or disguise the item, create diversions if needed, test the effectiveness of your hiding spot, and memorize its location. Happy hiding! " And when I put up this finger... " Think honey what's a duck say? Another property of a photon is its wavelength. Let's pour some vegetable soup in the pot. Title: How to Hook Salmon Eggs for Bait Salmon fishing is an exciting and rewarding activity that requires patience, skill, and the right bait. One popular choice among anglers is salmon eggs, which are both visually appealing and irresistible to fish. This tutorial will guide you through the process of hooking salmon eggs for bait, providing detailed steps and helpful tips along the way. By following these instructions, you'll be able to create effective and attractive bait presentations that increase your chances of reeling in a trophy-worthy catch. **Step 1: Gather Your Materials** Before starting, ensure you have all necessary materials at hand. You will need: * Fresh or cured salmon eggs (curing enhances their durability and visibility) * Size 2-6 hooks (depending on egg size; smaller hooks work best for small eggs) * A pair of needle nose pliers or hemostats * A scissors or sharp knife **Step 2: Prepare the Hook** Begin by threading the line through the eye of the hook. Choose a hook with a gap wide enough to accommodate one or two eggs, depending on the desired presentation. If using a swivel, attach it first and then tie the line to the hook using a clinch knot or other secure connection. **Key Tip:** Choosing the correct hook size and shape can significantly impact your success rate. For example, octopus hooks offer better holding power due to their curved shank and gap design. **Step 3: Select the Right Number of Eggs** Determine whether you want to use single or double egg presentations based on factors such as water current speed, target species, and personal preference. Double egg setups tend to provide more stability and resistance against strong currents, while singles may appear more natural and enticing when fished slowly. **Step 4: Position the Egg(s)** Place the egg(s) onto the hook point with the aid of your needle nose pliers or hemostats. Make sure they rest flat against the shank and are positioned close together if using multiple eggs. Ideally, the hook point should protrude from beneath the egg without piercing it initially. **Key Guideline**: Keep the eggs intact and avoid damaging them during this process, as undamaged eggs release scent and attractiveness longer than crushed ones. **Step 5: Secure the Egg(s)** Carefully wrap the line around the base of the egg(s), trapping them between the coils and the shank. Use several turns of line to create a snug fit but leave enough space near the hook bend for proper penetration upon striking. **Step 6: Finish the Knot** Once satisfied with the positioning and security of the egg(s), complete the knot by wrapping the tag end around the standing part of the line several times before passing it through the loop created by the initial wraps. Moisten the knot, pull tight, and trim any excess line. **Step 7: Test the Setup** I don't know how an owl goes. How will we put your socks back on? Title: How to Create Curl Bangs Like a Pro Introduction: Curl bangs can add volume, texture, and movement to your hairstyle, creating a playful yet chic look. This tutorial will guide you through the process of curling your bangs with precision and ease. With clear instructions, helpful tips, and essential guidelines, you'll master the technique of curl bangs in no time! What You'll Need: * Hair curler or wand (1-inch barrel recommended) * Heat protectant spray * Comb * Hair clip(s) * Hairspray * Bobby pins (optional) Step 1: Preparation Start by washing and drying your hair as usual. Apply heat protectant spray throughout your hair to prevent damage from styling tools. Make sure to evenly distribute the product using a comb. Divide your hair into sections, clipping them up except for the section containing your bangs. Key Tips: * Aim for medium hold hairsprays; too much strength may make it difficult to restyle later. * Use a ceramic or tourmaline curler for smoother results and less damage. Step 2: Sectioning Your Bangs Create a triangular shape when selecting the portion of your hair that forms the bangs. Point cut the ends if needed to create a wispy, natural appearance. The width should be about two fingers wide at the center part, tapering towards the temples. Guidelines: * Keep the length above eyebrows or longer depending upon personal preference. * For layered looks, ensure that there is enough weight near the roots so they don't stick out oddly after curling. Step 3: Curling Technique Wrap only the midshaft to ends of your bangs around the curling iron or wand while keeping the base away from direct contact with the heating element. Hold the tool vertically during this process. Maintain tension in the strands but avoid pulling tightly. Helpful Tip: * To determine the direction of curl, consider whether you want your bangs swept left, right, or straight down. If sweeping to one side, curl the hair moving away from that side (i.e., curl towards the face for a left sweep). Step 4: Releasing the Curl Gently release the curl once heated, letting it cool before touching it again. Once cooled, carefully slide your finger between the coil to loosen its form slightly without unraveling it entirely. Spritz lightly with hairspray to secure the set. Important Note: Do not brush or comb the curls immediately post-curling. Brushing creates frizz and disrupts the formation of the ringlets. Step 5: Creating Volume Tease the crown area gently using a comb for added lift and dimension. Smooth over the surface layer with a soft bristle brush for an elegant finish. Optional Step 6: Pinning Back For a more polished look, pin back one side of your freshly curled bangs using bobby pins. Choose discreet placement locations behind the ear or higher along the temple. Secure both sides symmetrically for balance. Conclusion: Practice makes perfect - experiment with different techniques until you find what works best for your unique hairstyle and preferences. Soon enough, achieving gorgeous curl bangs will become second nature. Enjoy flaunting your fabulous new do! I want to put it back in the box. Is that your second thing o' macaroni and cheese? Mama she was playin' with the plane. Hello young mathematicians! Today we are going to talk about topology, which is a branch of math that deals with properties of spaces that are preserved under continuous transformations. It might sound complicated, but don't worry, we will break it down into simpler concepts! Imagine you have a bunch of lines passing through the origin (the center point) in three-dimensional space. Each line represents a point in a new kind of space called the real projective plane, orRP for short. This space has some interesting properties, one of which we will explore today. Now, let's say we want to define what it means for a subset ofRP to be "open." To do this, we will relate points inRP to subsets of our familiar three-dimensional space ( ). Specifically, each point inRP corresponds to a unique line in passing through the origin. So, when we talk about an open subset ofRP , we really mean the set of all lines in that correspond to those points in the open subset ofRP . But here comes a challenge! The origin (0,0,0) in is tricky because it's the intersection of many lines passing through the origin. However, the origin by itself isn't considered an open set in since you can't draw a little circle around it without including other points. Because of this, we need to be careful when defining open subsets ofRP so that they behave nicely with respect to their counterparts in . So, when is a subset ofRP open? Simple! A subset ofRP is open if its corresponding subset of is open according to the rules we already know for . In other words, if we can draw little circles around every point in thesubsetof without leaving the subset, then the original subset ofRP is indeed open. By studying topology, we can better understand different kinds of spaces and the relationships between them. Who knows, maybe your fascination with topology could lead you to discover new mathematical ideas! Keep exploring and never stop being curious. Happy learning! The aim is to work out what number each letter stands for. Each letter represents a different digit, and the first digit of a multi-digit number should not be zero. In addition, there should ideally be only a single solution, and the words should also make up a meaningful phrase - as above. So, can you solve the problem? Answer on the next page... In column 4, the addition of S and M has produced a carry-over into column 5. The carry-over can only be a 1, since the letters represent single digit numbers. So for instance, if you add 8 and 9, you get 17, so you carry 1 into the next column. But you can never carry more than 1. So M = 1. In order for there to be a carry from column 4 to column 5, S + M has to come to at least 9, so S is either 8 or 9. Therefore S + M is 9 or 10, and so O is 0 or 1. But we have established that M = 1, so therefore O = 0. If there were a carry from column 3 to column 4, then E would be 9 and N would be 0. But O = 0, so there is no carry, and S = 9. If there were no carry from column 2 to column 3, then given that E + O = N and also that we know that O = 0, this would mean that E = N. But that is impossible. So therefore there is a carry from column 2 to column 3, and N = E + 1. If there were no carry from column 1 to column 2, then (N + R) mod 10 = E, and given that N = E + 1, this would mean that E + 1 + R = E mod 10, so therefore R would be 9. But we know that S = 9, so therefore there must be a carry from column 1 to 2, and R = 8. To produce a carry from column 1 to column 2, D + E = 10 + Y. Since Y cannot be 0 or 1, D + E must be at least 12. As D is at most 7, then E is at least 5. Also, N is at most 7, and N = E + 1. So therefore E is 5 or 6. If E were 6, then to make D + E at least 12, D would have to be 7. But N = E + 1, so N would also be 7, which is impossible. Therefore E = 5 and N = 6. To make D + E at least 12, we must have D = 7, and so Y = 2. A word of explanation about the use of mod 10. Modular arithmetic is sometimes referred to as clock arithmetic, although a clockface actually works in mod 12; so for example if it is 8 o'clock and you add 6 hours, you end up at 2 o'clock, so (8 + 6) mod 12 = 2. In the same way, if you add 7 and 8 you get 15. But if you are performing an addition, as we are here, you would carry the 1, and leave the 5. So therefore (7 + 8) mod 10 = 5. This tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step, providing detailed explanations and useful tips along the way. **Step 1: Gather Necessary Materials** Before starting the creation process, ensure you have these items ready: * A blank DVD or CD with sufficient storage space (at least 4.7 GB for a DVD). * A computer running Windows 7. * A drive capable of burning ISO files onto discs. **Step 2: Understand What a Recovery Disc Is** A recovery disc contains all necessary drivers and system files required to perform a clean installation or repair of your operating system. It's essentially a backup copy of your system which can be used when the primary OS becomes unstable or unusable. **Step 3: Access System Recovery Options** Click on 'Start', then type 'backup and restore' into the search bar and select 'Backup and Restore'. From there, choose 'Create a system repair disc' from the left side panel under 'Restore' section. **Step 4: Select Your Drive** You'll now see a new window titled 'Create a System Repair Disc'. Here, select the correct DVD/CD burner drive where you want to create the recovery disc. Make sure no important data resides on this disk as it will be erased during the writing process. **Step 5: Burn The Disk Image** After selecting the drive, click on 'Create Disc'. Windows 7 will now prepare the disc by converting the system image file (usually named `install.wim`) into an ISO format suitable for burning onto physical disks. Once prepared, the actual burning procedure begins automatically. Please do not interrupt this operation until completion. **Key Tips:** * Keep track of time - Depending upon your computer speed, this could take anywhere between 10 minutes to over an hour. * Monitor closely - Stay near your PC while the disc is being burned just in case anything goes wrong so you can react quickly. * Label appropriately - After creating the disc, label it clearly as 'Windows 7 Recovery Disc' followed by the date created. **Step 6: Verify Successful Creation** Once completed, eject the disc and check whether it was successfully written. You can do this by inserting the disc back into the same drive and observing if any error messages appear. If everything looks fine, congrats! You've just made yourself a handy recovery tool. **Guidelines:** * Store safely - Keep this disc away from dust, heat, moisture, and direct sunlight. Consider storing multiple copies at different locations for added security. * Regularly update - Create a new recovery disc every six months or after significant system changes like driver updates or major software installations. * Use carefully - Only use the recovery disc when absolutely needed, such as when performing system restores or reinstalls. Using it casually might lead to unexpected results. By following these steps and guidelines, you've ensured that you won't lose valuable data due to unforeseen circumstances affecting your Windows 7 installation. Good job! Title: How to Cut a Chicken Fillet: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of cutting a chicken fillet with precision and confidence. By following these steps, you'll be able to create evenly sized pieces that are perfect for cooking various dishes like stir-fries, curries, or saut ed recipes. This skill is essential for any home cook looking to prepare meals using fresh ingredients. Before starting, ensure your workspace is clean and equipped with a sharp knife, a chopping board, and a raw chicken breast. Always handle raw poultry carefully to avoid contamination. Step 1: Place the Chicken Breast on the Board Position the chicken breast on the chopping board with the smooth side facing down. The smoother side typically has less fat and is easier to cut. Placing the breast flat ensures stability while making cuts, reducing the risk of injury. Step 2: Trim Excess Fat (Optional) Examine the chicken breast for excess fat around the edges. Use your sharp knife to gently trim off any unwanted fat. While this step isn't necessary, removing excess fat can improve the taste and texture of cooked chicken. Step 3: Butterfly the Breast (Optional) If you prefer thinner slices or want to cook the chicken faster, consider butterflying the breast. To do so, place your hand firmly on top of the breast and make a horizontal cut almost all the way through, stopping about half an inch before reaching the other side. Open up the breast like a book, creating one large, thin piece. Step 4: Locate the Tenderloin The tenderloin is a small muscle located underneath the main part of the breast. It usually runs parallel to the larger portion and can be identified by its tapered shape. Decide whether to keep or remove the tenderloin based on personal preference or recipe requirements. Step 5: Remove the Tenderloin (Optional) To detach the tenderloin, slide your knife along the connective tissue between the tenderloin and the main breast. Apply gentle pressure until the tenderloin comes loose. Set aside if keeping; discard otherwise. Step 6: Pound the Meat Flatter (Optional) For more uniform cooking, pound the chicken breast into an even thickness. Cover the breast with plastic wrap or wax paper, then use a meat mallet or rolling pin to gently tap the thicker areas until they match the thinnest parts. Be careful not to tear the meat. Step 7: Make Lengthwise Cuts Decide how many pieces you would like your chicken breast to yield. Divide the width of the breast by the number of portions desired. For example, if you want four equal strips, divide the breast lengthwise into quarters. Holding your knife horizontally, begin at one end of the breast and slice slowly and steadily towards the opposite end. Repeat as needed for additional cuts. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Keep your knife sharp: A dull blade requires extra force when cutting, increasing the likelihood of accidents. Regularly sharpen knives for optimal performance. - Maintain proper posture: Stand upright with feet shoulder-width apart and distribute weight evenly between both legs. Avoid hunching over the cutting board. - Secure the ingredient: Firmly hold the chicken breast down using your non-dominant hand while making cuts. Never let fingers get too close to the knife edge. - Practice good hygiene: Clean surfaces thoroughly after handling raw chicken. Always wash hands, utensils, and cutting boards immediately after contact with uncooked poultry. That might be her only time though. Then he picked up all the stuff out of the other ones. These lists are based on samples of published texts in English, and (less often) samples of recorded spoken language. The lists differ slightly according to the sources chosen, but they are very reliable. The items listed may represent more than one actual word; they are lemmas. For instance the entry "be" contains within it the occurrences of "is", "was", "be" and "are". The top 100 lemmas account for 50% of all the words in the Oxford English Corpus. Title: How to Perform a Perfect Headstand in Gymnastics Introduction: The headstand is one of the most fundamental gymnastic positions that displays strength, balance, and control. It requires significant upper body and core strength as well as proper technique and alignment. This step-by-step guide will walk you through the process of mastering this impressive skill while ensuring safety throughout your practice. Before starting, warm up properly by doing some light cardio exercises and dynamic stretches to prepare your muscles for the demands of a headstand. Step 1: Prepare Your Space Find an open area with enough space around you to prevent any collisions or injuries during your attempts. Make sure the floor surface is soft yet firm; avoid slippery surfaces like marble or tile floors. A yoga mat or carpeted area would be ideal. Also, remove any sharp objects or breakable items near your practicing spot. Step 2: Practice Wall Assistance Stand about two feet away from a wall, facing toward it. Place both hands on the ground, shoulder-width apart, keeping them aligned with your shoulders. Lower your head down between your hands so that the crown of your head touches the ground. Walk your feet closer to the wall until your hips are directly above your shoulders. Keep your back straight and engage your core. Key Tip: Focus on distributing weight evenly between your forearms and the crown of your head. Avoid putting too much pressure on your neck or head, which could lead to injury. Step 3: Engage Leg Muscles Once you're comfortable in the tucked position (hips over shoulders), start engaging your leg muscles. Lift one leg off the ground and hold for a few seconds before returning it to meet the other leg. Repeat this action with the opposite leg. Gradually increase the duration you can maintain having one foot off the ground. Guideline: Remember to breathe naturally throughout these steps. Maintain steady breaths to supply sufficient oxygen to your brain and working muscles. Step 4: Kick Up into Position When you feel stable lifting one leg at a time, try kicking up into the full headstand position. From the tuck position, bend one knee and point the toes downward. Use the momentum generated from extending this leg upward and slightly backward to propel yourself into the vertical headstand position. As soon as you kick up, bring the second leg up as well. Key Tips: -Keep your gaze fixed on a stationary point in front of you to help maintain balance. -Ensure your spine remains neutral - do not arch or round your back. -Engage your entire core and squeeze your glutes tightly. Step 5: Hold the Position Try holding the headstand for several seconds once you reach the top. Beginners may only manage a couple of seconds initially but gradually build up endurance over time. To dismount safely, slowly lower one leg down towards the ground followed by the second leg. Return to the tucked position against the wall before standing upright again. Guidelines: -Always perform headstands under supervision or have someone nearby who can assist if needed. -Never attempt headstands on hard surfaces without adequate padding due to risk of injury. -If experiencing dizziness or discomfort, stop immediately and rest. Conclusion: Mastering a headstand takes patience, persistence, and practice. Consistently following these steps and incorporating regular strengthening exercises targeting your arms, shoulders, and core will expedite progress. Over time, performing headstands will become more natural and effortless. Happy training! Here's some green or blue pants for the doctor. Microeconomics is the science of how people make decisions at the small scale. It is different from macroeconomics which looks at how the economy works as a whole ("on aggregate"). In microeconomics, we might look at how a person chooses what to buy at the store, or how many things a company will make. Some parts of microeconomics include "Consumer Theory" and "Theory of the Firm", which study how people and businesses make decisions. Game theory looks at how people make decisions when the outcome (how something ends) depends on what decision another person makes; when agents are said to act "strategically". Letter Sound Accuracy, Egg Carton Game |Activity Type: Build Accuracy| |Activity Form: Game| |Group Size: Small Group| |Length: 10 minutes| |Goal: Given printed letters, the student can identify them and say the sound of each ( a -> /a/ )| |Items: All letter sounds learned so far| What to do - Put a letter sticker (or write a letter) in the bottom of each egg cup in the carton. The letters should be a mix of all letters learned so far. Then put a counter (or a dime) in the carton and close it. (Note that you can also play this game with letter combinations or irregular words, or a mix of all of these.) - Would you like to play the egg carton game? Here's how we play. I shake the carton and then open the lid and say the sound of the letter that the counter landed on. My turn first. - Open the lid, look underneath the counter, pause, say the sound, and hold the carton up so students can see the letter. You get one point for each correct sound. First player to earn 10 points wins. So that's one point to me. - Now I close the lid again and pass it to my neighbor. Pass the carton to the student on your left. Make sure that, when they say the letter sound, they show the letter to the other students. - If they are correct say: Well done. One point to you. Remember your score. Okay, now pass the carton to your neighbor. - If they are incorrect or don't know the sound, say: Can anyone help? What's the sound? Have the student repeat the sound and then pass the carton to his or her neighbor. If a student has trouble with multiple letters, make a note in an Activity Log. - Continue playing and keeping score. You can either pass when it is your turn or keep playing. Try to get some letters wrong and ask the students for help. View this video to see an example of how the egg carton game is played. Photos of the egg carton game. They found a snake, a spider, a bat and a skull. They screamed and ran out of the tent. They were very scared. They ran to their mom, who was sitting on a wide blanket. She hugged them and gave them some water and some hats. She told them that the tent was for a Halloween party. She told them that the snake, the spider, the bat and the skull were not real. They were just toys. She told them that they should not go into places they did not know. They should always ask before they enter. Anna and Ben felt sorry. They regretted going into the tent. They learned their lesson. They said sorry to their mom and thanked her for the water and the hats. They decided to play on the wide blanket, under the shade of an umbrella. They felt safe and happy. Title: Decide Whether to Call Someone: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Making the decision to call someone can be influenced by various factors such as time, context, relationship with the person, and your personal communication preferences. This guide will help you weigh these considerations systematically so that you can make an informed choice about whether or not to initiate a phone call. By following this tutorial, you'll learn how to assess situations effectively, ensuring clearer communication and fostering healthier relationships. Step 1: Identify Your Purpose Determine the reason behind wanting to call the individual. Key questions include: Is it work-related? Are you checking up on their wellbeing? Or do you simply want to catch up? Clearly defining your purpose ensures that both parties benefit from the conversation and avoid potential misunderstandings. * Time-sensitive matters warrant immediate action; thus, calling is appropriate. For non-urgent topics, consider scheduling a convenient time via text message instead. * Remember, respectful communication starts with being mindful of others' boundaries and schedules. Always ask if they have the capacity to engage in meaningful dialogue before launching into heavy subjects. Step 2: Evaluate The Context Consider the current circumstances surrounding your relationship with the person. Has there been any recent conflict or change in status (e.g., promotion, breakup)? If so, proceed cautiously and thoughtfully when deciding whether to call them. * Use empathy and emotional intelligence to gauge the appropriateness of reaching out during challenging times. * When in doubt, send a brief text first expressing your support or concern, then wait for a response indicating readiness to talk over the phone. Step 3: Assess Personal Preferences Everyone has unique communication styles and comfort levels regarding phone calls. Some people prefer quick texts, while others enjoy lengthy conversations. Reflect on past experiences communicating with this particular individual. * If they consistently respond positively to phone calls, chances are they appreciate hearing from you via voice chat. * However, if previous attempts at calling were met with resistance, consider using alternative methods like messaging apps or emails. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Be aware of cultural differences influencing communication preferences. What might be considered polite in one culture could come across as intrusive in another. * Respect privacy settings and "do not disturb" functions on devices. Ignoring these signals may lead to miscommunication or unwanted confrontation. * Establish mutual expectations around availability and preferred modes of communication early on in relationships to prevent awkwardness later. Conclusion: In conclusion, determining whether to call someone involves careful consideration of several factors, including your purpose, the context, and individual communication preferences. Following this comprehensive guide allows you to approach decisions related to making phone calls confidently, enhancing interpersonal connections and reducing unnecessary stress. Practice active listening and maintain open lines of communication to foster strong bonds based on understanding and trust. Copyright 2007 Dorling Kindersley Mosses, ferns, and their relatives are plants that do not produce flowers but reproduce by means of SPORES. Most live in shady or damp habitats. CONIFERS are non-flowering plants that reproduce by making seeds. The stem, or rhizome, of a fern grows horizontally through the soil. Tiny curled-up fronds (leaves) grow from small buds on the rhizome. The buds unroll and the frond expands. The fronds of some ferns grow 20 ft (6 m) long, but others reach only 1/2 in (13 mm). Most mosses grow in short clumps, or cushions. They do not have true roots, but short, slender growths, called rhizoids. Rootlike rhizoids anchor moss to soil, rock, or bark, but do not draw up water. Instead, leaves absorb moisture in the air. There are 11 main phyla of non-flowering plants: The last four form a group called gymnosperms-they produce seeds instead of spores. Non-flowering plants reproduce by releasing large numbers of tiny spores. These minute organisms consist of one or a few cells inside a tough coat. Many non-flowering plants rely on wind to carry their reproductive spores as far away as possible. This reduces competition with the parent plant for light, water, and important nutrients. If a spore lands in a damp place, it germinates (sprouts) and grows into a new plant. Trees and shrubs whose seeds develop in woody cones are called conifers. The 550 species include pines, firs, and cedars. Conifers form dense forests in colder, northern regions. Most keep their leaves all year long. Conifers have male and female cones. The male cones release pollen grains (male sex cells), which are blown by the wind. If pollen lands on the female cones, it fertilizes the female egg cells. The fertilized eggs develop into seeds. After one or two years, when the seeds have matured, the female cone opens up. It drops winged seeds, which germinate wherever they land. Leaves shaped like needles help conifers survive in cold, harsh climates. The leaves are tough and coated with a waxy outer coat, or cuticle. The narrow shape, toughness, and cuticles all help the leaves withstand high winds and extreme temperatures. They also reduce water loss. A group of plants called cycads have large seed-producing cones that can grow to more than 22 in (55 cm) long. They have sturdy trunks topped by long, divided leaves, and look more like palm trees than conifers. Cycads grow in tropical and subtropical regions. They are descended from a group of plants that flourished 250 million years ago. Today, there are 140 species of cycads. The main characters in myths are usually gods, demigods, or supernatural humans, while legends generally feature humans as their main characters. Many exceptions or combinations exist, as in the "Iliad", "Odyssey", and "Aeneid". Myths are often endorsed by rulers and priests and closely linked to religion or spirituality. In fact, many societies group their myths, legends, and history together, considering myths to be true accounts of their remote past. What next baby bear by Jill Murphy. " Your child has started school, but he still needs you to read to him at home. Your child will do better in school, and you'll enjoy the time spent together. Here are helpful tips for reading to and with young children in school, kindergarten through third grade: Keep reading to your child even when he can read. Read books that are too difficult or long for him to read alone. Try reading books with chapters and talk about what is happening in the story. Encourage your child to make predictions about what will happen next, and connect characters or events to those in other books and stories. Talk with your child about reading preferences that are beginning to develop. Ask whether she likes adventure stories, mysteries, science fiction, animal stories, or stories about other children. Encourage her to explain the reasons for preferences. Talk with your child about favorite authors and help him find additional books by those authors. Take turns reading a story with your child. Don't interrupt to correct mistakes that do not change the meaning. Talk about the meaning of new words and ideas introduced in books. Help your child think of examples of new concepts. Talk with your child about stories using the notions of the beginning, middle, and end of the story to organize thinking and discussion. Ask your child to tell why a character might have taken a specific action. Ask for information from the story to support her answer. Enjoy yourself and have fun. The most important thing you can do to help your child become a successful reader is communicate that reading is valuable and enjoyable. TIPS IN READING TO CHILDREN IN GRADES FOUR TO SIX It is critical that your child keeps reading and being read to at this age. Young readers need to become practiced at reading, and the only way to get good at it -- is to practice! Helpful tips for reading to and with children in grades four through six: Take turns reading a book with your child. Ask your child to compare a book to another familiar book. How are the characters alike or different? Do the stories take place in similar settings? How are the illustrations the same or different? Ask what part of the story or book your child liked best and why. Ask if your child liked the ending of the story. Why or why not? Ask your child what type of mood the story or chapter in a book creates. Ask how the author creates the mood. For example, does she use certain words, events, or settings that create a particular feeling? If your child has read more than one book by the same author, ask how the books are similar or different. Who else are you going to have at your party? It can be difficult to determine if an intersex baby is genetically male or female (with XY chromosomes or XX chromosomes). They may also have male and/or female secondary sex characteristics (such as body shape). However, there is a wide range of variation in sexual anatomy. There are many more subtle forms of sex anatomy, or sex chromosome differences. These don't even show physically. Some won't show up until later in life. Sometimes, the variation may appear when the baby reaches puberty or becomes an adult. It may not even be detected in an individual's lifetime, but as technology gets more advanced, that chance dims down. Jackson DeForest Kelley (January 20, 1920 - June 11, 1999) was an American actor. Glass of Water Puzzler Confused by the answer to Ray's water-in-the-glasses puzzler? You weren't alone. Here's what Ray posed: You have a four-ounce glass and a nine-ounce glass. You have an endless supply of water. You can fill or dump either glass. It turns out that it's possible to measure six ounces of water using just these two glasses. What's the fewest number of steps in which you can measure six ounces? Here's how Ray solved the puzzler. First, fill the 9 ounce glass with water. Next, pour the water in the 9 ounce glass into the 4 ounce glass, until it is full. This leaves 5 ounces in the 9 ounce glass. Now empty the 4 ounce glass. Now, fill the 4 ounce glass, using the remaining water from the 9 ounce glass. Once the 4 ounce glass is filled, you'll be left with just 1 ounce of water in the 9 ounce glass. Empty the 4 ounce glass of water again. Transfer the 1 ounce of water from the 9 ounce glass into the 4 ounce glass. Fill the 9 ounce glass with water. Pour water from the 9 ounce glass into the 4 ounce glass, until the 4 ounce glass is full. Guess what? Since the four ounce glass already has 1 ounce of water in it, it will only take an additional 3 ounces of water. Guess how much that leaves in the 9 ounce glass? You got it--6 ounces. Title: How to Perform Reverse Push-ups: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Reverse push-ups are an advanced upper body strength exercise that targets your triceps, shoulders, and chest muscles. Unlike traditional push-ups, reverse push-ups require you to lift your body weight while facing upward, engaging different muscle groups and providing a unique challenge. This tutorial will guide you through proper form, technique, and safety measures to help you master this impressive move. Please consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new fitness regimen. Step 1: Prepare Your Space Find a sturdy, elevated surface free from clutter or potential hazards. A bench, chair, or steps can work well as long as they have no wheels and won't slide during the exercise. The height should be sufficient to allow your arms to extend fully when positioned perpendicular to the ground. Make sure there is enough space around you to perform the movement without obstructions. Key Tip: Choose an object that feels stable and secure; wobbling surfaces increase injury risk. Step 2: Assume the Starting Position Place both hands shoulder-width apart on the edge of the chosen platform, palms down, fingers pointing forward. Extend your legs straight out behind you with toes curled under and heels touching the floor. Engage your core and maintain a neutral spine throughout the entire exercise. Your neck should be aligned with your spine, looking slightly ahead rather than directly at the floor. Guideline: Keeping your body aligned prevents strain on your back and ensures maximum benefit for your target muscles. Step 3: Lower Your Body Slowly bend your elbows, keeping them close to your torso, and lower yourself towards the ground until your head nearly touches the base of the platform. Throughout this motion, ensure your hips remain level and don't sag towards the floor. Maintain control and avoid rushing this part of the exercise. Key Tip: Focus on maintaining tension throughout your body - think about pulling yourself upwards even as you descend. Step 4: Press Upward Once your arms reach a 90-degree angle (or slightly less), press firmly into the platform with your hands and extend your elbows to return to the starting position. Squeeze your chest and arm muscles as you rise to maximize engagement and strengthening. Avoid locking your elbows completely at the top of the motion. Guideline: Control is crucial here - aim for smooth transitions between phases instead of rapid movements. Step 5: Modifications & Progressions If standard reverse push-ups prove too challenging initially, consider these modifications: * Elevate your feet further using a higher platform. * Perform the exercise against a wall instead of an elevated surface. * Use resistance bands looped over the platform for added support. As you gain strength, progressively decrease assistance or increase difficulty by performing variations like diamond push-ups (with index fingers and thumbs forming a triangle) or one-arm versions. Remember: Safety first! Always prioritize correct form and alignment over adding reps or increasing intensity. Over time, consistent practice will improve your ability to execute successful reverse push-ups. I think we should've taped this over again. Copyright 2007 Dorling Kindersley Coasts, which form the boundary between land and ocean, receive a constant battering from the wind and waves. In calm weather, the water merely laps at the shore, but on windy days, towering, foam-capped breakers smash onto coasts. It's no wonder that the shapes and even location of coasts are constantly shifting, as waves erode the land and as SEA LEVELS CHANGE. In some places, coasts are retreating inland by several metres each year. Coastal features, such as cliffs and arches, are formed by wave erosion. As the sea beats on rocky headlands, softer rocks are eroded (worn away) to form hollow caves. Twin caves on either side of a headland may eventually wear right through to form an arch. As the battering continues, the top of the arch collapses to leave an isolated pillar. In the last few million years, sea levels have risen and fallen by up to 200 m (660 ft). Scientists believe these are caused by temperature changes, as Ice Ages come and go. During Ice Ages, sea levels are low because large amounts of water are frozen. When the climate warms, the ice melts and sea levels rise. Today, sea levels look set to rise because of global warming. This will bring a risk of flooding to coasts. During an Ice Age, the weight of the ice depresses (pushes down) the land. Sea levels are low, so the crust beneath the ocean is not depressed. When the weather warms, melting ice causes sea levels to rise. This effect is partly offset by the land springing up when released from the ice's weight, while the ocean bed sinks beneath the weight of water. No I put about eighty teaspoons in before that one. Title: How to Clean a Circumcised Penis Introduction: Keeping a circumcised penis clean is an essential part of personal hygiene. A proper cleaning routine can help prevent irritation, infection, and other complications. This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to clean your circumcised penis effectively and safely. Step 1: Gather Necessary Supplies Before starting the cleaning process, gather all necessary supplies. These include mild, unscented soap, warm water, a soft cloth or washcloth, and a towel. Avoid using harsh soaps, as they may cause skin irritation. Step 2: Wash Your Hands Always begin by washing your hands thoroughly with soap and warm water. This practice reduces the risk of introducing bacteria into the genital area during the cleaning process. Step 3: Prepare Soap Solution Create a gentle soap solution by mixing a small amount of mild, unscented soap with warm water. The solution should be enough to create suds but not overly sudsy, which might lead to excessive drying out of the skin. Step 4: Separate Foreskin (if present) If there's any remaining foreskin attached, gently pull it back from the head of the penis. Make sure that this action does not cause pain or discomfort. If separation causes significant difficulty or distress, consult a healthcare professional. Step 5: Apply Soapy Water Dip the soft cloth or washcloth into the prepared soap solution. Squeeze out excess liquid before lightly rubbing the dampened cloth over the entire shaft of the penis, including the scrotum. Be careful around sensitive areas like the urethral opening at the tip of the penis. Step 6: Rinse Thoroughly Rinse off the soap residue completely under running warm water. It's crucial to ensure no soap remains, as leftover soap could result in skin irritation. Pat dry the area gently with a clean towel afterward. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Perform these steps daily, preferably during showering or bathing. - Never forcefully retract non-retractable foreskins, as doing so can cause harm. - Refrain from using shared towels to minimize potential cross-contamination risks. - Consult a medical expert if redness, swelling, unusual discharge, or persistent pain occurs. - Regular checkups are recommended to monitor overall penile health. Conclusion: Proper cleansing of a circumcised penis promotes good hygiene, preventing possible health issues while maintaining optimal comfort and wellbeing. By following the abovementioned steps meticulously along with observing the provided key tips and guidelines, one can maintain excellent penile health effortlessly. Passive Voice Memory - New Crown Grade 2 - Lesson 8 - One set of cards per group This is a variation of the children's game memory or (shinkei suijaku) in Japanese. It uses the passive forms "used" and "seen." The objective is to match a sentence with the corresponding picture. It is fairly easy, and is best used for introductory lessons stressing comprehension, and not language production. 1. Break the students into lunch groups and have them put their desks together. 2. Have the students lay the cards out on their desks face down 3. The student whose turn it is, first selects a sentence card (one of the long cards) and turns it over. The student then reads the sentence aloud, e.g. "This is used in a hospital." 4. The student then selects a picture card (the smaller cards) and turns it over. If the picture is of an object that is used in the designated place (e.g. a stethoscope), then the student takes the two cards and gets one point. If the picture card does not match the sentence card, then the student returns the cards placing them face down, and it becomes the next students turn. 5. Students play until there are no cards remaining on the desk. The student with the most points at the end of the game wins. This can be simplified by removing "seen" and replacing it with "used." The objects featured in the picture cards are fairly hard words. Because of their difficulty, and the actual lack of a need to say the names of the objects, I simply did not teach the students the words for the objects. The aim of the activity is to get a basic understanding of the meaning of the passive voice, not to learn the names of these objects, but optimally objects that students already know the words for would be used instead. Aromatic hydrocarbons are aromatic organic molecules that form flat ring-shaped bonds. The most simplest of aromatic hydrocarbons are benzene and indole. They can have one ring, "heterocyclic", like benzene or 2 rings, "bicyclic" like napthalene (primary ingredient in mothballs) or many rings, "polycyclic" like anthracene (red dye colourant). Hey how come you crossed it with the pencil? Do you want to use our pizza cutter on this slice of pizza? Creating an Instagram avatar can be a fun and creative way to personalize your profile. An avatar is a small image that represents you and appears next to your name throughout the Instagram app. By following this detailed tutorial, you'll learn how to create an engaging and unique Instagram avatar with ease. ### Section 1: Preparation **Step 1: Choose Your Image** Decide on an image that best represents you or captures your interests. This could be a photo of yourself, a favorite hobby, pet, or any other visual element that reflects your personality. Make sure the chosen image has good resolution and lighting. A clearer image will result in a better-looking avatar. **Key Tips:** * Select a square image (1:1 aspect ratio) as it fits perfectly within the circular frame of the Instagram avatar. * Opt for simple backgrounds without too many distractions to keep the focus on the main subject. --- **Step 2: Crop and Edit Your Image** Open your preferred editing software or mobile app and crop the selected image into a square format if needed. Adjust brightness, contrast, saturation, and sharpness to enhance the overall appearance. You may also want to remove any unwanted elements from the picture during this process. **Key Guidelines:** * Maintain the focal point of the image by positioning it centrally. * Experiment with different filters or effects to add a cohesive look to your avatar and other social media profiles. --- ### Section 2: Uploading the Image to Instagram **Step 3: Access Your Profile** Log in to your Instagram account using either the mobile app or website. Tap on your current avatar at the bottom right corner of the screen to access your profile page. If using the website version, click on your username located at the top right corner of the screen instead. --- **Step 4: Update Your Profile Picture** On your profile page, tap 'Edit Profile.' On desktop, simply hover over your existing avatar and select 'Update Profile Photo.' Now, choose between uploading a new photo or selecting one from your camera roll or gallery. **Key Points:** * Use high-quality JPEG files for optimal results when uploading through the website. * For consistency across platforms, consider matching your Instagram avatar with those used on other social media sites. --- **Step 5: Position the Image Correctly** Once you have selected the image, resize and move it around so that the most important part of the image sits nicely inside the cropped circle. Zoom in or out accordingly while ensuring all essential details are visible. Save changes once satisfied with the composition. **Guidelines:** * Keep in mind that only the center portion of your selected image will appear as your avatar. * Avoid placing text or critical information near the edges since they might get cut off. --- ### Congratulations! You now know how to create an eye-catching and representative Instagram avatar. Remember to periodically update your avatar to reflect seasonal themes, holidays, or changing preferences. Most importantly, enjoy showcasing your creativity and individuality through your newly crafted avatar! [reaches for the pitcher and "pours" into a cup] Why do you think he's giving him some water? Title: How to Perform a Rugby Pop Pass The pop pass is a fundamental skill in rugby that allows players to quickly move the ball between teammates. It involves passing the ball backwards or laterally while keeping it low to the ground. This type of pass is particularly useful when trying to break through a tightly packed defense or when making a lateral movement along the field. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to execute a successful rugby pop pass: **Step 1: Grip the Ball Correctly** Start by gripping the ball with both hands at either end, fingers spread wide apart. Your index fingers should be placed on top of the seams, pointing towards your target. This grip will help ensure a secure and accurate pass. Make sure your thumbs are wrapped around the back of the ball, supporting its weight without applying too much pressure. A relaxed but firm grip enables better control and reduces the risk of fumbling the ball during the pass. ![Gripping the Rugby Ball](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/a/ad/Hold-a-Rugby-Ball-Step-1.jpg/aid3485960-v4- That wouldn't happen Sis if you'd keep your mouth shut and eat. Reading] Title: How to Deal with People Who Criticize Something You Made Introduction: Criticism is an inevitable part of creating anything and sharing it with the world. It can be difficult to hear negative feedback about something you've put time, effort, and passion into, but learning how to handle criticism gracefully can help you grow as a creator and improve your work. This guide will provide you with steps and tips on dealing with people who criticize something you made. Step 1: Take a deep breath and remain calm. When receiving criticism, it's natural to feel defensive or upset. However, reacting negatively can escalate the situation and potentially damage relationships. Instead, take a moment to collect yourself before responding. This pause allows you to process your emotions and think clearly about how to proceed. Key tip: Remember that criticism is often more about the person giving it than the work itself. They may be projecting their own insecurities or biases onto your creation. Step 2: Consider the source of the criticism. Not all criticism is equal. Analyze where the critique is coming from. Is it from someone knowledgeable in the field, offering constructive feedback? Or is it from someone with a grudge or ulterior motive? Understanding the source can help you determine whether the criticism has merit and should be acted upon. Guideline: Be open-minded to critiques from experts or those with relevant experience, as they might offer valuable insights for improvement. Step 3: Assess the validity of the criticism. After considering the source, examine the content of the criticism. Is there truth to what they're saying? Can you identify specific aspects of your work that could be improved based on their feedback? If so, view this as an opportunity for growth and make necessary adjustments. Key tip: Focus on the message rather than the tone. Even if the delivery is harsh, try to find the underlying value in their words. Step 4: Respond politely and professionally. Once you've processed the criticism, craft a thoughtful response. Thank the person for taking the time to share their thoughts, even if you disagree with them. Acknowledge their points and express your appreciation for their perspective. Guideline: Maintain respect and avoid getting drawn into arguments or debates. Stay focused on understanding their point of view and demonstrating professionalism. Step 5: Learn from the experience. Regardless of whether the criticism was warranted or not, use this encounter as an opportunity to learn and grow. Reflect on how you handled the situation and consider ways you could improve your reaction in future instances. Additionally, analyze your work and determine if any changes are needed based on the feedback received. Key tip: Keep a file or journal of critiques you receive, as these can serve as valuable references when revising your work or starting new projects. Step 6: Surround yourself with supportive individuals. While it's important to be receptive to criticism, surrounding yourself with positive influences is equally crucial. Seek out mentors, peers, and friends who encourage and inspire you, providing constructive feedback without tearing you down. Guideline: Cultivate a network of supporters who uplift and motivate you, helping counterbalance the occasional negative critique. Conclusion: Receiving criticism can be challenging, but handling it effectively is essential for personal and professional growth. By following these steps and maintaining a positive attitude, you'll be better equipped to navigate critical encounters and turn them into opportunities for improvement. Disable Comments on Instagram on iPhone or iPad: A Comprehensive Guide Instagram is an excellent platform for sharing photos, videos, and connecting with others. However, there may be instances when you want to disable comments on your posts to maintain a positive environment or avoid unwanted interactions. This tutorial will guide you through the process of disabling comments on Instagram using your iPhone or iPad, ensuring that only approved content appears under your posts. Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. Make sure your Instagram app is updated to the latest version before proceeding. 2. The changes made in this tutorial are reversible; if you change your mind later, you can re-enable comments. 3. Only postal administrators (the account owner) can modify comment settings. 4. Keep in mind that turning off comments entirely might prevent users from engaging positively with your posts. Consider setting up keyword filters instead to block specific words or phrases. 5. You cannot disable comments for individual stories or live videos. 6. When you disable comments while posting a new photo or video, the restriction remains until you manually enable them again. Step 1: Open the Instagram App Start by launching the Instagram application on your iPhone or iPad. If you're not already logged in, enter your credentials to access your account. Step 2: Navigate to Your Profile Tap on the profile icon, usually located at the bottom right corner of the screen. It looks like a small person silhouette. Once you tap on it, you should see your own profile page with all your published posts. Step 3: Select the Post With Active Comments From your profile page, scroll down to find the particular post where you wish to turn off comments. Tap on the selected post to open it and view its details. Step 4: Access Comment Settings Below your post, you'll notice several options such as likes, messages, save, etc., represented by their respective icons. Look for and tap on the speech bubble icon, which represents 'comments.' After tapping, you'll see the existing comments thread below your image. Swipe left over any comment to reveal additional actions including reporting or deleting it. But we need more global control here - so swipe further! Step 5: Manage Comment Controls Swiping left beyond the regular comment controls reveals two additional icons: one for restricting accounts from commenting and another for advanced comment settings. Tap on the gear icon labeled 'Advanced Settings'. Step 6: Turn Off Comments Under Advanced Comment Settings, look for 'Turn Off Commenting'. Slide the button next to it towards the right side until it turns blue indicating that commenting has been disabled. Step 7: Confirm Changes A confirmation message pops up saying 'Comments have been turned off.' Press 'OK' to confirm and finalize the action. Now no user can leave a comment on this specific post anymore. Remember, these steps help manage negativity but also limit engagement. So weigh the pros and cons carefully based on your unique situation before deciding to disable comments completely. Alternatively, consider managing comments effectively using features like keyword filtering or limiting replies to people you follow or mention in your posts. We are sharing a special blog today, with corroboration from first grader Jackson Kirby. Jackson recently made a visit to ChickenTown for help with his Tempe elementary school science project. Jackson wanted to demonstrate the life cycle of a chicken egg. With help from the Mayor of ChickenTown, Mark Rhine, Jackson began his journey. Below are excerpts of Jackson's background research report. "I wanted to learn where chickens came from. I wanted to learn how a chick grows in an egg. I chose to do a demonstration for my science project. I will demonstrate the life cycle of a chick egg! All girl chickens are hens, and all boy chickens are roosters. The rooster fertilizes the egg. It is called mating. Not all eggs can become a chick. The eggs we get at the store are laid by hens, that did not mate with the roosters. First, I got fertilized eggs from ChickenTown. ChickenTown is a happy farm where chickens live. Then, I brought the eggs home and put the eggs in an incubator to keep them warm. Last, I put X on one side and O on the other side so I could remember to flip the eggs two times a day. I flipped them every morning and every single night!!! On day 1, the chick is a very small white spot. I had to keep it warm so it would grow. The chick grows inside the egg everyday! On day 2 the white spot turns into an EMBRYO. It is growing blood vessels! On day 3, it looks like a question mark backwards. Its heart is beating! It eats food and water from the yolk. On day 4 you can see the eye! I looked for it with my special flashlight to see it growing. I turned off all the lights and put the egg on the light. A sac full of clear water grows around the chick. It is called the AMNIOTIC SAC. It protects the chick. On day 5, it's wings and legs start growing! Another sac called the ALLANTOIS MEMBRANE is growing. It helps the chick breathe. On day 7, it's been 1 week. The chick is now called a FETUS. It has all its important parts now. It has a brain, heart, stomach, eyes, ears, and a beak. It even has its toes! On day 8, the wings and legs get bigger! On day 9, it starts to look like a chick and not an alien. On day 11, it grows eyelids. The beak can open and close! It also is growing its feathers. On day 14, it has been 2 weeks. It is a lot bigger. It has claws now! Pretty soon it gets very crowded in the egg. On day 18, almost all the yolk is gone. On day 19, there is only a little bit left. The chick is almost out of food. On day 20, it practices breathing. I can now hear it peeping. On day 21, it's hatch day. It uses its egg tooth to get out of the shell. The egg tooth is a hard bump on the chicks beak. First the egg pips that means breaks the shell. Then it zips! That means it gets all the way out of the egg. The chick is very tired. I interviewed Mark Rhine the Mayor at ChickenTown. He said raising eggs to chickens is very important for the farm. I am helping him by hatching eggs. He said that makes me a farmer too. He will take the chicks when they are two weeks old. They will have a happy life laying eggs and roaming free." Jackson successfully hatched 6 chicks, and 2 turkeys. What an adventure! Sadly, participating in the life cycle of something as simple yet truly magnificent as a chicken egg, is not a common experience children get to have these days. It is our responsibility to show our children more than a television. The usefulness of man is dying, but not on our watch!. If you ask Jackson what his favorite part of his science project was, he will say when the eggs hatched of course. Then he says simply, learning about life. Title: How to Choose a Cell Phone Plan Introduction: Selecting a cell phone plan can be an overwhelming task given the numerous options available in today's market. This guide will walk you through the process step-by-step, providing in-depth explanations and helpful tips along the way. By following these instructions carefully, you should feel confident about choosing a plan that meets your needs both practically and financially. Step 1: Assess Your Needs Determine what type of user you are - heavy, moderate, or light. Heavy users typically consume more than 5GB per month with constant streaming and downloading; moderates fall around 2-4 GB monthly; lights use less than 2GB mainly for calling and texting. Knowing this upfront prevents overpaying for unused data. Step 2: Compare Carriers There are four major carriers (Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile, Sprint) and several MVNOs (prepaid brands like Cricket Wireless, Boost Mobile). Research coverage maps online to see which networks perform best where you live and work. Don't forget to check customer service ratings too! Key Tip: Consider purchasing phones unlocked directly from manufacturers so they won't bind you to any specific carrier. Step 3: Understand Data Options Most plans offer shared data buckets but some providers have introduced unlimited plans as well. Be aware of throttling speeds after reaching certain limits if opting for an 'unlimited' package. If you assessed yourself as a light user, consider family plans or shared data packs since they often come cheaper when divided among multiple people. Step 4: Check International Calling & Roaming Fees If you travel frequently abroad or call internationally, look into international roaming packages offered by different companies. Some might charge hefty fees while others provide free texts/minutes depending on destination countries. Step 5: Read The Fine Print Always read the terms and conditions before signing up. Watch out for auto-renewal clauses, early termination fees, hidden charges, etc. It pays to understand exactly what you're getting into before committing. Step 6: Calculate Total Cost Add device costs, taxes, activation fees, potential line access fees, insurance premiums, and other possible expenses to get the real picture of total cost. Remember, lowest advertised rates may not necessarily mean cheapest overall expenditure once all additional costs are factored in. Step 7: Negotiate Or Look For Discounts Many providers give discounts based on employment status, military background, student enrollment, or even loyalty. Reach out to their customer support and negotiate a better deal. There's no harm in asking! In Conclusion: Choosing a cell phone plan involves careful consideration of various factors including usage habits, network reliability, budget constraints, and personal preferences. Taking time to go through these steps methodically ensures making an informed decision instead of rushing into one hastily. Happy hunting! Um, and the rest she really wouldn't recognize. Title: How to Always Win at Risk Introduction: Risk is a strategy board game produced by Hasbro. The objective of the game is global domination by controlling every territory on the game board. This tutorial will provide you with strategies and techniques that will significantly increase your chances of winning at Risk. Step 1: Understand the Game Setup Familiarize yourself with the game components including the game board, armies (game pieces), cards, and dice. Each player starts with armies placed on territories they control. Take turns placing one army per territory until all starting armies are distributed. It's crucial to understand the value of setups; having more territories early gives an advantage due to increased income during reinforcement phases. Key Tip: Strategic placement of initial troops can give you a head start. Try occupying high-value continents like North America or Europe where possible. Step 2: Know Your Odds In combat, understanding probability is essential. When attacking with 'x' amount of troops against 'y', here's what you should expect: * 1 v 1: ~50% chance of success * 2 v 1: ~78% chance of success * 3 v 1: ~91% chance of success * 3 v 2: ~64% chance of success * 4 v 2: ~80% chance of success * 4 v 3: ~58% chance of success Remember these numbers as rough guides only; luck plays its part too! Step 3: Defending Wisely Defense is often about choosing when to fight back. If attacked, consider retreating if odds aren't favorable. Retreating saves troops for future battles. Also, remember defender has an inherent advantage - attacker needs to roll higher than defender to win. Key Guideline: A common mistake is overcommitting forces trying to hold onto marginal lands. Don't be afraid to concede ground if necessary. Step 4: Utilizing Cards Effectively Capture sets of territories containing a complete continent to earn risk cards which may contain extra armies. However, don't hoard them - using them wisely for additional troops can turn the tide in a critical battle. Step 5: Planning Ahead Visualize potential outcomes after each move. Consider both best-case and worst-case scenarios. Good planning includes anticipating opponents' moves and preparing countermeasures. Step 6: Controlling Continents Holding entire continents provides bonus armies during reinforcement stages. Focus on dominating large continents first before moving onto smaller ones. Remember, bigger continents yield more bonuses. Key Strategy: Australia might seem isolated but capturing it secures a steady stream of three armies per turn. Step 7: Timing Is Everything Knowing when to strike is vital. Waiting too long could allow others to grow stronger, but striking prematurely risks losing valuable resources. Look for opportunities where you can maximize gains while minimizing losses. Step 8: Diplomacy & Alliances While not explicitly stated in the rules, diplomacy among players is common. Form temporary alliances to target mutual enemies, then break them once their usefulness ends. But keep your word when giving it, as reputation matters. Conclusion: Winning consistently at Risk requires strategic thinking, probabilistic reasoning, foresight, and sometimes even diplomatic skills. With practice, patience, and adherence to these steps, you'll find yourself winning more frequently. Happy conquering!
