A separate space is created for folktales, which are not considered true by the people who tell them. As stories spread to other cultures or as faiths change, however, myths can come to be considered folktales. Sometimes myths and legends get merged. Their divine characters get recast as humans or as demihumans (such as giants, elves, and faeries). You don't have anything to pour it in. Parts of Speech Verbs: All the Right Moves Verbs are words that name an action or describe a state of being. Verbs are seriously important, because there's no way to have a sentence without them. While we're on the topic, every sentence must have two parts: a subject and a predicate. There are four basic types of verbs: action verbs, linking verbs, helping verbs, verb phrases. You Could Look It Up Verbs are words that name an action or describe a state of being. The action of an action verb can be a visible action (such as gamble, walk, kvetch) or a mental action (such as think, learn, cogitate). Quoth the Maven To determine if a verb is transitive, ask yourself, "Who?" or "What?" after the verb. If you can find an answer in the sentence, the verb is transitive. Action Verbs: Jumping Jack Flash Action verbs tell what the subject does. For example: jump, kiss, laugh. An action verb can be transitive or intransitive. Transitive verbs need a direct object. Intransitive verbs do not need a direct object. Chain Gang: Linking Verbs Linking verbs join the subject and the predicate. Linking verbs do not show action. Instead, they help the words at the end of the sentence name and describe the subject. Here are the most common linking verbs: be, feel, grow, seem, smell, remain, appear, sound, stay, look, taste, turn, become. Although small in size as well as number, linking verbs are used a great deal. Here are two typical examples: Quoth the Maven To determine whether a verb is being used as a linking verb or an action verb, use am, are, or is for the verb. If the sentence makes sense with the substitution, the original verb is a linking verb. Many linking verbs can also be used as action verbs. For example: Mother's Little Helper: Helping Verbs Helping verbs are added to another verb to make the meaning clearer. Helping verbs include any form of to be. Here are some examples: do, does, did, have, has, had, shall, should, will, would, can, could, may, might, must. Verb phrases are made of one main verb and one or more helping verbs. Identify each of the verbs in the following sentences. Remember to look for action verbs, linking verbs, and helping verbs. One more time, with gusto! Underline the verbs in each of these sentences. Let's talk about how to add and subtract numbers with "negative squares." You may have heard of positive numbers like 1, 4, or 9, which are just the squares of 1, 2, and 3 respectively. But did you know there are also negative squares? These are numbers like "-1", "-4", and "-9", which are really just the squares of "i", "2i", and "3i". ("i" is the imaginary unit, which means i * i = -1.) Now, when you add or subtract these kinds of numbers, something interesting happens. For example, take a look at this: ```makefile (2 + 3i) + (4 - 5i) = 6 - 2i ``` Did you notice how the real parts (the numbers without the "i") were added together separately from the imaginary parts (the numbers with the "i")? This works because adding or subtracting numbers with "imaginary squares" is just like adding or subtracting regular numbers! But now let's try something a little different. What if we want to find the square root of a number with an "imaginary square"? For example, what is the square root of "-4"? Well, since (-4) is the same as "(2i)\*(2i)", its square root should be "+2i" or "-2i". And sure enough, both of those answers work: ```makefile (+2i)*(+2i) = -4 (-2i)*(-2i) = -4 ``` Pretty cool, huh? Just remember, whenever you're working with numbers that have "imaginary squares," make sure to keep track of the real and imaginary parts separately. That way, you won't get confused by the minuses inside the squares! an an an an an an an an a. Yep there's a little bit Chi [nickname]. "Where is--he?" he asked hoarsely. "Do you mean Mr. Marx?" "Do you want money?" A vowel is a sound where air coming from the lungs is not blocked by the mouth or throat. All normal English words contain at least one vowel. Vowels have two sounds: A short sound and a long sound. A, E, I, O, U and Y are the English vowels, although 'Y' can also behave as a consonant when it is at the beginning of a word. Long vowel includes those vowel sounds whose pronunciation are the same as its letter name. A, E, I, O and U are the long vowel sounds. When a short word or syllable ends with a vowel-consonant-e combination (a-k-e), the vowel is usually long and the "e" at the end of the word is silent. However, this rule does not apply in all cases. 1. Bake: When 'A' is followed by 'E' and a single sound is heard i.e. A. 2. Ride: Here 'I' is followed by 'E' and a single sound is heard i.e. I. There is a rule for pronunciation of long vowels. 'When two vowels go walking the first one does the talking.' 1. Long A: A as in mail. Here, the sound of A is heard. 2. Long E: E as in tea. Here, the sound of E is heard. 3. Long I: I as in kite. Here, the sound of I is heard. 4. Long O: O as in road. Here, the sound of O is heard. 5. Long U: U as in glue. Here, the sound of U is heard. Title: How to Check Your iPhone's RF Exposure Introduction: Radio Frequency (RF) exposure is an essential concern for many people who use smartphones regularly. This tutorial will guide you through checking your iPhone's RF exposure, which refers to the amount of radiofrequency energy that your device emits when connected to wireless networks or cellular services. By following these steps, you can understand how much RF radiation your iPhone produces and learn ways to minimize exposure if necessary. Step 1: Understand SAR values Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) is a measure of the rate at which energy from radiofrequency (RF) waves is absorbed by body tissues. It is usually expressed in units of watts per kilogram (W/kg). Every iPhone has a maximum SAR value declared by Apple during its testing phase. You must familiarize yourself with this concept before diving into checking your iPhone's RF exposure. Key Tip: Keep in mind that while lower SAR values are preferable, they do not directly correlate with safety since other factors also contribute to potential health risks associated with RF exposure. Step 2: Access your iPhone's SAR information To find your specific iPhone model's SAR values, follow these sub-steps: a) Open the 'Settings' app on your home screen. b) Scroll down and select 'General.' c) Tap on 'About.' d) Locate 'Legal & Regulatory' and tap on it. e) Look for 'RF Exposure' or 'SAR' and tap on it. f) Here, you will see two SAR values - one for head usage and another for body usage. Make sure both are within the acceptable limits set by regulatory bodies such as the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) in the United States. By accessing this information, you become aware of the RF emission levels of your particular iPhone model. Step 3: Learn about exposure reduction measures Although SAR values provide some insight into RF exposure, there are additional steps you can take to reduce overall exposure further. Consider implementing these practices: a) Use hands-free options like earbuds or speakerphone mode whenever possible to keep the phone away from your head. b) Limit calls in areas with weak signals, as phones emit more radiation when struggling to connect to networks. c) Send texts instead of making voice calls when feasible. d) Avoid carrying your iPhone close to sensitive body parts like reproductive organs for extended periods. Implementing these measures allows you to proactively manage your RF exposure beyond simply understanding SAR values. Step 4: Regularly check for software updates Apple continually works on improving their devices and minimizing RF emissions. Therefore, keeping your iPhone updated to the latest iOS version ensures that any available reductions in RF output are applied. a) Go back to the main 'Settings' menu. b) Select 'General.' c) Tap on 'Software Update.' d) Install any pending updates. Conclusion: By completing this tutorial, you have learned how to check your iPhone's RF exposure by finding and interpreting its SAR values, implemented exposure reduction strategies, and ensured regular software updates. Although no definitive evidence links low-level RF exposure to adverse health effects, taking precautions remains prudent given ongoing research and concerns surrounding this topic. Title: How to Comment HTML Code Step 1: Understand What Comments Are In computer programming, comments are non-executable lines of text included in the code to explain its purpose or functionality. They serve as documentation and help others understand your code better. In HTML, commenting is used to add notes or temporarily disable sections of code without deleting them. Key Tip: Always comment your HTML code! It makes debugging easier and allows other developers to quickly grasp what you've done. Step 2: Learn the Syntax HTML uses the syntax `<!-- Your Comment Here -->` for adding comments. Anything placed between these tags will be ignored by web browsers when rendering the page. Step 3: Add Single Line Comments Single line comments can be added using two hyphens followed by your note and then closing with another double hyphen. For example: ```html <!DOCTYPE html> <!-- This is a single line comment --> <html lang="en"> ... </html> ``` This type of comment is useful for brief annotations about specific parts of your markup. Step 4: Create Multi-Line Comments For longer comments that span multiple lines, use the opening tag (`<!--`) before the first line and close it after the last line (-->). Like so: ```html <!DOCTYPE html> <!-- This is a multi-line comment block. It can contain several sentences or even paragraphs if needed. Just keep everything within the start and end tags. --> <html lang="en"> ... </html> ``` These blocks are ideal for lengthy descriptions or complex sections of code. Step 5: Use Comments for Debugging Comments can also be used to hide problematic areas while troubleshooting. If you suspect some elements might be causing issues, simply wrap them in comment tags: ```html <div id="problematicElement"> <!-- Problem here? Let's see! --> <p>Some content.</p> </div> ``` Once identified and fixed, remove the comment tags to reactivate the element. Step 6: Avoid Confusing Content with Comments Never insert actual content inside comment tags, assuming it won't appear on the live site. Search engines may still index this 'invisible' information, potentially harming SEO rankings. Also, users who know how to view source could read these hidden texts. Guideline: Only use comments for their intended purpose - explaining and documenting your code. Step 7: Keep Your Comments Updated As projects evolve, old comments become outdated. Regularly review and update your comments to ensure they accurately reflect the current state of your codebase. Stale comments lead to confusion and misinterpretation. By following these steps, you'll write cleaner, more maintainable HTML code, making life easier for yourself and any collaborators working on your project. Happy coding! DOWNLOAD AND PRINT THIS OUT (also see our hundreds of online sets in our Quizlet Group!) WHY? Games are fun and motivational. They increase student talk time (production). Visuals add context to the language learning. They are quick and easy to use. They allow for repetition and proper scaffolding of the learning. WHERE? Flashcards are now freely available all over the internet. Just download and print. (see the sites I've listed below or visit EFL Classroom's sharing area). Vocabulary power points can easily be printed [File - Print Preview - "x" slides/page - Print] and cut up by students for use. If you have the time, print an empty BINGO card and have the students draw/label their own cards! You can also just photocopy from books then cut these pictures up. WHICH? Use flashcards in the L2! Either without a label or with a label on the back. If you print and they don't have labels - get your students to label them! WHEN? Anytime! They can be used to assess student ability/knowledge. As a review or formative tool. As an "study" part of the lesson to practice language in a controlled fashion. Or just as a fun, "activating" part of a lesson. TIPS? ** Laminate your "good" card sets. ** Keep in labeled envelops or zip lock bags. ** Set up your classroom so it is easy for students to be grouped or spread out. ** Have a strict set of signs and signals to manage students. ** Model the game in front of the whole class with a few students. Go slow! ** Get students to invent their own games! HOW? There are literally a zillion ways to use flashcards to teach. Here are a few of them lumped into some main categories. This is probably the most simple and repetitive type of game. There are many variations. a) Recall. One student shows the student(s) a flashcard and the others try to make a sentence or simply state the name of the card. The student "teacher" checks on the back label and if correct puts in a "correct" pile. If incorrect, it is set aside for review later. b) Memory Story. One student picks up a card and makes a statement using some target language. For example. "Yesterday I went shopping and bought .." The next student turns up a card and continues. "Yesterday I went shopping and bought a and a " c) Concentration. There are two sets of flashcards. Either picture/picture or picture / word. Students attempt to turn over and match from both sets. If they do so, they may continue. The student with the most pairs at the end, wins. Here's an online example. http://tiny.cc/MZwhY Many like this on our Game page. d) Snap. The cards are face up on the table. The teacher says a sentence and the first student to "snap" or slap the right card gets to keep it. The most cards at the end, wins. You can play this full class by putting large flashcards on the board and having students run up to the blackboard and slap the correct flashcard. These games use some target language which the teacher models and puts in the board. Students play the game using the flashcards and the designated language structure. Many times, the language can be a closed question / answer (ex. Did you .. last night? - Yes, I did / No, I didn't) Some example grammar structures: Do you like / Do you usually .. / Do you have . Did you yesterday? Have you ever ? Will you tomorrow? a) Go Fish. Use two sets. Students in groups of 3 or 4 ask each other questions using the target language. If the student asked has that card, they must give it and that student collects a pair. They may continue until they don't collect. If a student runs out of cards, they pick up some more cards to continue. Most pairs at the end wins. b) Survey. Give each student a card. They survey others around the class using their vocabulary flashcard. Ex. Have you ever ..? Report back to the group or the class using the structure - Some of us c) Conversation prompt. There is one pile of cards in the middle. One student turns over a card and using the flashcard as a prompt - asks another student a question. That student responds with one sentence. The other students in the group must ask one follow up question each. Some example prompts: When did you last ? / Have you ever .? / Do you prefer . Or .? If someone , what would you do? / What do you think about ? d) Find your match! Copy some flashcards in different numbers. 6 of one, 5 of one, 4 of one 3 of one etc Give one card to each student. Using the target language on the board, the students must go around the classroom and find other students with a similar card. If they find a match, they link arms and continue trying to find others with a similar card. (Ex. " I love . What about you? " "Oh, I love . Bye." ) Alternatively - students can choose their own response from the items brainstormed on the blackboard. Have them use a post it to create their own flashcard! e) The "You Can" Game. A person in a group turns over a flashcard. They must make a statement/sentence using "can". Ex. You can .. or An / A . can .. Continue around the group awarding a point for each statement. f) Guessing Game. Create some sets of flashcards with common people / places / things. One learner picks up a flashcard and describes it using It is a thing that . It is a place where . It is a person who . The first student to correctly guess it, wins the card. Most cards when time is up, wins. 3. CLASSROOM ACTION GAMES These games use flashcards to get students moving around the classroom and competing. a) Charades. Bring a student to the front of the class. Show them a flashcard. They must act out the object / action. The other students guess or a team guesses as many as possible in one minute. The next team tries to beat them. Make sure they use full sentences! (ex. He is an elephant!) . After a whole class demonstration, get students doing this in small groups. b) Pictionary. Same as charades only this time instead of acting out the flashcard, they must draw it. You can also have multiple teams drawing on the board - first team to guess the correct word wins. c) Telephone Whisper. Put your students in rows. Show the last person in the row a flashcard. They must make a sentence with an adjective (ex. It is a huge elephant) and whisper it to the next person. Continue until it gets to the front of the class. That student writes the phrase on the board. If it is a the same as the flashcard - a point is awarded. 4. OPEN ENDED GAMES These games are for higher level students and give them an opportunity to use language in a meaningful and fuller manner. a) Story Dominoes. One stack of cards in the middle. A student picks up a card and starts a story, stating one sentence. The next student picks up a card and continues the story. And so on until the last card when the story concludes. Start again and see if the group can recall and repeat the story! b) Liar, Liar pants on Fire! Each student in the group gets 3 flashcards. They must make a statement about each flashcard and their life. One must be a lie. The other students guess which one is the lie. Ex. (for animals): I like crocodiles. / I have seen a giraffe in real life. / I have kissed a dog . Which is the lie? c) Making Connections. Use two sets of assorted flashcards. Spread them out into two groups. Students must make a sentence using a flashcard from both sides. Ex. An elephant (a) doesn't like to swim(b). Continue until all cards are matched. d) Not like the others. Use a set of assorted flashcards. Students turn over 4 cards and put them face up in the middle. Students must make statements why one is different from the others using the phrase, A/An . is not like the others because .. A point awarded for all logical answers. When students run out of statements, start with a new set of four flashcards. e) Discussions. Students turn over a card and state their opinion about the topic of the card. Go around the group with each student stating their opinion. Discuss. WEB BASED FLASHCARD RESOURCES Here's another teacher's nice list of flashcard games/activities (but more for an L1 class). Hello young mathematicians! Today, let's learn about a fun concept called "generating functions." Don't worry, it's not as complicated as it sounds! We're going to explore how generating functions can help us discover patterns in sequences of numbers. Imagine you have a bunch of number cards neatly lined up like this: 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, ... Do you recognize this sequence? It's the famous Fibonacci sequence! Each number is the sum of the previous two numbers (e.g., 1 + 2 = 3, 2 + 3 = 5, etc.). Now, suppose your teacher asks you to find the pattern or rule for the **even-indexed** Fibonacci numbers (the numbers located in the even positions of our list). These would be: 0, 2, 10, 44, ... Quite interesting, huh? Let's dive into finding out more with generating functions! First, we need to understand that a generating function is simply a way to represent a sequence of numbers using polynomial or power series. In our case, the Fibonacci sequence has the generating function represented by $\frac{x}{1 - x - x^2}$, which means each term in the sequence corresponds to a coefficient when expanding this expression. Our challenge now is to figure out the generating function just for those even-indexed Fibonacci numbers (0, 2, 10, 44, ...). The hint provided suggests looking at the average between $f(x)$, representing all Fibonacci numbers, and another special version of $f(x)$, where every term is multiplied by $(-1)^n$. Let me illustrate this idea step-by-step: 1. Write down both expressions: * Original Fibonacci generating function: $f(x)=\sum_{n\ge0}c\_nx^n=\frac{x}{1-x-x^2}$ * A modified version: $f(-x)=\sum_{n\ge0}(-1)^nc\_nx^n$ 2. Calculate their average by adding them together and dividing by 2: $$\frac{1}{2}[f(x)+f(-x)]$$ 3. Observe that many terms will cancel out due to the $(-1)^n$: * When n is odd, $(-1)^n=-1$; thus, these terms vanish since $-c\_n+c\_n=0$. * Only even values of n remain; hence, we get the generating function for even-indexed Fibonacci numbers. By doing this, we should end up with a new generating function containing only information about the even-indexed Fibonacci numbers! How cool is that?! While this explanation may seem complex, remember that generating functions open doors to understanding intricate patterns within sequences of numbers. Keep practicing, and soon enough, you too can uncover secrets hidden within sequences! Happy math adventures! He knows cheese, but he usually eats it in stick form. Line dancing is a form of group dancing that is done to either country or hip-hop music. The term line dance is derived from the idea that the group of people participating in the dance are all facing the same direction when conducting the series of moves in the line dance. A line dance can go from 32 beats upwards of up to 92 beats per minutes, and can also be either a 4 or 2 wall dance. When the dance starts off everyone is facing the front wall, after all of the counts of the dance are complete (let's say we were doing a 32 count dance) then everyone would finish the dance facing either the back wall or the left or right wall. One example of a line dance is the "Men In Black" line dance. This dance is usually done to a hip-hop song, such as "Yeah 3X", by Chris Brown. Once the first 32 counts of the dance are completed everyone will finish the dance facing the left wall, and would have turned one quarter to their left counter clockwise. Once the series of moves is completed 4 times the group will end up facing the front wall again. No, we ate those for dinner on your birthday, what are they? THE KNOWLEDGE TOOLBOX How to ask your horse to Squeeze the horse gently with your legs and once the horse starts walking, they should be relaxed. As the horse walks, you should feel side-to-side sway. While walking, the horse's head will nod forwards and backwards. Keep your arms relaxed, so your hands can move with the nodding and maintain some tightness on the horse. The walk is a "four-step gait," which means the horse moves each leg in turn and places all four legs on the ground individually. The same principles of walking apply to the trot where you squeeze the horse with your legs, but with greater strength this time. A gentle kick with the heels may also be required. Unlike the walk, the horse doesn't nod, so you an keep your hands in the same position without restricting its movement. There are two forms of the trot: rising and sitting. The rising trot has the same rhythm as the horse's legs. You sit as one pair of legs hit the ground and you rise when the other pair hit the ground - like a rhythmic standing and sitting exercise. For the sitting trot, you obviously sit, but you must keep your legs and back relaxed as a tense body may send a message to the horse to trot faster. The sitting trot is considered more difficult to learn of the two. The trot is a "two step gait," which means the horse moves its legs in pairs. Actually, horses moves their legs in diagonal pairs - left front and right back together, while right front and left back move together. To turn a horse, you must use the reins and legs. To turn a horse to the left, gently pull back on the left rein and ask the horse to bend its neck to the left. Use your legs at the same time to encourage the horse forwards. Do the same if you want to turn right, but on the opposite side. To halt a horse, you must a combination of legs, hand and the seat. When the horse is walking, stiffen your lower back and squeeze your legs, while stiffening the hands and gently pulling back the reins towards the body. As soon as the horse has halted, relax. received from Equine "Raw" material is materials such as ores which we can clean and mix with other materials to make another material like steel, for example. Cotton is a raw material used to make textiles, which are materials to make clothes. You don't want to hear what I think of it. Once upon a time, there was a girl named Ali. She was just 3 years old. Can day should go down that Massachusetts avenue? Hello young scholars! Today, let's talk about something exciting - improving your thinking skills through fun daily challenges! Just like how practicing math problems every day helps you get better at math, there are ways to exercise your brain and make it stronger too. Imagine receiving a question in your mailbox every day, but instead of just answering it, you try to figure out the answer all by yourself. This activity is called "GMAT Question of the Day," and it's designed to challenge people who want to improve their problem-solving abilities. Although GMAT is typically for older students aiming for college, we can still learn valuable skills from it! Here's a simple example: Question: If Sally has 5 apples and she gives 2 apples to her friend, how many apples does Sally have left? To solve this, follow these steps: 1. Read the problem carefully and understand what's being asked. In this case, we need to find out how many apples Sally has after giving some away. 2. Break down the problem into smaller parts. First, let's think about how many apples Sally starts with (5) and then subtract the number she gave away (2). 3. Use basic operations to find the solution. Subtraction in this case: 5 (apples Sally has initially) - 2 (apples Sally gives away) = 3. And there you have it! With a little thought and practice, you found the answer. Keep doing this regularly, and soon enough, you'll notice improvements not just in math but also other subjects requiring critical thinking. Plus, it's really cool to receive a challenging question in your mailbox each day! So, keep those brains active, and remember: Practice makes progress! I'll cut it and give it back. Each worksheet should consist of: ...Two ID cards that contain a person's: ...And two blank ID cards. Write your name, age, marital status, job, country, address and phone number on the board. Elicit the questions from the students, such as: 'Where do I live?', 'What is my phone number? Get the students to do the same activity in pairs. Write a famous person's information on the board. Elicit the changes in the form of the questions (e.g. What's your name? - What's his/her name?) Tell the students they will ask for two people's information. Then divide students into pairs. Give one student Worksheet A - which has four ID cards on it. Two of the ID cards are totally filled out. Two of the ID cards just have people's names. Give the other student Worksheet B - this will also have four ID cards, including the details which Student A needs to fill in the blanks on their worksheet and vice versa. Give them a minute to look at the information and ask questions about information they don't understand. I mean it's okay if you don't know. Title: How to Enable Auto-Correction on an iPhone Auto-correction is a handy feature on iPhones that automatically corrects spelling mistakes as you type. This can help save time and improve the accuracy of your messages and other text inputs. Here's a step-by-step guide on enabling auto-correction on your iPhone. **Step 1:** Unlock your iPhone and open the **Settings** app. The Settings app has a gray icon with gears and is typically found on the home screen or in the app library. *Unlocking your phone before opening Settings ensures that you have easy access to all settings without any restrictions.* **Step 2:** Scroll down and tap on **General**. General contains various device settings such as keyboard preferences, language & region, software updates, etc. *This step takes you closer to finding the specific setting related to the keyboard function.* **Step 3:** Tap on **Keyboard**, which should be around midway through the list under the 'PERSONALIZATION' section. *The Keyboard option houses all customizable features related to typing input including predictive text, spell checking, shortcuts, and more.* **Step 4:** Locate the toggle switch labeled **Auto-Correction** near the top of the page. If this feature isn't currently enabled, the switch will appear white and positioned to the left (off). To turn it on, simply slide the switch to the right until it turns green. *Enabling Auto-Correction here means that whenever you make a typo while typing, iOS will suggest corrections based on contextual information gathered from dictionaries and your personal usage patterns.* **Key Tips and Guidelines:** - Remember that sometimes, especially when using proper nouns or uncommon words, auto-correct might get things wrong. However, you can always undo its changes by tapping the suggested word above the virtual keyboard or shaking your device and selecting 'Undo Typing'. - For those who prefer having control over their typos but still want some assistance, consider turning on only **Check Spelling**. It puts a red line beneath incorrectly spelled words, allowing you to manually choose whether to correct them or leave them as they are. - There's also an option called **Predictive Text** just below Auto-Correction. Enabling Predictive Text gives you word suggestions even before you start typing, making your typing faster and easier. - Lastly, if English isn't your native language or you frequently communicate in multiple languages, enable **Multilingual Keyboard Support** further down the same menu. With this feature turned on, your iPhone becomes smarter at detecting which language you're using and adjusts accordingly. His clothing, however, betrayed him, for there was no mistaking it. He will ride on XXX to somebody else's house = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Soderstrom/The/000629a.cha = = = February is also the only month of the calendar that, once every six years and twice every 11 years consecutively, either back into the past or forward into the future, will have four full 7-day weeks. In countries that start their week on a Monday, it occurs as part of a common year starting on Friday, in which February 1st is a Monday and the 28th is a Sunday, this was observed in 2010 and can be traced back 11 years to 1999, 6 years back to 1993, 11 years back to 1982, 11 years back to 1971 and 6 years back to 1965, and will be observed in 2021. In countries that start their week on a Sunday, it occurs in a common year starting on Thursday, with the next occurrence in 2026, and previous occurrences in 2015 (11 years earlier than 2026), 2009 (6 years earlier than 2015), 1998 (11 years earlier than 2009) and 1987 (11 years earlier than 1998). This works unless the pattern is broken by a skipped leap year, but no leap year has been skipped since 1900 and no others will be skipped until 2100. He already said I could have it. That's not how I had it at all. 3. Graphs of y = a sin(bx + c) and y = a cos(bx + c) by M. Bourne In this section, we meet the following 2 graph types: y = a sin(bx + c) y = a cos(bx + c) Both b and c in these graphs affect the phase shift (or displacement), given by: The phase shift is the amount that the curve is moved in a horizontal direction from its normal position. The displacement will be to the left if the phase shift is negative, and to the right if the phase shift is positive. There is nothing magic about this formula. We are just solving the expression in brackets for zero; `bx + c = 0`. Need Graph Paper? Sketch the curve y = sin(2x + 1) In this Flash interactive, drag the curve left or right using the arrows. Observe the "c" and "displacement" values and how they change when you move the curve. The period here is `(2 )/ = 2`. Phase Angle and Phase Shift Phase angle is not always defined the same as phase shift. The phase angle for the sine curve y = a sin(bx + c) is usually taken to be the value of c and the phase shift is usually given by `-c/b`, as we saw above. Reminder: In the last section, we saw how to express sine curves in terms of frequency. Example: Electronics engineers separate the terms "phase angle" and "phase shift", and they use a mix of radians and degrees. We may have a current expressed as follows: I = 50 sin (2 (100)t + 30 ) This means the amplitude is `50\ "A"`, the frequency is `100\ "Hz"` and the phase angle is`30^@`. See an application of phase angle at An Application to AC Circuits in the complex numbers chapter. Also, see a discussion on this issue at Phase shift or phase angle? in the math blog. To keep things simple for now, we will mostly use the term phase shift in this chapter. Sine Graph Java Applet - Phase shift The following applet will help you to understand the following graph sketching exercise. In this Java applet, you can vary each of the amplitude, period and phase shift by using the sliders at the bottom. You can also change the function to whatever you like. Try changing it to a*cos(b*x+c) and then vary the values of a, b or c with the sliders. Sketch the graph of the following. 1. `y = sin(2x + /6)` 2. `y = 3\ sin(x + /4)` 3. `y = 2\ cos(x - /8)` 4. `y = -cos(2x - )` So, you think you've got the idea? How To Make A Graph In Excel If you need to know how to make a graph in Excel, you are probably trapped in a job full of drudgery. Maybe you are not. You need to decide for yourself if you have an exciting job. Microsoft makes the process of creating graphs easy for the user, if not particularly exciting. Follow the steps below to make a graph of the type you want. Excel lets the user make pie charts, bar graphs and line graphs from the options within the program. Things You Will Need to Make Graphs in Excel - An Excel Spreadsheet - A rough idea of the overall design - Basic Knowledge of Excel How to Make Graphs in Excel: - Start by labeling each line in the graph. Copy and paste all of the cells in the table. Repeat the process for each cell. Most people already have the data entered before they get to this point, but if you do not, now god time to enter it. - Insert each item into the table individually using the Graph Wizard. You can find the graph wizard from the insert tab in Excel 2007 and Excel 2010. Earlier versions of the program had it under the Insert pull down menu. - Select the type of graph you want to use. The type of graph you use depends on the data a person wants to show. If you want to show percentages can use a pie graph. If you are comparing overall totals, use a bar graph. A person wanting to track changes over time should use the line graph option. - Check the graph for accuracy. Excel lets you edit the charts you make with the program. Correct any spelling errors you find. Make sure the data you use represents the concepts you want to convey. The data entered into the spreadsheet should be checked before th graph is even made. - Save the spreadsheet and save the graph. You can print out the graph. Printouts make nice visual aids for a person giving a presentation. What are you going to do with the pail and shovel? Here let me pour you some more. So too he hoped, was the Blue Moon. Title: How to Apply Sunscreen Spray Sunscreen spray is an easy and convenient way to protect your skin from harmful UV rays. It's quick-drying, lightweight, and less greasy compared to lotion sunscreens. This tutorial will guide you through the process of applying sunscreen spray effectively while providing useful tips and guidelines along the way. **Things You'll Need:** 1. A bottle of broad-spectrum sunscreen spray with SPF 30 or higher. 2. A mirror (optional but recommended). ### Step 1: Choose the Right Product Before purchasing sunscreen spray, ensure that it offers broad-spectrum protection against both UVA and UVB rays and has an SPF rating of at least 30. Also, consider whether you need waterproof or sweat-resistant formulas depending on your activities. ### Step 2: Shake Well Shake the bottle well before every application to mix any separated ingredients back together ensuring even distribution of active ingredients across your skin. ### Step 3: Find Proper Lighting Find a place where there's plenty of natural light so you can see all areas of your body clearly when applying the sunscreen. Using a mirror can also be helpful especially if you are alone. ### Step 4: Hold the Can Correctly Hold the can upright about six inches away from your skin. This distance allows the particles to disperse correctly without clumping. ### Step 5: Apply Generously Spray liberally onto exposed skin. Don't forget often overlooked spots like ears, tops of feet, backs of knees, and hands. For hard-to-reach places such as your back, ask someone else for assistance or use a spray designed for this purpose. ### Step 6: Rub It In Gently rub the sunscreen into your skin after spraying until it disappears. The goal is to create a thin, uniform layer over your entire body. Avoid inhaling the mist directly; instead, wait until it lands on your skin before rubbing it in. ### Step 7: Wait Before Dressing Up Allow the sunscreen to dry completely before dressing up to avoid staining clothes. Most sprays only take a few seconds to absorb fully. ### Step 8: Reapply Regularly Reapply every two hours, or immediately after swimming or sweating heavily. Even 'water resistant' sunscreens lose effectiveness over time due to absorption, perspiration, and towel drying. ### Key Tips & Guidelines: - Always apply sunscreen generously. Less isn't more here - missing small patches could lead to serious sunburns. - Be mindful of wind direction while spraying to prevent wasting product and to make sure you cover all necessary areas. - If possible, stay out of direct sunlight during peak hours (between 10 am and 4 pm) regardless of sunscreen usage. Seek shade whenever feasible. - Use sunscreen year-round, not just during summer months. Snow, sand, and other reflective surfaces increase exposure to UV radiation even in colder weather. - Check expiry dates regularly since most sunscreens last only three years post manufacture date. Discard old bottles and replace them promptly. By following these steps meticulously, you're one step closer to maintaining healthy, youthful looking skin! Why can't we do it right now? Cognitive symptoms are problems with attention, certain types of memory, the concept of time, and with the ability to plan and organize. Cognitive deficits caused by schizophrenia can also be difficult to recognize as part of the disorder. They are the most disabling of the symptoms because cognitive problems affect everyday functioning. These problems may also be side effects of anti-psychotic drugs, antidepressants, sleeping medication and anti-anxiety drugs. In these compounds, it has two normal oxidation states: +1 and +2. +2 is more common. Most +2 copper compounds are blue. +1 copper compounds can be white. Copper compounds are weak oxidizing agents. They corrode many metals. This corrosion takes the metal and puts it in the chemical compound, leaving the copper behind. An example would be iron and copper(II) sulfate reacting to make copper and iron(II) sulfate. +1 copper compounds are reducing agents when in air. They are normally made by reduction of +2 compounds. Malaria is in these places: Cleaning a laptop screen is an important task that can help maintain your device's appearance and functionality. A dirty or smudged screen can make it difficult to see what you are doing and could potentially damage the display if left unattended. This tutorial will provide a comprehensive guide on how to properly clean a laptop screen using simple household items. **Things You'll Need:** * Microfiber cloth or lint-free coffee filter * Distilled water * Mild dish soap (optional) * Isopropyl alcohol (70% or higher) * Spray bottle ### **Step 1: Gather Your Materials** Before starting the cleaning process, ensure all necessary materials are readily available. Using appropriate tools will minimize any risk of damaging your laptop screen during the cleaning process. ### **Step 2: Turn Off and Unplug Your Laptop** Always turn off and unplug your laptop before beginning the cleaning process. Not only does this prevent accidental input while handling the device, but it also allows you to better identify dirt and grime accumulation on the screen. ### **Step 3: Dust Off The Screen** Use a dry microfiber cloth or a lint-free coffee filter to gently dust off the laptop screen. These materials are preferred as they won't scratch the surface of the display and are effective at picking up loose particles. Avoid using paper towels, tissues, or regular clothes as these may contain small particles that could harm your screen. ### **Step 4: Prepare Your Cleaning Solution** If plain distilled water isn't sufficient to remove stubborn stains, consider adding a few drops of mild dish soap to create a gentle cleaning solution. Mix well until suds form, then transfer into a spray bottle. Alternatively, mix equal parts distilled water and isopropyl alcohol (70% or higher concentration) for tougher messes. However, be aware that excessive usage of alcohol might cause discoloration over time. ### **Step 5: Lightly Mist The Solution Onto The Cloth** Spray a light layer of your chosen solution onto your cleaning cloth. It's crucial not to oversaturate the material; excess moisture could seep through gaps between the keys and damage internal components. Remember, prevention is always better than cure! ### **Step 6: Wipe Down The Screen** Start wiping down the screen with circular motions, applying slight pressure where needed. Be careful around edges and corners to avoid lifting or bending the screen out of its socket. Regularly check your progress by holding the cloth away from the screen to inspect visibility improvements. ### **Step 7: Dry Thoroughly** Once satisfied with the results, allow the screen to air dry completely before turning your laptop back on. To expedite drying, pat the screen lightly with another dry microfiber cloth or coffee filter. Never use heat sources such as hairdryers or radiators to speed up evaporation, as extreme temperatures can warp sensitive electronic components. ## Tips and Guidelines * Always read and adhere to manufacturer recommendations regarding cleaning procedures specific to your device model. * Refrain from pressing too hard when scrubbing, especially near delicate areas like webcams and logos. * If possible, tilt your laptop slightly forward so gravity assists in draining any potential liquid towards the base rather than inside the machine. * Perform routine maintenance checks every week or two depending on frequency of usage and environmental conditions. By following this detailed tutorial, you should now have successfully cleaned your laptop screen without causing any unnecessary damages. Happy computing! Here are some ways to spark your child's interest in learning all summer long: Give your child a journal. Suggest that she write down things she wonders about. For example, "What does it mean when there is a full moon?" or "Why can't I stay up later?" She can find answers on her own or with your help, then write them in the journal. Make time to talk. One study showed that when families talk often about many subjects, kids have higher IQs. Look for opportunities to talk with your child, such as during meals or in the car. Support your child's interests. For example, if your child loves playing the guitar, read books about the instrument, go to a concert, or encourage her to write to a famous guitarist. Try a new activity you can do with your child, such as a sport. Choose something she's excited about. Show how interested you are in learning, improving, and not giving up. Set an example by learning about things you love. If you enjoy cooking, for instance, take a cooking class, check out a cookbook at the library, or try new recipes. Invite your child to help. The two main characters that are inside the letters are Wormwood, a demon that has the job of leading his "patient" away from Christianity (or at least making him a useless Christian). Screwtape is Wormwood's uncle and a "high" demon. Someone asked, "How can you assume that the speed of the raindrop is 8 meters per second?" That number comes from some resesarch we did a few years ago on a squall line (line of cumulonimbus) in the tropics. We were looking at a squall line with Doppler radar. This particular radar "saw" raindrops rather than clouds or air. So our Doppler radar could be used to estimate the raindrop fall speed. In the storm we were interested in, the raindrops in the heavy rain were falling at 8 meters per second. (Horizontally, the raindrops follow the wind pretty well - something you know if you are trying to stay dry in a windy rainstorm. This means that scientists can use Doppler radars to study winds in a storm.) These measurements have been used to check the equations scientists use to predict their speed - basically balancing air resistance with the pull of gravity. The speed of the drop (or droplet) varies with its size. Very small drops, like those in clouds, fall so slowly that the tiniest breath of upward-moving air can keep them aloft. If you fly through a cumulus cloud, it's bumpy - the air is moving up and down. So some droplets go up, and some go down. If they are tiny, the sinking droplets simply evaporate. So the cloud stays up there in the sky. Scientists have also measured raindrop fall speeds with upward-looking radars that can "see" both the drops and air. This way they find out how fast the drops move downward relative to the air. So we know that drizzle drops fall at about 2 meters pers second, and raindrops smaller than ours would fall in between 2 meters per second and 8 meters per second. Why are the speeds different? Drops fall because they are pulled downward by gravity. The drop speed varies with size because of air resistance. Air resistance slows the drops down. If there were no air, drops starting from the same height would fall together at the same speed. This speed would increase with time due to the pull of gravity. With air, the drops quickly reach a speed at which the pull of gravity is balanced by air resistance. You can see this effect with a simple experiment. Take a piece of paper, and squeeze it into a ball. Take a tiny pice of paper (less than 1 cm), squeeze it into a ball. Drop them at the same time. See how long it takes the two pieces to fall to the ground. The tiny piece should fall more slowly. If you make the tiny piece even smaller, it will fall even more slowly. What about snowflakes? They fall very slowly, like downy feathers do when you drop them. Using the paper again, you will find that the paper squeezed into a ball will fall faster than a similar size piece that is not squeezed into a ball. Scientist holding large hailstone. Photograph courtesy of the National Center for Atmospheric Research. What about big hailstones? If you guessed that they would fall faster, you are right. Large hailstones can fall at speeds faster than 30 meters per second! How do scientists know? Again, they can track hail with radar. Or, they can make fake hailstones that have the same shape and density as real hailstones, drop them, and measure the time it takes the stones to fall a certain distance. When I started working at the National Center for Atmospheric Research, one of the scientists was dropping fake hailstones down a five-story stairwell and timing their fall. The hailstones were painted so that the surface looked like a chessboard or checkerboard, so the cameras could see how they tumbled as they fell. This was fun to watch! Through the window, that is. These measurements can then be used to check and refine the equations that describe falling objects. In Japanese, sentences use subject-object-verb (SOV) word order, so the verb is at the end of the sentence and the subject is at the beginning. Many sentences have no subject, and the listener can infer the subject based context and the form of a verb. Paul would you open the door for Mommy please? Once upon a time there was a kind rabbit. He loved to hop around in the sunshine and eat carrots. One day he heard a crunching noise. He looked around and saw a big dog. The dog smiled and said: "Hello, I am hungry. Would you like to share your carrots with me?" The rabbit thought for a minute and then agreed to share. He said: "It's very kind of you to ask." He separated some of his carrots and gave them to the dog. But then the dog took too big a bite out of one of them and it disappeared! The rabbit was surprised, but tried to stay calm. He said: "That wasn't very kind of you. Please only take one bite at a time so that I have enough to share." The dog said sorry and obeyed the rabbit's request. The Rolling Stones are an English rock band that formed in London, England in 1962. The band members were: Mick Jagger (rhythm guitar and vocals), Keith Richards (lead and rhythm guitar and vocals), Brian Jones (lead guitar), Bill Wyman (bass guitar), and Charlie Watts (drums). The band were influenced by American blues and rock musicians like Howlin' Wolf, Chuck Berry, Bo Diddley and Muddy Waters. In the beginning they had their first hits with covers versions of songs of those artists. Along with the Beatles and the Kinks, they helped to lead the British Invasion of the early to mid 1960s. Title: How to Get Rid of Gas in Babies Introduction: As a new parent, you may often find yourself dealing with a gassy baby. This is completely normal as babies have immature digestive systems that can lead to gas build-up causing discomfort and fussiness. Fortunately, there are several ways to help your baby pass gas and relieve their discomfort. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to get rid of gas in babies. Step 1: Recognize the Signs of Gas in Your Baby Before attempting any remedial measures, it's crucial to identify whether your baby is indeed suffering from gas pain. Look out for these signs: - Excessive crying or fussiness after feeding - Tightened stomach muscles - Drawing legs towards the belly - Passing gas frequently but still seeming uncomfortable - Difficulty sleeping or staying asleep Step 2: Burp Your Baby Regularly During Feeding Sessions Burping helps release air trapped in your baby's tummy during feedings. Make sure to burp your little one every few minutes while nursing or bottle-feeding. If they start getting fussy mid-feed, this could be a sign they need to burp. Key Tip: Try different positions like holding them upright over your shoulder, sitting them on your lap facing away from you and patting their back gently, or laying them down on their tummy across your knees. Step 3: Use the Right Bottle if You're Formula Feeding Some bottles can cause babies to swallow more air than others, exacerbating gas problems. Choose anti-colic bottles designed to reduce air intake. These usually feature ventilation systems that allow air to flow into the bottle rather than your baby's mouth. Always ensure the nipple hole size matches your baby's age and sucking strength. Step 4: Massage Your Baby's Belly Gently Massaging your baby's belly can aid digestion and soothe gas pains. Lay your baby down on their back and using gentle circular motions, rub their tummy in clockwise direction (same direction as food moves through the gut). Be careful not to press too hard; just enough pressure should be applied to make the skin move slightly. Step 5: Encourage Movement After Eating Moving around can help your baby expel built-up gas. After feeding, hold your baby upright against your chest for about 10-15 minutes before placing them in their crib. Once they're strong enough, try bicycling their legs - move their legs in a cycling motion as though they're riding a bike. This movement can push gas out of their system. Step 6: Consider Probiotics Probiotics contain live bacteria similar to those naturally found in the human body. They promote healthy gut flora which aids digestion and reduces symptoms related to excessive gas. Consult with your pediatrician first regarding dosage and brand recommendations suitable for infants. Step 7: Seek Professional Help When Needed If despite trying all these methods your baby continues to experience severe discomfort, consult your healthcare provider. Persistent crying, vomiting, diarrhea, blood in stools, or failure to gain weight might indicate an underlying medical issue requiring professional attention. Conclusion: Helping your baby deal with gas requires patience and consistency. By following these steps, you'll create a routine that prevents gas build-up and alleviates discomfort when necessary. Remember, what works best will vary between individual babies, so don't hesitate to experiment until you find the right approach for yours. To make sense of voltage, we will need to make an analogy. Lets imagine that electrons are represented by a marble on a flat plane. At this point, the plane is level and the marble does not move. If the plane is lifted at one side, the marble will roll down to the lower point. In electricity, the high point is a point with lots of electrons and the low point is a point with a lack of electrons. The high point is called the high potential and the low point is the low potential. The difference between these two points is called the potential difference. The larger the potential difference, the larger the voltage. Voltage can be thought of as the measure of the pressure pushing the electrons. The higher the pressure, the higher the voltage. Voltage is represented by the letter E. The basic unit of measure is volts or the letter V. One volt will push 1 amp of current through 1 ohm of resistance. Resistance will be discussed in a later section. When you count, remember to point to objects. This gets the concept that numbers actually mean something. Title: How to Download Anime for Free Anime has gained immense popularity worldwide due to its unique storytelling techniques, captivating characters, and stunning animation styles. However, streaming anime online can be inconvenient at times due to internet connectivity issues or geo-restrictions. Therefore, downloading anime episodes for offline viewing is an excellent alternative. This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to download anime for free while ensuring that all steps are legal and ethical. **Disclaimer:** It's crucial to note that unauthorized sharing and downloading copyrighted material without proper licensing constitute piracy which is illegal and punishable by law. Always respect creators' rights and support them by using legitimate sources whenever possible. Step 1: Identify Legal Sources =============== Before proceeding further, familiarize yourself with some reliable websites offering free anime content legally. Some popular options include Crunchyroll, Funimation, VIZ Media, Tubi TV, and Viewster. These platforms offer ad-supported services where users can watch shows for free but may have limitations compared to their premium counterparts (like watching ads or delayed episode releases). Key Tip: Never opt for shady sites promoting 'free' content without any clear indication of legality as they might expose your system to malware or other security risks. Step 2: Create an Account ================= Register on one of these chosen platforms. Registration usually requires providing basic information such as email address, username, and password creation. Having an account enables access to available features like bookmarking series, receiving updates about new episodes etc., making your overall user experience smoother. Step 3: Browse through Catalog ================== Explore the site's library to find the anime show(s) you wish to download. Use filters provided - genre, language, date added, rating, etc.-to narrow down results if necessary. Step 4: Check Availability For Offline Viewing ========================== Not every platform allows direct downloads even from their official apps; therefore, check whether your selected platform provides this feature. If not, consider switching to another service where offline viewability is enabled. Step 5: Initiate The Download Process ===================== Once confirmed that the platform supports offline viewing, look for the download button typically placed near the video player or under the episode description. Clicking it should initiate the download process. Depending upon your network speed, file size, and device specifications, the time taken for completion may vary. Step 6: Manage Downloaded Files =================== After successful download, manage your files wisely. Organize them into folders based on genres, seasons, or alphabetically. Regularly clean up unnecessary data to save storage space. Guideline: Keep track of storage usage since high occupancy could slow down your device performance over time. Step 7: Transferring Downloaded Files To Other Devices ========================================= If planning to move downloaded files onto different devices, ensure compatibility beforehand. Most modern smartphones, tablets, and computers support common formats used in anime distribution like MP4 or MKV. Utilize cloud storage solutions, USB drives, or local WiFi networks for transfer purposes. Remember: Sharing copyrighted content across multiple systems contrary to intended usage terms violates digital right laws. Be cautious when distributing downloaded media beyond personal consumption. By following these steps meticulously, you can enjoy your favorite anime shows anytime, anywhere, without worrying about buffering interruptions or internet availability constraints! Session 5, Part B: Measuring Heights of Tall Objects (35 minutes) Measuring With Shadows What other methods exist for measuring indirectly? One such method is to use shadows. For tall objects that are difficult to measure directly, such as skyscrapers and giant redwood trees, shadows can be very useful, as they lie on the ground and are fairly easy to measure. The "shadow" method also relies on similar triangles. Go outside on a sunny day with a friend, and find a tall lamppost that is casting a shadow. (If you can't find a lamppost, use another tall object.) You need to be able to measure the length of the shadow, so be wary of shadows that fall onto roadways. A large parking lot or field is ideal. Note 4 Measure the length of the shadow of the lamppost. Make sure that the lamppost is on flat, level ground, since the lamppost and the shadow should be perpendicular to each other. Record the measurement. Now measure the shadow your friend casts on level ground. Make a sketch of the lamppost and shadow and label what you know. Also draw a right triangle that shows your friend and his or her shadow as the legs of another triangle. Again, label what you know. Your sketch should look something like this: Why are the two triangles (lamppost/shadow and friend/shadow) similar? Think about the angles in each of the triangles. How were those angles formed? Do the sunbeams form an angle? Also, remember that if two of the angles in both triangles are congruent, the triangles must be similar. Close Tip If you know that the triangles are similar, how can you find the height of the lamppost? Determine the height of the lamppost. Discuss the different proportions that you might use to calculate its height. Note 5 Why is the height of the lamppost a derived measure (i.e., measured indirectly)? Let's assume that your measurements were accurate to the nearest 0.25 m. Use proportions to calculate an upper and lower limit on the height of the lamppost. What do you think is the best value for the height of the lamppost? Imagine that you have a tall tree in your yard that needs to be cut down. You want to make sure that the tree won't hit your house when it falls. How might you approximate the height of the tree? Video Segment In this segment, Katy and Lombi use the method described in this section to find out the height of a tree in the schoolyard. They set up a similar triangle by measuring the length of the shadow of a meterstick. What are some of the advantages and disadvantages of using a shadow to measure lengths? [speaking over switching noise; 4.8 sec silence, switching, silence] Title: How to Blend in With the Boys in Your Class Introduction: Integrating yourself into a group of boys can be challenging but rewarding. By following these steps, you'll learn how to build relationships, understand their interests, and communicate effectively with them. Remember, blending in doesn't mean losing your identity; it's about finding common ground and being respectful of others. Step 1: Understand the Group Dynamics Analyze the social structure of the class. Identify who the leaders are, who they hang out with, and what activities they enjoy. This will give you an idea of where you might fit in. * Key Tip: Observation is crucial here. Spend time observing interactions without inserting yourself until you have a good understanding of the dynamics. Step 2: Find Shared Interests Look for hobbies or topics that both genders share. It could be anything from sports, video games, books, movies, or even science projects. * Guideline: Don't feign interest just for acceptance. Genuine enthusiasm goes a long way in building lasting connections. Step 3: Participate Actively Join in conversations related to shared interests. Ask questions, contribute ideas, and show genuine engagement. * Key Tip: Active listening is vital. Show that you value their opinions by responding thoughtfully instead of zoning out or interrupting. Step 4: Be Respectful Respect their space and individuality. Avoid stereotyping or making assumptions based on gender. * Guideline: Treat everyone equally regardless of gender. If someone isn't interested in typical 'boy things', don't write them off. Instead, try to discover other areas of mutual interest. Step 5: Use Appropriate Language Match their tone and language style but avoid using overly masculine terms if it makes you uncomfortable. Stay true to yourself. * Key Tip: Using slang or jargon associated with their interests (like gaming lingo) can help you connect more closely. But remember not to overdo it. Step 6: Stand Up For Yourself While blending in, maintain your self-respect. Don't let anyone belittle your thoughts or dismiss your contributions. * Guideline: Confidence attracts people. Speak up when necessary and assert your views politely yet firmly. Step 7: Engage in Group Activities Participate in group tasks and projects. Offer help, collaborate, and take initiative when required. * Key Tip: Leadership roles aren't exclusive to any gender. Prove your worth by taking charge when needed and demonstrating leadership skills. Step 8: Maintain Individuality Even as you blend in, hold onto what makes you unique. There's no need to conform completely to fit in. * Guideline: Authenticity resonates with people. Let your personality shine through rather than trying to mimic others. Conclusion: Blending in with the boys in your class involves understanding their dynamics, finding common grounds, participating actively, respecting individuality, using appropriate language, standing up for oneself, engaging in group activities, and maintaining individuality. Patience and persistence are essential throughout this process. Over time, you'll find yourself comfortably integrated within the group. You don't have this at home, do we? Firefox was created in 2002, under the name "Phoenix" by the Mozilla community members who wanted a standalone browser rather than a bundle of different Mozilla applications. Even in its beta phase, Firefox proved to be popular with its testers and was praised for its speed, security, and add-ons compared to Microsoft's then-popular Internet Explorer 6. Firefox was released in November 2004 and was highly successful with 60 million downloads within nine months, marking the first time that Internet Explorer's popularity was being challenged. Firefox is considered the spiritual successor of Netscape Navigator since the Mozilla community was created by Netscape in 1998. Um, I'm going to make mice come out of my head. Sharks can sense a drop of blood from 2.5 miles away. They can detect one part of blood in 100 million parts of water. Sharks are so powerful that their bite can generate a force of up to 6 tons per square inch. Sharks, in their 400 million years on earth, have shown an extraordinary ability to resist cancer and other diseases. This has raised hopes among medical researchers that the oceans' most feared predator might turn out to be the cancer patient's best friend. Investigators continue to study the immune system of sharks to see if it can provide the answer to stopping the spread of cancer in humans. Sharks can live up to 100 years. The biggest shark is the whale shark which can be up to 50 feet (15 m) long. It has approximately 300 rows of teeth, with hundreds of tiny teeth in each row. It's a filter feeder and sieves enormous amounts of plankton to eat through its gills as it swims. It is also the biggest fish in the sea. The second biggest fish and shark is the basking shark which is about 40 feet (12.3 m) long and is another filter feeder. Sharks can generate about six and a half tons per square inch of biting force. Sharks have no bones - a shark's skeleton is made up of cartilage. The biggest meat-eating shark is the Great White which grows to be up to 21 feet (6.4 m) long. The smallest sharks are the Dwarf Lanternfish (6-7 inches), Spined Pygmy Shark (7 inches) and Pygmy Ribbontail Catshark (6-7 inches). The dogfish sharks are named for their tendency to attack their prey as a pack of wild dogs would. The ostrich is often credited with laying the largest eggs, but the largest egg in the world was actually laid by a shark, the whale shark. The egg, 14 inches (36 cm) long, was found in the Gulf of Mexico in 1953. Sharks can go up to at least 6 weeks without feeding. The record for a shark fasting was observed in an aquarium with the Swell Shark, which did not eat for 15 months. Title: How to Draw a Female Body Drawing the human body is a challenging yet rewarding task that requires practice and patience. This tutorial will guide you through the process of drawing a female body, focusing on anatomy, proportions, and details. By following these steps, you'll be able to create a realistic and well-proportioned artwork. **Step 1: Gather your materials** Before starting, ensure you have the necessary tools. When you grow up you're going to be dead XXX. How to Be a Better Partner Being a good partner is essential for maintaining healthy relationships and ensuring mutual happiness and satisfaction. It requires effort, commitment, and a willingness to grow together with your significant other. Here's an extensive guide on how to be a better partner: **Step 1: Communicate Openly and Honestly** Open communication forms the foundation of any successful relationship. Share your thoughts, feelings, and concerns honestly while being receptive to what your partner has to say. This fosters trust, understanding, and emotional intimacy between you two. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Use "I" statements instead of blaming or accusing language (e.g., "I feel unheard when..." rather than "You never listen!"). - Practice active listening by summarizing your partner's points and asking clarifying questions. - Regularly check in with one another about how things are going emotionally and relationally. **Step 2: Show Appreciation and Affection** Express gratitude for your partner's efforts, kindnesses, and contributions to the relationship. Small gestures like hugging, holding hands, leaving love notes, or simply saying "thank you" can make a big difference in strengthening your bond. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Verbalize specific reasons why you appreciate them (e.g., "Thank you for making dinner last night; it was delicious"). - Surprise them occasionally with thoughtful gifts or experiences that show you know and care about their interests. - Schedule regular date nights to keep the romance alive and maintain connection outside daily routines. **Step 3: Support Each Other's Goals and Dreams** Encourage personal growth and development within your partnership. Celebrate achievements, help overcome obstacles, and provide constructive feedback when requested. Jointly set goals for shared dreams as well as individual aspirations. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Discuss career plans, hobbies, and passions regularly to stay updated on each other's progress. - Offer assistance when needed but avoid being overbearing or controlling in your support. - Attend important events such as performances, exhibitions, or presentations to demonstrate interest and pride in your partner's accomplishments. **Step 4: Manage Conflict Responsibly** Disagreements are inevitable in any relationship. However, handling conflicts effectively can lead to deeper understanding and stronger bonds. Focus on resolving issues rather than winning arguments, and always strive for compromise. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Identify triggers and patterns in disagreements to address underlying causes proactively. - Take breaks during heated discussions to cool off and regain perspective. - Apologize sincerely when wrong and forgive readily to prevent grudges from forming. **Step 5: Maintain Individual Identity** While it's crucial to prioritize your relationship, don't lose sight of who you are individually. Pursue personal interests, friendships, and self-care practices to ensure overall well-being and balance within the partnership. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Set aside time for yourself weekly to engage in activities that bring joy and fulfillment. - Cultivate independent thinking and decision-making skills to foster autonomy and confidence. - Encourage your partner to do the same and respect their need for alone time. By following these steps, you will become a more attentive, supportive, and loving partner. Remember that building a strong relationship takes patience, dedication, and continuous effort. Keep working at it, learn from mistakes, and celebrate milestones along the way! Because Indians have feathers around their brains? Taunton is a town in the county of Somerset. You like babies huh we'll start at the beginning. Washington is a state in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States. It is north of Oregon, west of Idaho, east of the Pacific Ocean, and south of British Columbia. (British Columbia is part of Canada.) There are more than 6,000,000 people in Washington. Most live in the western part of Washington, which gets more rain. About a quarter of the people live in the east part, where it gets less rain, and some parts have a desert climate. The largest city on the east part is Spokane, and it is also the second biggest city in the state. The Cascade Mountains go down the middle of the state and break it into two sides. The state's nickname is the "Evergreen State" because it has a lot of pine trees. Washington was the 42nd state to join the United States, on November 11, 1889. It is often called "Washington State" so that it does not get confused with the nation's capital, Washington, D.C. The name "Washington" comes from President George Washington. Title: How to Deactivate a Pinterest Account Pinterest is a popular social media platform that allows users to discover, save, and share ideas and inspirations through virtual pinboards. However, there may come a time when you want to take a break from Pinterest or delete your account permanently. This tutorial will guide you through the process of deactivating your Pinterest account, providing detailed explanations and helpful tips along the way. ### Part 1: Backing Up Your Data (Optional) Before deactivating your Pinterest account, consider backing up your data as this action is irreversible. Although you can reactivate your account within 14 days of deactivation, any pins, boards, or followers gained during that period will be lost upon reactivation. To back up your data: 1. Log into your Pinterest account. 2. Click on your profile picture at the top right corner of the page, then select 'Settings' from the dropdown menu. 3. Under 'Account settings', click on 'Data privacy'. 4. Select 'Download data' and enter your email address. You'll receive an email with a link to download your data archive once it's ready. Key tip: Depending on the size of your content, processing your request might take several hours or even days. Be patient and check your email regularly. By completing these steps, you have ensured that your valuable pins, boards, and other data are saved before deactivating your account. ### Part 2: Deactivating Your Pinterest Account Now that you have backed up your data, let us move forward with deactivating your account: 1. Sign in to your Pinterest account. 2. Click on your profile picture at the top-right corner of the screen, then choose 'Settings' from the dropdown menu. 3. Scroll down to 'Account management' section. Here, you'll find two options: 'Deactivate account' and 'Close account'. Choose 'Deactivate account' if you plan on returning to Pinterest later; otherwise, select 'Close account'. Keep in mind that choosing 'Close account' means all your data will be deleted forever. * If selecting 'Deactivate account': A popup window will appear asking you to provide feedback about why you're leaving. While optional, sharing your thoughts could help improve Pinterest's services. Afterward, confirm your decision by clicking 'Deactivate my account'. * If selecting 'Close account': Confirm your choice by entering your password and clicking 'Send email to close account'. An email containing a verification link will be sent to your registered email address. Follow the provided instructions to complete the deletion process. Key tip: Make sure you don't log in again after initiating either option since doing so will cancel the deactivation/deletion process. Congratulations! By following these simple yet thorough steps, you have successfully deactivated your Pinterest account. Remember that if you chose to deactivate, you can always reactivate your account within 14 days by logging back in. Now you're going to cry 'cause you don't like your shirt off, huh. Title: How to Get Back to a School Time Schedule Introduction: Transitioning from summer vacation to a school time schedule can be challenging for both children and parents. It requires adjustments in sleep patterns, meal times, and daily routines. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to get back to a school time schedule, ensuring that your family is well-prepared for the new academic year. Step 1: Gradually Adjust Bedtimes Start by shifting bedtime earlier by 15 minutes every three days, about two weeks before school starts. This gradual change allows your child's body clock to adjust slowly, making the transition less disruptive. For example, if your child usually goes to bed at 9 pm during the summer, aim for an 8:45 pm bedtime in the first few nights, then 8:30 pm, and so on until you reach the desired school night bedtime. Key Tip: Maintain consistency. Make sure your child goes to bed at the same time every night, even on weekends, during this adjustment period. Step 2: Establish a Relaxing Bedtime Routine A consistent bedtime routine signals to your child that it's time to wind down and go to sleep. This could include activities like taking a warm bath, reading a book, or listening to calming music. Avoid screens for at least an hour before bedtime as they emit blue light which can interfere with sleep. Guideline: The entire bedtime routine should take around 30 minutes to complete. Step 3: Encourage Regular Exercise During the Day Physical activity can help regulate sleep patterns. Encourage your child to engage in some form of exercise during the day, but avoid vigorous activities close to bedtime as they might have the opposite effect and keep your child awake. Key Tip: Find a balance between physical activity and rest. Too much exertion can lead to overtiredness, making it harder for your child to fall asleep. Step 4: Adjust Meal Times Just like with bedtimes, meals should also be shifted to align with the school schedule. Serve dinner at a reasonable hour - ideally, at least two hours before bedtime - to give your child enough time to digest their food. Also, consider introducing breakfast into your morning routine if it isn't already part of it. Step 5: Reintroduce Morning Alarms About a week before school begins, start setting alarms to mimic the school wake-up time. Like with bedtimes, do this gradually, waking up ten minutes earlier each day until you reach the actual school wake-up time. Guideline: Place the alarm clock out of reach to prevent your child from hitting snooze and going back to sleep. Step 6: Plan Ahead for School Mornings Prepare as much as possible the night before to reduce stress in the mornings. Lay out clothes, pack lunches, gather homework, etc., so everything is ready when you wake up. Key Tip: Consider using a checklist to ensure nothing gets forgotten amidst the morning rush. Step 7: Practice Patience and Flexibility Remember, changes may not happen overnight. Be patient with yourself and your child throughout this process. If things don't go according to plan one day, don't worry; simply return to your scheduled routine the next day. Conclusion: Adjusting to a school time schedule takes preparation and patience. By following these steps, you can ease your family back into a structured routine, ensuring everyone is rested, fed, and organized for the upcoming school year. And did you read books in here? Species that lack lungs respire through gills. In most cases, these are external gills, visible as tufts on either side of the head, although the amphiumas have internal gills and gill slits. Some salamanders that are terrestrial have lungs that are used in respiration, although these are simple and sac-like, unlike the more complex organs found in mammals.
