Be Kind to Haters: A Comprehensive Guide ========================================= Introduction ------------ In today's interconnected world, interactions with people from all walks of life are inevitable. Unfortunately, this also means encountering individuals who may express negativity, criticism, or hostility towards you - often referred to as 'haters.' It is essential to respond to such situations with kindness and understanding rather than resorting to anger or defensiveness. This comprehensive guide will provide detailed steps on how to be kind to haters while protecting your mental well-being and promoting positive communication. ### Step 1: Understand the Reasons Behind Hatred ---------------------------------------------- #### Explanation Hatred rarely stems from a place of contentment or happiness. People who lash out at others typically do so because they are struggling with their issues, which can include jealousy, low self-esteem, fear, or frustration. By recognizing that the hater's behavior likely reflects their internal struggles, you can empathize with them instead of taking their words personally. #### Key Tips & Guidelines * Remember that the hater's actions say more about them than they do about you. * Avoid assuming malicious intent without understanding the context or underlying reasons. ### Step 2: Maintain Perspective ----------------------------- #### Explanation It is crucial to keep things in perspective when dealing with haters. One negative comment should not overshadow the numerous positive interactions you have had with other people. Moreover, consider whether the hater's opinion holds any weight or validity in your life before deciding how to react. #### Key Tips & Guidelines * Focus on the broader picture of your relationships and achievements. * Evaluate if the hater has any expertise or credibility related to their critique. ### Step 3: Respond Instead of Reacting ------------------------------------- #### Explanation Taking time to process a situation before responding allows you to think clearly and act calmly. Responding thoughtfully demonstrates maturity and composure, reducing the likelihood of escalating conflict further. #### Key Tips & Guidelines * Take deep breaths and count to ten if necessary. * Consider writing down your thoughts before sharing them verbally or in writing. ### Step 4: Practice Active Listening ----------------------------------- [MOT puts the helicopter on the airport] Participant five zero seven three a c. However the symbol "l" and the number "1" are easily confused. In 1979 the BIPM made an exception for the symbol for the litre. They said that people could use either "L" or "l" as its symbol. Your brother had pizza you didn't have any. If you are sitting in a boat or in St. Nicholas, a sound coming from the north shore will sound louder than the same sound heard by a person on land. Sound can be amplified when it travels over water. The reason is that the water cools the air above its surface, which then slows down the sound waves near the surface. This causes refraction or bending of the sound wave, such that more sound reaches the boat passenger or St Nicholas resident. Sound waves skimming the surface of the water can add to the amplification effect, if the water is calm. The speed of sound is the distance travelled during a unit of time by a sound wave propagating through an elastic medium. In dry air at 68 F, the speed of sound is 1,126 ft/s. This is 768 mph, or approximately one mile in five seconds. However, the speed of sound varies from substance to substance. Sound travels faster in liquids and non-porous solids than it does in air. It travels about 4.3 times as fast in water 4868.766 f/s, and nearly 15 times as fast in iron 16,797.9 f/s than in air at 68F degrees. Just hold the concerts on wet windy days when the water is at a chop and you'll have no complaints. Title: How to Perform a Front Handspring Performing a front handspring is an impressive gymnastics move that requires strength, flexibility, and control. This step-by-step guide will walk you through the process of executing a successful front handspring. Before attempting this skill, make sure you have mastered basic tumbling skills such as cartwheels, roundoffs, and backbends. It's also essential to warm up thoroughly before practicing any advanced tumbling moves. **Step 1: Prepare your body** Before starting, ensure you are physically prepared for the demands of a front handspring. Regularly engage in exercises that improve upper body strength (push-ups, dips), core stability (planks, leg raises), and lower body power (box jumps, squat jumps). Additionally, focus on increasing overall flexibility, particularly in your shoulders, hips, and hamstrings. **Step 2: Master the hurdle position** The hurdle position is crucial for initiating a front handspring. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and bend your knees deeply into a squat while keeping your heels on the ground. Swing your arms backward then forward as if performing a long jump. As your arms reach their peak extension, explosively extend your legs and leap upward. Drive your knees toward your chest during the jump, creating height and momentum. Land softly in a lunge position, one foot in front of the other, ready to spring off again. Practice this motion repeatedly until it becomes second nature. **Step 3: Perfect the roundoff technique** A strong roundoff sets up the perfect entry into a front handspring. Start by standing tall, then perform a powerful hop followed by a quick tuck roll. Your hands should contact the floor simultaneously, elbows slightly bent. Quickly push off the floor with both hands, transitioning from the tucked position into a bridge. Extend your legs over your head and land on your feet in a lunge position. Focus on maintaining speed throughout the entire movement. **Step 4: Connect the roundoff to the hurdle position** Now practice connecting the roundoff directly into the hurdle position without pausing between steps. From the completed roundoff, immediately snap your hands downward and lift your hips high into the air while jumping forward. Aim to bring your feet together under your hips as you would in a vertical jump. Continue extending your legs and swinging your arms forward to generate additional momentum. **Step 5: Add the handspring** With all previous components solidified, now add the actual handspring motion. Leap into the hurdle position as described earlier but instead of landing in a lunge, plant both hands firmly on the mat, fingertips facing forward. Keep your legs straight as you press your palms into the ground and kick them up overhead. At the same time, quickly arch your back and look behind you to spot your landing zone. Push forcefully off the floor with your hands, snapping your feet beneath you as you return to a standing position. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Always train on appropriate mats or sprung floors to reduce impact forces and risk of injury. * When learning new skills, work with a qualified coach who can provide feedback and guidance. * Visualize yourself successfully completing each component of the front handspring before attempting it. * Maintain proper form throughout every stage - rounded backs, flexed feet, and loose limbs increase instability and decrease power output. * Prioritize safety above everything else; never rush progress or attempt difficult moves beyond your current abilities. yeah, she clearly recognized the duck and the cow. Well over one thousand musical compositions have been inspired by Hugo's works from the 1800s until the present day. In particular, Hugo's plays, in which he rejected the rules of classical theatre in favour of romantic drama, attracted the interest of many composers who adapted them into operas. More than one hundred operas are based on Hugo's works and among them are Donizetti's "Lucrezia Borgia" (1833), Verdi's "Rigoletto" (1851) and Ernani (1844), and Ponchielli's "La Gioconda" (1876). Hugo's novels as well as his plays have been a great source of inspiration for musicians, stirring them to create not only opera and ballet but musical theatre such as Notre-Dame de Paris and the ever-popular Les Mis rables, London West End's longest running musical. Additionally, Hugo's beautiful poems have attracted an exceptional amount of interest from musicians, and numerous melodies have been based on his poetry by composers such as Berlioz, Bizet, Faur , Franck, Lalo, Liszt, Massenet, Saint-Sa ns, Rachmaninov and Wagner. Follow @universetoday on Twitter It is often difficult to grasp just how large the planets actually are. There are a number of ways to measure a planet, including diameter, volume, and surface area. Mercury is the smallest planet in our Solar System since Pluto was demoted to a dwarf planet. It has a diameter of 4,879 km, and a surface area of 17.48 x 107 km2, which is only about 11% of Earth's surface area. Mercury's volume is even smaller in comparison at 6.083 x 1010 km3, which is only 5.4% the volume of Earth. Venus is similar in size to Earth, which earned it the title of Earth's twin. Venus has a diameter of 12,100 km and a surface area of 4.6 x 108 km2. These measurements are 95% and 90% of Earth's diameter and surface area respectively. With a volume of 9.38 x 1011 km3, Venus' volume is 86% of Earth's. Earth has a diameter of 12,742 km and a surface area of 5.1 x 108 km2. Its volume of 1.08 x 1012 km3 gives the planet the largest volume of any of the terrestrial planets. Mars is also a small planet, the second smallest in our Solar System. Mars' diameter is 6,792 km, only about 53% of Earth's diameter. At only 28% of Earth's surface area, Mars has a very small surface area of 1.45 x 108 km2. Mars' volume of 1.63 x 1011 km3 is only 15% of Earth's volume. All of the gas giants are larger in size than the four inner planets. Jupiter is the largest planet in our Solar System. It has a diameter of 143,000 km, which is more than 11 times the size of Earth's diameter. The numbers only get larger from there. Jupiter has a surface area of 6.22 x 1010 km2. That is 122 times greater than Earth's surface area. Jupiter's volume of 1.43 x 1015 km3 is an incredible number. You can fit 1321 Earths inside Jupiter. Saturn is the second largest planet in our Solar System. It has a diameter of 120,536 km across the equator, and a surface area of 4.27 x 1010 km2. With a volume of 8.27 x 1014 km3, Saturn can hold 764 Earths inside. Uranus has a diameter of 51,118 km and a surface area of 8.1 x 109 km2. Although Uranus is much smaller than Jupiter, it is still large. With a volume of 6.83 x 1013 km3, you could fit 63 Earths inside the gas giant. Neptune is slightly smaller than Uranus, but still very large. The planet has a diameter of 49,500 km. You could fit 57.7 Earths inside Neptune, which has a volume of 6.25 x1013 km3. Neptune has a surface area of 7.64 x 109 km2, which is 15 times Earth's surface area. Astronomy Cast has an episode on each of the planets including Mercury. Title: How to Eat Pizza in the Most Proper Way Possible Introduction: Pizza is one of the most popular foods worldwide, enjoyed by people of all ages. Step 1: Choose Your Pizza Decide what type of pizza you want - thin crust, deep dish, or stuffed. The choice depends on personal preference but keep in mind that thicker crusts might require different handling techniques which we'll discuss later. Key Tip: Always opt for quality over quantity. A gourmet slice from a reputable pizzeria often provides a better experience than several slices of mass-produced pizza. Step 2: Cutting the Slice If you have a whole pie in front of you, use a pizza cutter or sharp knife to divide it into manageable slices. Title: How to Perform the Candy Prank The candy prank is a classic joke that has been entertaining people for generations. It's simple, harmless, and can be pulled off with minimal resources. This tutorial will guide you through the process of executing this amusing prank, providing detailed explanations for every step along with useful tips and guidelines. By following these instructions, you'll have your friends laughing (and possibly spitting) in no time! **Step 1: Gather Your Materials** To perform the candy prank, you'll need: * A handful of small candies or mints (preferably hard-shelled) * A flat surface or tray * Water * A paper towel or cloth Key Tip: Choose candies that are similar in size and shape to ensure they stay together when stacked. Hard-shelled candies work best as they won't dissolve when wet. Guideline: Make sure the surface you choose is stable and large enough to accommodate the stack of candies without tipping over. **Step 2: Prepare the Candies** Wet your fingers slightly and lightly moisten the bottom half of each candy. This will help them stick together when stacked. Be careful not to make them too wet; otherwise, the candies may become difficult to handle or even disintegrate. Key Tip: Alternatively, you can use a tiny amount of corn syrup or honey instead of water for better adhesion. Guideline: Avoid handling the candies excessively as this might cause fingerprints or oils from your skin to transfer onto the surfaces, making them less likely to stick together. **Step 3: Build the Stack** Begin building your candy structure by placing one candy at the center of the tray or flat surface. Carefully add more candies, one at a time, on top of the initial piece, forming a vertical column. Try to keep the stack symmetrical and balanced. Key Tip: As you build the stack, press down gently on each new layer before adding the next one. This ensures a solid connection between layers and prevents the entire structure from collapsing prematurely. Guideline: Take your time during this stage - patience is crucial for creating a tall and sturdy candy tower. **Step 4: Present the Prank** Once you've built a sufficiently high stack of candies, approach an unsuspecting friend or family member. Casually mention that you found some delicious-looking candies and offer them one from the top of the pile. Key Tip: Maintain a straight face while presenting the prank to increase its effectiveness. The more convincing you appear, the funnier their reaction will be when they discover the truth. Guideline: Be mindful of your audience's age and sensitivities. If they're young children or particularly sensitive individuals, consider modifying the prank so as not to disappoint or upset them unnecessarily. **Step 5: Enjoy the Reaction** As soon as your target reaches for the top candy, give the base of the stack a gentle nudge or blow on it. The entire structure should come crashing down, revealing the deception. Their surprised expression coupled with laughter makes for a memorable moment! Key Tip: Remember, safety first! Make sure there are no hot drinks or fragile items nearby that could potentially get damaged during the collapse. Guideline: After successfully performing the candy prank, take responsibility for any cleanup required. Sweep up the scattered candies and wipe away any residue left behind. The Doctours Wife, had a Chamber-maide attending on her; who, notwithstanding all the ugly deformities in _Ruggiero_, regarding more his person then his imperfections (because hee was a compleate and well-featured youth) bestowed her affection most entirely on him, and oftentimes did supplie his wants, with her owne best meanes. _Ruggiero_ having this benefite of the Maides kinde love to him, made it an hopefull mounting Ladder, whereby to derive some good liking from the Mistresse, presuming rather on his outward comely parts, then anie other honest quality that might commend him. The Mistresse knowing what choyse her Maide had made, and unable by any perswasions to remoove her, tooke knowledge of _Ruggieroes_ privat resorting to hir house, and in meere love to her Maide (who had very many especiall deservings in her) oftentimes she would (in kinde manner) rebuke him, and advise him to a more setled course of life; which counsell, that it might take the better effect; she graced with liberall gifts: one while with Gold, others with Silver, and often with garments, for his comelier accesse thether: which bounty, he (like a lewde mistaker) interpreted as assurances of her affection to him, and that he was more graceful in her eye, then any man elsee could be. Yes many children sang that, didn't they? | Linking/Action Verbs Practice Linking Verb: A linking verb is a verb that links a word in the predicate to the subject. That word will either be a noun (predicate noun), which will rename the subject or an adjective (predicate adjective), which will describe the subject. Action Verb: A verb that shows action. It may or may not have a noun or pronoun following it that receives the action of the verb (direct object). There are some words (sense words) that will be linking or action verbs depending on how they are used in a sentence. For example: Mary seems to like the house. The house seems large. Write down the verbs in the following sentences. Then tell whether they are action or linking verbs. 1. The ghost appeared in the doorway. No I mean stand up over there. Mom is there a y at Peggy? Belizean cuisine is inspired by British, Mexican and Western Caribbean cooking. Since Belize is a melting pot of culture, it has adopted dishes from many different countries. The basic ingredients are rice and beans. These are often eaten with chicken, pork, veal, fish or vegetables. Coconut milk and fried plantains are added to the dishes to create a truly tropical taste. Exotic ingredients include armadillo meat, venison, iguana, iguana egg, and fried paca. Conch soup is a traditional dish. It has a characteristic taste and thick consistency due to added okra, potatoes, yams, cassava flour and a touch of toasted habanero. Belizean food is almost always served alongside white rice in coconut milk. When the snow is high you can clear the street. Want me to read it to you? 'I've done all I could. Title: Bond with Family Distracted by Their Electronics Introduction: In today's digital age, family members often find themselves engrossed in their own electronic devices, creating a barrier to quality time together. This tutorial aims to help you bridge that gap and bond with your loved ones despite their electronics usage. By following these steps, you can create meaningful connections and engage in enjoyable activities as a family. Step 1: Set Aside Dedicated Time Key Tip: Choose a specific day or time when all family members are likely to be available and less occupied with work or school commitments. Consistency is crucial for fostering a strong bond. Explanation: Establishing a routine encourages everyone to prioritize family time over individual screen time. It also creates anticipation and helps make such gatherings an essential part of your family's lifestyle. Step 2: Communicate Your Intention Key Guideline: Express your desire for more connection and communication within the family without criticizing anyone's electronics habits. Make sure to emphasize the benefits of spending time together. Explanation: Openly discussing your intentions sets expectations and allows family members to understand the importance of this initiative. Approaching the subject tactfully ensures that they feel supported rather than attacked, increasing their willingness to participate. Step 3: Plan Engaging Activities Key Tip: Opt for group activities that cater to various interests, ensuring everyone finds something enjoyable and feels included. Examples include board games, cooking classes, outdoor excursions, or craft projects. Explanation: Shared experiences promote conversation, laughter, and collaboration while providing opportunities for learning new skills and strengthening bonds. Inclusive activities ensure that no one feels left out or forced into participating in something unappealing. Step 4: Implement an "Electronics-Free Zone" During Family Gatherings Key Guideline: Encourage all participants to leave their devices in another room or turn them off completely during family events. Lead by example by doing the same yourself. Explanation: Limiting access to electronics minimizes distractions and demonstrates respect for the planned activity and fellow participants. Modeling responsible behavior reinforces its significance and increases the likelihood of others following suit. Step 5: Create Opportunities for Conversation Key Tip: Designate times throughout the gathering for open discussions about each other's lives, thoughts, feelings, and aspirations. Consider using icebreaker questions or conversation starters. Explanation: Meaningful conversations foster understanding, empathy, and closeness among family members. Providing structured prompts helps guide interactions towards deeper levels of engagement and connection. Step 6: Acknowledge Progress and Effort Key Guideline: Praise small victories and express gratitude for everyone's participation in reconnecting as a family. Avoid focusing solely on lapses in attention or slipups related to electronics usage. Explanation: Recognition and appreciation encourage continued effort and reinforce positive behaviors. Focusing on progress instead of perfection reduces pressure and cultivates a supportive environment conducive to growth. Conclusion: By implementing these steps consistently, you can successfully bond with your family despite their electronics usage. Remember that building stronger relationships takes patience, persistence, and practice; don't be discouraged if initial attempts encounter resistance or challenges. With time, your efforts will contribute to creating cherished memories and fostering lasting connections within your household. Now, don't worry if you haven't heard of this term before - it's just a fancy name for a way of connecting different mathematical systems together using functions. First, imagine you have two boxes full of toys, which we'll call Box A and Box B. Each box has its own set of rules for playing with the toys inside. These sets of rules are like mathematical structures, where certain things are allowed and others aren't. Now, suppose you want to move toys from Box A to Box B while keeping some of the special properties those toys had in Box A. To do this, you need a function (or rule) that connects the two boxes in a nice way. This function takes toys from Box A and sends them to new toys in Box B that preserve the desired properties. Sometimes, though, not all toys can make the journey directly from one box to another without losing some of their special features. That's where derived functors come in handy! They allow us to create new functions that account for these lost properties by adding extra information along the way. Think of derived functors like helpful guides who accompany the toys during their trip and remember any important details that were forgotten or overlooked during the initial transfer process. These guides ensure that when the toys reach Box B, they still carry all the essential characteristics they had back in Box A. So, next time you find yourself moving toys between boxes or working within different mathematical structures, keep derived functors in mind as your trustworthy companions helping maintain connections and preserve vital toy traits! What You Need: - Spring-style clothespins - Assortment of small children's clothes - Picture of clothes hanging on a clothesline What to Do: - Have your child help you put up a clothesline at a height that he can reach. - Help your child label the clothespins with numbers 1-10, using the marker. Show your child how to open the clothespins and use them to hang things on the line. Working with clothespins helps build hand muscles, which will help kids with writing... an extra bonus! - Let him lay out the clothespins in numerical order. Younger children can get some help, by taking peeks at a piece of paper with numbers written in sequence. - Mix up the clothespins again and ask your child to pin the clothes on the line in numeric order. They went to go to their home. Title: How to Perform Long Multiplication Long multiplication is a method used to multiply large numbers efficiently. This technique involves breaking down the problem into smaller parts, making it easier to manage and understand. By following these steps, you'll be able to perform long multiplications with confidence. ### Step 1: Understand the Basics Before diving into long multiplication, ensure that you are comfortable with place values (ones, tens, hundreds, thousands, etc.) and basic multiplication facts up to at least 9 x 9. Familiarize yourself with the concept of carrying over numbers when necessary - this skill will prove crucial during long multiplication. ### Step 2: Set Up the Problem Write the two numbers one above the other, aligning them by their place value columns. The ones place should be under the ones place, the tens place under the tens place, and so forth. For example: 5873 x 641 ### Step 3: Multiply the Digits in the Ones Place Start by multiplying the digit in the ones place of the second number (in our case, 1) by every digit in the first number. If there is a result in the tens or higher place, write it above the line in its appropriate column. There is no need to carry over any digits yet. It should look like this: 5873 x 641 0 ____ 1 ### Step 4: Move One Column to the Left and Repeat Next, move one column to the left in both the first and second numbers. Now multiply the new digit in the ones place of the second number (in our case, 4) by all the digits in the first number. Remember to add zeros as needed since we moved one column to the left. Then, include any carried-over digits if applicable. Add an extra zero at the end because we have now reached the tens place. Your work should appear as follows: 5873 x 641 35368 0 ____ 1 ### Step 5: Continue Moving and Multiplying Repeat step four for the remaining columns until you reach the leftmost digit of the second number. Be sure to continue adding zeros as required and always start from the rightmost digit when performing the multiplication. After completing this process, your final calculation would look similar to this: 5873 x 641 35368 350520 3523800 22488368 ### Key Tips and Guidelines * Always remember to pad zeros when moving columns to maintain proper place value representation. * Carry over any excess amounts greater than 9 to the next column on the left. * Work systematically from right to left, focusing on one digit at a time before proceeding to the next. * Practice regularly to build familiarity and fluency with the process. By mastering long multiplication, you can confidently tackle complex arithmetic problems involving larger numbers. With patience and practice, anyone can become proficient at solving such calculations by hand. Even if they try and kill you. Can you pour it in there for me? Look at the birds in the birdbath here. Title: How to Find Distance Using Google Maps on iPhone or iPad Google Maps is a popular navigation app that can help you find distances between two locations with ease. This tutorial will guide you through the process of measuring distance using Google Maps on your iPhone or iPad. Step 1: Open Google Maps Locate the Google Maps application on your device's home screen or app library and tap on it to open. If you don't have the app installed yet, download it from the App Store. Step 2: Allow Permissions For accurate results, ensure that Google Maps has access to your location services. Go to Settings > Privacy > Location Services, then toggle the switch next to Google Maps to grant permission. Step 3: Search for Locations Tap on the search bar at the top of the screen and enter the names or addresses of the starting point and destination. As you type, suggestions will appear below; select the correct one when you see it. Repeat this for both the start and end points. Key Tip: You can also drop pins directly onto the map by touching and holding down on the spot where you want to place it. A red pin will appear along with an information box displaying the address. Step 4: View Directions Once you've entered both locations, click on the 'Directions' button (a right-pointing arrow inside a rectangle) located above the keyboard. By default, it shows directions from your current location to the entered destination. Step 5: Choose Mode of Transportation At the top of the directions panel, there are several transportation modes available like driving, walking, cycling, or transit. Tap on the icon representing your preferred mode of transport. The route displayed on the map changes based on the selected option. Step 6: Calculate Distance Now that you have set up your route, look near the bottom of the directions panel. Here, you'll see the total distance listed under 'Distance'. For more specific details about individual segments of the journey, press the arrow buttons beside each step in the list view until you reach the section whose distance you wish to know. Guideline: Remember that the measured distance might vary slightly depending on whether you measure 'as the crow flies' (direct line) or following roads. Google Maps calculates distances based on actual road networks. In Conclusion, utilizing Google Maps to determine distances while navigating is incredibly useful and straightforward once you understand the steps involved. With its precise calculations and various transportation options, Google Maps makes planning trips easier than ever before. And then he was going to tie his shoe. Some samples can be placed directly under the microscope. However, many samples look better when placed in a drop of water on the microscope slide. This is known as a "wet mount." The water helps support the sample and it fills the space between the cover slip and the slide allowing light to pass easily through the slide, the sample, and the cover slip. Make a wet mount. Determine for yourself if an object looks better under the microscope mounted dry or wet. Tools & Materials - 2 flat slides - 2 cover slips - paper towel To make a wet mount place a sample on the slide. Using an eyedropper put a drop of water on the sample. Place one end of the cover slip on the slide and slowly lower the other end using the end of a toothpick. This will help to prevent air bubbles from getting trapped under the cover slip. The water should just fill the space between the cover slip and the slide. If there is too much water and the cover slip is floating around, remove some water by holding the edge of a paper towel next to the edge of the cover slip. If there is too little water and some of the space under the cover slip is still dry, add more water by placing a drop right next to the cover slip. A little practice will help you learn how much water to add. Now you are ready to compare wet mounts and dry mounts. Cut two small pieces of your hair. Place one on a slide and put a cover slip on it. This is a dry mount. Place the other piece of hair on the other slide and make a wet mount. Look at both slides. Draw a picture of both slides. Does one slide look better than the other? Repeat this exercise with other samples. Sometimes your sample is a little too thick for a wet mount. If the sample touches the cover slip and prevents it from lying flat, the water won't be able to fill the space between the slide and cover slip. You can fix this by cutting four small pieces of a material that is thicker than the sample and use these to support the cover slip. Plastic from a milk container works well. Make a wet mount as before but support the cover slip with the material. Yeah [yes] I see some shoes over there. So at home he mostly tends to play with like his dolls and his stuffed animals? Bees can flap their wings as fast as 11,000 times per second. They flap their wings to do a lot of things, but one reason is to heat and cool the hive at all times. The worker bees keep the hive at a steady temperature all year round with their wing flaps. They would like it to be 92-93 degrees -- which is pretty warm. Honey bees fly in a radius of about 3-5 miles from their homes to forage for flowers and food. Bees gather both nectar and pollen from flowers and trees. Nectar: Honey is actually like bee throw-up!!! They bring the nectar back to the hive and regurgitate the nectar into a honey cell. Then through flapping their wings (again!), the bees evaporate some of the liquid in the nectar throw-up until it is honey. Then they seal it for later use. Pollen: Bees use pollen, which is really sticky, and combine it with nectar to make bee bread. They feed this to the baby bees, which are also called brood. Bees have a lot of hairs -- everywhere -- even on their eyes! People think this helps them to be really good at collecting pollen which they then move into their pollen baskets on the back of their legs and take home. Bees see color and they use their eyesight to see flowers. Bees can see colors that we cannot see -- in the ultra-violet rage. Some flowers even have "runways" that are colored maps showing the bees where to land. We people cannot see these markings on the flowers, but the bees can. Bees preform an essential act by moving pollen and nectar from one flower to another. They pollinate the flowers and trees which allows fruits and vegetables to be created and to grow. The honey bees that we are used to tlking about fly in the daytime and not at night. Africanized bees can fly with moonlight though. A hive can make 50-200 pounds of honey a year, and it takes over 150 trips to a flower or tree to make just one teaspoon of honey. You want to read it or throw it around? Title: How to Assemble a Gaming Chair Introduction: Gaming chairs have become increasingly popular due to their ergonomic design, promoting comfort during prolonged gaming sessions. This tutorial will guide you through assembling your new gaming chair with clear and concise steps. Things You'll Need: - A new gaming chair - Phillips head screwdriver - Optional mallet or hammer Step 1: Unpack All Components Carefully unbox all parts of your gaming chair, ensuring nothing is damaged during transportation. Lay out each piece including the base, gas lift, seat, backrest, armrests, screws, washers, and any additional tools provided by the manufacturer. Key Tip: Keep track of small components like screws and washers to avoid losing them. Step 2: Attach Base to Gas Lift Place the gas lift upright so that the wider end faces upwards. Position the star-shaped base over the top of the gas lift, aligning the holes. Insert the appropriate screws through the bottom of the base into the gas lift using your screwdriver. Tighten securely but do not overtighten as this could damage the threading. Guideline: Typically, there are five screws needed here. However, always refer to your specific model's instruction manual. Step 3: Install Back Rest onto Seat Pan Turn both the back rest and seat pan upside down. Align the slots on the back rest with the protrusions on the underside of the seat pan. Slide these together until they click into place. Key Tip: Some models may require pushing down firmly on the back rest while others might benefit from gently tapping with a mallet or hammer. Step 4: Secure Back Rest to Seat Pan Flip the combined unit right side up. From underneath, insert screws through the predrilled holes where the back rest meets the seat pan. Hand tighten initially, then gradually increase tension evenly across all points. Step 5: Fix Arm Rests Depending on your model, arms rests can be attached at different stages. They usually screw directly into the sides of the seat pan or sometimes clip onto brackets fixed beneath the seat pan. Follow your particular model's assembly guide for precise instructions. Step 6: Connect Chair to Base Position the assembled chair (without wheels) over the star-shaped base making sure the hole in the center of the base matches the upward projection from the gas lift mechanism. Lower the chair slowly onto the base. Once contact is made, secure it with the locking ring provided. Step 7: Attach Wheels to Base Snap each wheel onto one of the prongs around the circumference of the base. Push firmly until you hear an audible 'click', indicating it's locked in position. Repeat this process for all five wheels. Conclusion: After completing these steps, you should now have successfully assembled your gaming chair! Before sitting, double check all connections for stability and ensure everything feels sturdy and safe. Enjoy your enhanced gaming experience! Torno Torno Suggestions for Use To understand children's learning with open-ended materials, it is often useful to step back and observe their spontaneous play and problem solving. Children use blocks in a variety of ways: - To create designs - arrangements with no particular function. - To build functional structures - things that move, shelters, and enclosures. - To build representations of real objects - sailboats, animals, and people. - To rearrange patterns - recombining a given set of blocks into different arrangements. - To act out narratives - using the blocks to tell a story. - To encounter and solve problems Children will almost always start building on their own and may often build for long periods of time with great concentration. We have included some suggestions for use to further stimulate their ideas. However, we encourage parents and teachers to let children take the lead. + Language Development + Mathematical Thinking - Look at all the pieces. How do they feel? What colors do you see? What shapes do you see? - What other things do you know that turn? - Can you tell me a story about what you made? + Physical Knowledge - Are there enough dowels to fit in each of the holes? - Are there enough squares to put on each of the dowels? - Are there any empty holes on the turning base with nothing in them? How many? + Aesthetic Ideas - How many different ways can you turn Torno Torno? - Can you turn it without touching the base? - What is the fastest you can turn it? What is the slowest? - What do you think will happen if you put the ball on the turntable and spin the turntable very fast? Does it matter where you put the ball on the base? + Imaginative Play - Do you like the way the color balls look when they are turning? - Would you like to change the way you have put them on the base? - Do you like to put the same colors together or to try different ones? - Let's look at some books on modern sculpture and see if we can find some creations like the ones you made. How are they the same? What materials did the sculptor use? What other materials would you like to add to your creation? - This is my motor rolling machine. - I made lots of people with hats on top of their heads. This one has a yellow hat, a red hat, a blue hat, a green hat. - When you turn it really fast it makes a whooshing sound like the waves. [Back to Product Page] There are many good techniques for taking notes. Some are very familiar, some are very unusual. All of them work. The trick is to find the one that works best for you. Taking notes in class is a way to remember the points in the lecture the teacher emphasized and therefore considered important. These are the points you will probably be tested on. Taking notes should be an active process that involves your complete attention both mental and physical. Whether you use mind-mapping, the Cornell method, or index cards you are creating memory traces in your brain that help you learn. Here are some tips for taking good notes no matter what method you use. - Take notes when it is important to remember what the teacher is saying. That is, what does the teacher want from you? - Read the assignment BEFORE you come to class. Teachers do actually relate the lecture material to what's in the book. - Make your notes selective. Don't write everything down, but don't trust your memory either. And... if the teacher says something will be on the test...write it down. - Make a brief summary at the end of a lecture. Ask yourself, what did we talk about today? Was it new or related to other material that's been covered? - Experiment with different ways of taking notes. Try matching your note taking style with the type of class.. or see if changing your style improves your ability to remember the material. - Don't fall for note taking myths. These myths include: writing down everything the teacher says; outlining is the most effective way of taking notes; good listening should be enough; lecture material will be covered in the book so there's no need to take notes. All of these myths will get you into hot water when it comes time to study for a test. Follow the links provided in the table on the right to look at the different types of note taking. These are not the only ones. But one of them or an adaptation of one may work for you. Jenny would you like to play with some toys with your mom? To understand M-theory one must first have some knowledge of string theory. For hundreds of years, scientists have thought that the simplest objects in the universe are points, like dots. String theory says that this is wrong and that the simplest objects in the universe are shaped like pieces of string. These strings are so small that even when looked at very closely they look like points. Each basic particle is created by the strings vibrating in different patterns. The reason scientists had not thought of this idea for so long is that strings are much harder to work with than points. They seem to break such rules as causality and special relativity, which says that information cannot travel faster than the speed of light. Once upon a time, there was a charming oyster. It lived in the sea and everyone in the village said how beautiful it was. One day, a boy came to the village. He had a question, and he wanted the oyster to answer it. The boy asked the oyster, Why are you so charming? The oyster thought and thought, and then it answered. It said, Because I love to laugh and dance in the sea! The boy smiled and thanked the oyster for its answer. Then he said goodbye and the oyster waved back. From then on, the oyster was known as the most charming oyster in the sea. "On the grounds," deliberately replied Chunder Bindabun, "that he surreptitiously did pull his horse's head. " Juggins was too dumbfoundered to reply to the accusation, and several spectators came forward to testify that they had personally witnessed him curbing his steed, and--it being contrary to the _lex non scripta_ of turf etiquette to pull at a horse's head when he is winning--Juggins was very ignominiously plucked by the Jockey's Club. The Duchess made the desperate attempt to argue that, if Juggins was a pot, Mr Bhosh was a kettle of equally dark complexion, since he also had reined up before attaining the goal--but Chunder Bindabun was able easily to show that he had done so, not with any intention to forfeit his stakes, but merely to salute his betrothed, whereas Juggins had pulled to prevent his horse from achieving the conquest. So, to Mr Bhosh's inexpressible delight, the Derby Cup, full as an egg with golden sovereigns, was awarded to him, and the notorious blue ribbon was pinned by the judge upon his proud and heaving bosom. [GRA has been holding all the pop beads in her arms] Title: How to Find Good Soap: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: When it comes to personal hygiene, soap is an essential item that we use daily. However, with so many options available in the market, finding good soap can be overwhelming. This guide will help you navigate through the process by providing detailed steps, useful tips, and guidelines to ensure that you find the best soap suited for your needs. Step 1: Identify Your Skin Type The first step towards finding good soap is understanding your skin type. There are five main categories - normal, oily, dry, sensitive, and combination skin. Each type requires specific care and ingredients. For instance, people with oily skin might prefer soap containing clay or charcoal which absorbs excess oil, while those with dry skin should opt for moisturizing soaps rich in natural oils like olive or coconut oil. Key Tip: If you're unsure about your skin type, consult a dermatologist or do a simple test at home. After washing your face, wait for an hour; if your skin feels tight, you probably have dry skin. Shiny patches indicate oily skin, redness or irritation suggest sensitive skin, and both greasy and dry areas mean you have combination skin. Step 2: Look for Natural Ingredients Good soaps usually contain mostly natural ingredients. Avoid products filled with synthetic fragrances, colorants, and harsh chemicals such as sodium lauryl sulfate (SLS) which can strip your skin off its natural oils leading to dryness and irritation. Instead, look for plant-based ingredients like aloe vera, shea butter, essential oils, etc. These components provide gentle cleaning, nourishment, and soothing benefits. Guideline: Check the label before purchasing. The ingredient list must primarily consist of words you recognize and understand. Remember, the closer these beneficial elements appear near the top of the list, the more there is in the product. Step 3: Choose Soap Based on Its Purpose Different situations call for different types of soap. Hand soaps are designed to remove germs effectively without causing excessive dryness. On the other hand, body washes or bar soaps need to strike a balance between cleansing and maintaining skin moisture. Then there are specialty soaps formulated for particular purposes like exfoliating, treating acne, managing eczema, etc., choose accordingly. Step 4: Consider Eco-Friendly Options Consider buying environmentally friendly soaps made from sustainable sources using biodegradable packaging. Many companies now offer organic, cruelty-free, and zero-waste soaps. By choosing eco-friendly alternatives, you're making a positive contribution to our planet while ensuring good quality soap. Step 5: Read Reviews & Get Recommendations To get unbiased opinions, read online reviews from multiple platforms. Pay attention to comments regarding scent, lather, longevity, effect on skin, etc. Also, ask friends, family members, or colleagues for recommendations based on their experiences. Step 6: Test Before Purchasing Whenever possible, try samples before committing to a full-sized product. This allows you to check whether the soap agrees with your skin. Most stores allow testers; otherwise, consider buying travel sizes first. Conclusion: Finding good soap involves considering several factors including your skin type, preferred ingredients, purpose, environmental impact, and user feedback. Following this comprehensive guide ensures that you end up with a high-quality soap tailored to meet your requirements, enhancing your overall bathing experience. Have you ever heard of "dynamical systems"? No? Well, don't worry because today we're going to learn about it in a fun and easy way! Imagine if we could predict how things change over time, like the number of pets you have or the growth of your plants. That's what studying dynamical systems is all about! There's this cool website called DSWeb which gathers different computer programs (software) designed to help us explore these changing systems. It's kind of like having a bunch of tools that let you create neat projects and ask interesting questions, just like how you use building blocks to make structures or paint sets to create art. On DSWeb, there's something called JuliaDynamics. Think of them as friendly guides who created some awesome toolkits (packages) so that you can dive deeper into understanding dynamic systems without getting confused by complicated math formulas. They even made tutorials to show you how to use their tools - isn't that nice? Now, I want you to imagine playing with toy cars on a ramp. You can control how high the ramp is and watch how fast or slow the car zooms down depending on that height. In real life, sometimes things get messy when trying to figure out exactly how changing one thing affects another. But, using JuliaDynamics' packages, you can easily run simulations, adjust settings, and see patterns clearly. How cool is that? So go ahead, check out DSWeb and give those tutorials a try. Have fun learning and exploring the fascinating world of dynamical systems! Who do you think is really right let's go listen. "We'll have to beat them with passes," Johnny explained. He looked at the girl beside him and marvelled. And then he gave some to Nina. An uncle is a name used in an extended family. An uncle is either a brother of one of someone's parents. That person is the uncle's nephew (male) or niece (female). An uncle's child is a cousin. A (also written as great-uncle or grand-uncle) is the brother of a grandparent. In some cultures an uncle is considered a close family member. Based on the old Chinese saying "the oldest son in the family is the father of the family" an uncle is often the head of a family. In many cultures no single word (such as uncle) describes both their parents' brothers. Instead there are words to describe a person's kinship to their mother's brother or a person's kinship to their father's brother. An uncle can also be someone not related by blood or marriage as in a term of endearment or respect. In this use uncle can also be part of a nickname such as Uncle Sam. oh, these [magnet balls] are magnets Chi. Once upon a time there was a rat. His name was Peter and he was very brave. One day, Peter started to explore the world outside his house. He came across a bottle full of a strange liquid. Peter sniffed it and it smelled bad. He thought it was poison. Though he was scared, Peter decided to drink a drop. Suddenly, the poison tasted sweet! Peter was so surprised that he dropped the bottle and it broke. From the poison spilled out a rare gem. Peter was so excited that he started jumping up and down. A nearby rabbit watched him curiously. The rabbit asked, "What happened? Why are you so happy?" Peter smiled and said, "I found something very special. It's a rare gem that I found in the bottle of poison I drank!" We can build a forest on here. People often travel from far away to be with other family members at Christmas. Those people who cannot travel often make long-distance phone calls on Christmas Day. Many people also see Christmas as a time to reach out to others that they know might be lonely, and invite them to dinner on Christmas Day. Christmas is seen as a time for people of all ages to have fun together, for cousins to get to know each other, for grandparents to see their grandchildren and for the family to admire the babies that have been born during the year. Metallurgists use many tools to determine the characterizes of metal using a technique called metallography. One of the most useful tools is the Scanning Electron Microscope. To use an SEM a metal sample is polished and hit with electrons to produce a clear image of the metal. This permits for the metal microstructure to be seen. A microstructure is what the metal looks like when zoomed in very closely. There are many types of microstructure such as pearlite and ferrite. Friends is an American comedy television series. Well that looks like we can see in this rabbit's house. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/11/5949DL.cha = = = Sent in by: Stacie of Tinley Park, IL When a friend tells you to bounce, you can say how high. - 2 4-foot wooden dowels or broomsticks - 2 big rubber bands - 2 3" 3-ring binders - measuring stick or tape measure - tennis ball - rubber ball - golf ball - ball of clay - paper and pen for making a chart - To find out just how high different types of balls will bounce, you can use a Daredevil Ball Jump to measure the height of each bounce. This activity can be done in two parts, but first you need to build the Daredevil Ball Jump. - Hold two long, skinny poles about 3 feet apart. You can use wooden dowels, broomsticks, or branches. - Stretch a rubber band between the dowels. If the rubber band you have is too small to reach, connect a couple together, or use string. The ball has to jump over the rubber band (or string). - Staple a couple of pieces of paper to the rubber band. The ball moves pretty quickly and sometimes it's hard to tell if it went over or under the rubber band. If you can see and hear the ball hit the paper, you'll know it went under the rubber band. - You will also need a ramp for the balls to roll down. You can use a couple of 3-ring binders to make a ramp on a tabletop. Position your dowels so that after the ball rolls down the ramp, it bounces over the string. - Use a measuring stick or tape measure to measure the highest point on the dowel where the ball will still clear the paper. As you test each ball, start with the rubber band close to the ground. Move it up the dowels until you hear or see the ball touch the paper. - Before you start bouncing, draw a chart so you can record your data. Draw 4 columns. In the left column, write each different type of ball you are testing in a separate row. Across the very top of your chart write "bounce height." At the top of the column next to the types of balls write "room temperature." Save the remaining two columns for the second part of this activity. - Predict which balls you think will have the highest and lowest bounces, then get bouncing! Write down how high each ball bounces. Once you've bounced all your balls and recorded their bounce heights, what do you notice? Are you surprised by your results? What do you think makes some balls bounce higher than others? Do you think there is anything you could do to change the way the balls bounce? How? Write back to ZOOM to share your results and predictions with other ZOOMscientists. If you'd like to keep experimenting with ball bounces, try some of your own ideas or heat things up with Daredevil Ball Jump II. The height the ball bounces doesn't just depend on what it's made of or the temperature, it also depends on how high the platform is that you roll the ball off of. Why don't you roll the ball off a higher or lower surface and see what happens? See if the proportions are the same, height of the surface to the height of the bounce, it might be a good idea. The enercia the ball gains when it falls differs depending on the height of the surface and changes the height of the bounce, the more enercia the higher the bounce or the higher the surface the higher the bounce. The Dollar Bill's Birthday The U.S. $1 bill turns 150 Do you know whose face was on the first U.S. one-dollar bill? (Hint: It wasn't George Washington's.) Salmon P. Chase appeared on the first dollar bills, which were printed 150 years ago, in 1862. At the time, he was the Secretary of the U.S. Treasury-the department that handles the government's money. Before 1862, gold and silver coins were the main national currency. That changed during the Civil War (1861-1865), when thousands of soldiers were fighting for the Union. President Abraham Lincoln needed a lot of money to pay the troops, but there was a shortage of coins. A man named Spencer Clark came to the rescue. He built a machine that could cut bills from huge sheets of paper. On August 29, 1862, Clark and five workers started cranking out bills in Washington, D.C. Today, the U.S. Bureau of Engraving and Printing is entrusted with that job. Each year, it prints billions of bills, ranging from $1 to $100.This article will appear in the October 1, 2012 issue of Scholastic News Edition 5/6. For more from Scholastic News, click here. Shirley loves to SHAKE her SHAKERS Rationale: In order for children to be successful in phonics, reading and spelling, they need to understand phonemes. Children learn to recognize different phonemes and sounds by matching letters to their vocal gestures in spoken contexts. In this lesson, children will learn the sound and spelling of the consonant digraph /sh/ through an expressive representation and written practice. They will also be able to practice using and spotting the /sh/ sound in both written and spoken language. Copy of ''A Crash in the Shed'' by Geri Murray Poster with tongue twister (Shirley found a shellfish and shipped it to the shop) 1. Ask a question to get the students interested in the lesson. ''Has anyone ever been to a football game or a basketball game? What are some of the spirit/pep items you see while at the game? 2. Ask students, have you ever had someone ask you to be quiet by saying /sh/? Well, today we are going to focus on the /sh/ sound. Can you say /sh/? Model how to move your mouth to make the /sh/ sound. [Remember to shake your shakers when you hear the /sh/ sound.] 3. Now, we are going to learn how to do a tongue twister with the /sh/ sound. Has anyone ever heard of a tongue twister? Repeat after me, Shirley found a shellfish and shipped it to the shop. Everyone say it three times. Now we will say it again, but this time, I want you to stretch out all of the words so we can hear /sh/. [Remember: Use the motions!] Now, I want you to break off the /sh/ when we say the tongue twister: ''Sh/irley found a sh/ellfish and sh/ipped it to the sh/op.'' 4. Now is a good time to have students practice writing the letters. Pass out primary paper and pencil to your student(s). It is a good idea to not use mechanical pencils with beginning reading and writing students. Now we are going to use this paper to practice writing the letters s and h. We are going to write the lowercase letter s right now. Form a tiny c up in the air, and then swing back. This makes an s. Next we are going to write the letter h. Observe students and help them if they need help. You can model it again if you need to. Now when you see s or h in a word you can recognize it and remember that it makes the /sh/ sound. 5. Now, I'm going to show you how to find /sh/ in the word wish. I am going to stretch wish out and I want you to listen for the /sh/ like in shakers in this word. w-i-sh. There it is, there is the /sh/. It's your turn now.'' 6. Now I'm going to say some words and you are going to shake your shaker every time you hear the /sh/ sound. ''Wish, now put your finger over your mouth when you hear the /sh/ in wish. Do you hear it in bear? Do you hear it in ship? Do you hear it in hops? Do you hear it in wash?'' 7.''Now I'm going to read you the book, A Crash in the Shed by Geri Murray .This is a great book with lots of words with the /sh/ sound. Let's read and see what this Crash could be in the Shed and see if you can hear the /sh/ sound. Remember to use your shaker every time you hear the /sh/ sound. After reading the book once read it again but this time stop at the end of each sentence and have the students identify the words they heard that have the /sh/ sound in them. 8. For further assessment have the students identify different objects in the classroom that have the /sh/ sound in their name. pencil sharpener, shapes, shoes, etc 9. For an extension activity each student could make their own personal shaker to use at their very own next AU football game. A Crash in the Shed by Geri Murray Collection Copyright 2006, The Did you ever see a real one? They get out at the same time. An' I always believe that if you're busy your mind's busy so you know you... Cats are active carnivores, meaning that in the wild they hunt live prey. Their main prey is small mammals (like mice). They will also stalk, and sometimes kill and eat, birds. Cats eat a wide variety of prey, including insects such as flies and grasshoppers. Their main method of hunting is "stalk and pounce". While dogs have great stamina and will chase prey over long distances, cats are extremely fast, but only over short distances. The basic cat coat colouring, tabby (see top photo), gives it good camouflage in grass and woodland. The cat creeps towards a chosen victim, keeping its body flat and near to the ground so that it cannot be seen easily, until it is close enough for a rapid dash or pounce. Cats, especially kittens, practice these instinctive behaviours in play with each other or on small toys. Cats can fish. Check Your Messages on YouTube on iPhone or iPad Follow this step-by-step guide to learn how to check your messages on YouTube using an iPhone or iPad. This tutorial will help you stay connected with your friends, family, and other contacts through YouTube's messaging feature. ### Step 1: Launch the YouTube app On your iPhone or iPad home screen, locate the YouTube app icon (red rectangle with a white triangle inside) and tap it once to open the application. If you cannot find the app, swipe down on your home screen or search within the App Library to locate it. Key tip: Make sure that you have the latest version of the YouTube app installed. To update the app, go to the App Store, search for "YouTube," and tap 'Update.' ### Step 2: Sign in to your account (if necessary) If you are already logged into your YouTube account, proceed to Step 3. Otherwise, if you see a 'Sign in' button at the top right corner, tap it and enter your Google Account credentials (email address and password). Tap 'Next' and then 'Agree' to accept the terms and conditions. Once signed in, continue to Step 3. Guideline: Keeping your YouTube account signed in allows you to access all its features without having to log in every time. However, be cautious when sharing your device with others to avoid unauthorized access to your personal information. ### Step 3: Access the YouTube sidebar menu Tap the three horizontal lines located at the upper left corner of the YouTube interface. The sidebar menu provides various options such as Home, Explore, Subscriptions, and more. We need to access the Messenger feature, so keep reading. Tip: Remember that the location of certain features might change slightly depending on updates made by the developers; however, most functionalities remain consistent over time. ### Step 4: Select 'Messenger' from the sidebar menu Scroll down until you find the 'Messenger' option listed under 'Library,' then tap it. You can now view all your existing conversations and start new ones with your contacts. Key takeaway: Using YouTube Messenger enables quick communication with fellow users who share similar interests or channels, making it easy to discuss content directly related to their videos. ### Step 5: Manage your messages Once inside the Messenger section, you can perform several actions like: * **Starting a conversation:** At the top right corner, tap the blue circle with a plus sign ('+'). Enter the name or email address associated with the user's Google Account and type your message in the text box below. Then hit 'Send.' * **Replying to messages:** Simply tap any conversation thread to open it and type your response in the text box at the bottom. Press 'Send' when ready. * **Deleting a conversation:** Swipe left on the conversation thread and select 'Delete.' Confirm deletion if prompted. Note that this action is irreversible. * **Muting notifications:** Open a conversation thread, tap the three dots next to the user's profile picture, and choose 'Notification settings.' Set the toggle switch beside 'Messages' to OFF. By understanding these functions, you can effectively manage your messages and maintain clear communication with your contacts. In conclusion, following this comprehensive tutorial has allowed you to successfully check your messages on YouTube using an iPhone or iPad. By staying up-to-date with your communications, you can enjoy enriched interactions with others through shared video experiences. mhm, as soon as you finish talkin' to me. _ Messer_ Guiglielmo _of_ Rossiglione _having slaine Messer_ Guiglielmo Guardastagno, _whom hee imagined to love his wife, gave her his heart to eate. Title: How to Have a Healthy Bond with Your Parents Introduction: Having a healthy bond with your parents is an essential aspect of personal growth and overall well-being. This relationship shapes our values, beliefs, and behaviors from childhood into adulthood. However, building or rebuilding this connection may require effort, patience, and understanding. Here are some steps that can help you foster a strong and positive relationship with your parents. Step 1: Understand Your Emotions and Reactions Before engaging in any communication with your parents, take time to understand your emotions and reactions towards them. Reflect on past experiences, both good and bad, and identify triggers that lead to negative feelings. By acknowledging these emotions, you'll be better prepared to approach conversations calmly and constructively. Key Tip: Journaling can be a helpful tool for self-reflection. Writing down thoughts and feelings provides clarity and perspective. Step 2: Initiate Open Communication Open dialogue is crucial in establishing trust and respect. Share your thoughts, ideas, concerns, and aspirations honestly but tactfully. Avoid blaming language; instead, use "I" statements to express yourself clearly. For example, say "I feel unheard when..." rather than "You never listen to me." Guideline: Choose appropriate timing for serious discussions. Both parties should be relaxed and free from distractions. Step 3: Practice Active Listening When your parents speak, give them undivided attention. Show interest by nodding, maintaining eye contact, and asking relevant questions. Try to comprehend their point of view even if you disagree. Remember, active listening shows respect and validates their opinions. Key Tip: Paraphrase what they said to ensure mutual understanding. For instance, "So, what I hear you saying is..." Step 4: Set Boundaries Respectfully Healthy relationships involve clear boundaries. Determine what behavior makes you comfortable and communicate this assertively yet politely. If your parents overstep these limits, gently remind them about your boundary. Guideline: Be consistent with setting and enforcing boundaries. Inconsistency might send mixed signals, causing confusion. Step 5: Show Appreciation Regularly Express gratitude regularly for all that your parents do for you. A simple thank you note, phone call, or face-to-face conversation can strengthen your bond significantly. Recognizing their efforts lets them know you value and respect them. Step 6: Spend Quality Time Together Engage in activities that everyone enjoys. Shared hobbies provide opportunities for fun, laughter, and creating new memories together. The goal is to build a deeper connection beyond familial roles. Key Tip: Consider volunteering or attending community events together. These shared experiences often result in stronger bonds due to working towards common goals. Step 7: Seek Professional Help When Needed If conflicts persist despite your best efforts, consider seeking professional help. Therapists specializing in family dynamics can guide you through challenging conversations and teach effective conflict resolution strategies. Remember, every individual and family situation is unique. What works for one person may not work for another. Patience, empathy, and resilience are vital throughout this process. Over time, implementing these steps will contribute to a healthier, happier relationship with your parents. oh, what color what shape is the tail? Don't we need something for the form? Title: How to Create a Stunning Sunset Makeup Look ================================================== A sunset makeup look is a captivating and radiant beauty trend that mimics the stunning colors of a summer sky at dusk. This glowing and warm-toned makeup style can be achieved with ease by following these 12 simple steps. With this comprehensive guide, you will learn about various products, tools, and techniques required to create an eye-catching, dimensional, and alluring appearance reminiscent of nature's most beautiful light show. ### Things You'll Need * Primer (optional) * Foundation or BB cream * Concealer * Setting powder * Blush * Bronzer * Highlight * Eyeshadow primer * Eye shadow palette with warm shades such as orange, red, yellow, pink, and gold * Eye liner - pencil, gel, or liquid * Mascara * False lashes (optional) * Lip liner * Lipstick or lipgloss * Makeup brushes - flat foundation brush, fluffy blending brush, small detailer brush, angled contour brush, tapered highlighting brush, eyeshadow blending brush, flat eyeliner brush, precision eyebrow brush * Cotton swabs or pointed Q-tips * Beauty sponge ### Step 1: Prep Your Skin That's what I was going to ask you too actually... [S tries to give it back; D overlaps] Downloading videos from Veoh can be an exciting experience as you get to watch your favorite content offline, at your own pace. This tutorial will guide you through the process with clear, detailed steps that ensure success. ### Part 1: Installing Video DownloadHelper (Browser Extension) Step 1: Open Your Browser To begin, open Google Chrome or Firefox on your computer. Unfortunately, Safari and Internet Explorer are not compatible with this method. Key Tip: Make sure your browser is up-to-date before installing any extensions. Step 2: Search for Video DownloadHelper In the address bar, type `https://addons.mozilla.org` if using Firefox or `https://chrome.google.com/webstore` if using Chrome, then search for 'Video DownloadHelper'. Click on the extension result that appears. Guideline: Choose the correct extension by looking for the developer name - 'Michel Gutierrez' or 'softwareishard', along with over 10 million downloads and high ratings. Step 3: Add to Your Browser Click the blue 'Add to Firefox' button if using Firefox or the 'Add to Chrome' button if using Chrome. A popup window may appear asking for permission; click 'Add' or 'Accept & Install'. Step 4: Pin the Icon Once installed, pin the new icon (a puzzle piece in Chrome, an elephant head in Firefox) next to the address bar for easy access. ### Part 2: Finding and Downloading Veoh Videos Step 5: Go to Veoh Website Navigate to the Veoh website (veoh.com), find the video you want to download, and play it. Step 6: Initiate Download Look at the top right corner of your browser where the pinned icon resides. If there are one or more spinning icons around it, click on it to initiate the detection process. Otherwise, refresh the page and try again. Step 7: Select Desired Quality After clicking the icon, wait a few seconds while the extension scans available streams. Once detected, choose the preferred quality by ticking its checkbox under 'Choose Format'. Highlighted formats indicate higher resolution but larger file sizes. Key Tip: Always select the highest possible quality for optimal viewing. Step 8: Begin Download Now, press the 'Download' button located near the bottom left corner of the dropdown menu. Depending on your settings, the video might start downloading directly or ask for a filename and location first. Guideline: To change default saving directory, go to Options > General Preferences within the Video DownloadHelper addon settings. Part 3: Troubleshooting Common Issues Issue 1: No Detection If no spinning icons appear when playing a video, disable ad blockers temporarily since they often interfere with detection. Also, ensure JavaScript is enabled. Issue 2: Slow or Unresponsive Downloads Slow internet speeds could cause slow downloads. Pause other bandwidth-intensive tasks like streaming music or large files uploads during video downloads. Issue 3: HTTPS Errors These occur due to security restrictions imposed by websites. Try disabling SSL verification in the Video DownloadHelper options. However, exercise caution when doing so, as it poses potential risks. Remember always respect copyright laws and only download content you have rights to. Enjoy watching your favorite Veoh videos offline! But when she tasted it, she didn't like it. She said, "This spaghetti is poor. I don't want to eat it." Her mommy said, "Lily, you have to examine the spaghetti first before you say you don't like it. Maybe you just need to mix it up a bit." So, Lily took her fork and examined the spaghetti. It has one of the longest and most well known histories as a country in Africa and the world. Ethiopia was one of the few countries in Africa that escaped the Scramble for Africa. The word "Ethiopia" is from the Greek word (IPA: / ait io pia/) meaning sun light burned face. It is the most populous landlocked country in the world. Be able to explain how angle of attack is used to increase lift. 5. Be able to identify the control surfaces of an airplane. 6. Be able to explain the movement of air around an airfoil. [takes out some shapes to build another home] A white dwarf is very hot when it is formed, but since it has no source of energy, it will gradually radiate away its energy and cool. This means that its radiation, which gives it a blue or white colour at the start, lessens over time. Over a very long time, a white dwarf will cool to temperatures at which it will no longer emit light. Unless the white dwarf gets matter from a companion star or some other source, its radiation comes from its stored heat. This is not replaced. Each episode featured two songs.
